zh: 使用头脑与心灵、双手与双眼的才能。对大部分人来说是与生俱来之物。
en: Brain or heart or hand or eye. Most of us are born with most of them.

zh: 健康将受到损害。
en: Health will be injured.

zh: 激情将转化为入迷。
en: Passion will become Fascination.

zh: 理性将转化为恐惧
en: Reason will become Dread.

zh: 健康将彻底毁掉。
en: Health will be destroyed.

zh: 我如饥似渴地吸收着知识，仿佛影子吸收光线。也许我离提升不远了。
en: I soak up knowledge as a shadow soaks up light. I may be close to advancement.

zh: 我已经准备好改变了。
en: I am ready to improve.

zh: 我的情绪比平常更为高昂。有些事物我永远也不会理解，因而永远都那么珍贵，而如今我离它们更近了一点。
en: My emotions run higher than usual. There are things I'll never understand, and those things will always be precious, but just now I am closer to them. [Glimmering can be used with Study to gain Passio

zh: 我已经准备好改变了。
en: I am ready to improve.

zh: 属于我的这具身体。世间存在各种各样的身体，但这一具是属于我的。我的头脑需要它，正如菌菇需要泥土。
en: This is my body. There are other bodies, but this one is mine, and my mind needs it as a fungus needs soil.

zh: 我身强体壮，不论白天还是晚上。
en: I am strong by day as well as by night.

zh: 也许我可以休息一下，恢复健康。
en: Perhaps I can rest, and recover.

zh: 我记得一段更为温暖的时光。
en: I could recall a warmer time.

zh: 激情之火衰弱不振。
en: My passions burn low.

zh: 我的颅内幻象翻腾。
en: My skull roils with visions.

zh: 我已成了如影随形的疼痛的俘虏。
en: I have become subject to pain that will never depart.

zh: 我的情绪赶染上了使人毛骨悚然的恐怖。
en: My emotions have been infected with the creeping taint of fear.

zh: 我被反复出现在梦中的烛火撩拨着。
en: I am teased by recurring dreams of candlefire.

zh: 我乃智慧七柱之一。
en: I am a pillar of wisdoms.

zh: 健康将得以恢复。
en: Health will return.

zh: 激情将得以恢复。
en: Passion will be clarified.

zh: 永久的将一种（激活的和耗尽的均可）能力转化为其伤疤版本
en: Permanently converts an ability (active or exhausted) to its scar version

zh: 锻炼身体有益身心。
en: I have benefited from exercise.

zh: 表面上可能看不出来，但我已经像铁匠和士兵那样强壮了。
en: You might not know it to look at me, but I have grown strong as a smith or a soldier.

zh: 我可以把很多东西徒手捏爆了。
en: There are many things now that I could crush in my fists.

zh: 达至机器般的精妙。筋腱裹束起白骨，外包裹光滑紧绷的皮肤。“我已达顶点，往后只有衰落。”
en: Machine perfection. Sinew-bound ivory, tight in satin skin. 'From here, I can only deteriorate.'

zh: 身动如山移，出手如压顶。“用力气掌控他人。用力量掌控自我。”
en: I move like a mountain and strike like the sky. 'To master others is strength. To master yourself is power.'

zh: 我的白日梦有时会比睡梦更加光怪陆离。
en: My daydreams sometimes outpace my dreams.

zh: 我因一天弄折六根铅笔而出名。
en: I have been known to break six pencils in a day.

zh: 我醒时的思绪被幽灵幻影所驾驭。
en: Phantoms ride my waking mind.

zh: “看哪，他们站在我门外叩门；若我听见声音，我就开门，要他们进到我这里来，我与他们、他们与我一同坐席。”
en: 'Behold, they stand at my door and knock; if I hear any voice, I will open the door, and they will come in to me, and sup with them, and they with me.'

zh: 还有更多，更多，更多，更——
en: The more, the more, the more, the m -

zh: 我能流利地引经据典了。
en: I am flush with clever quotations.

zh: 我的学识足够让我理解自身的渺小。
en: I have learnt enough to know how little I know.

zh: 我的脑子装满了的知识，快要撑裂颅骨。
en: My skull creaks at the corners with the knowledge I've accrued.

zh: 这份令人发寒的沉静正是笃定带来的沉静。
en: This chilly stillness is the stillness of certainty.

zh: 我的头脑好似玻璃，记忆好似光。
en: My mind is glass, and my memory light.

zh: ……将转化为健康。
en: ...will become Health.

zh: ……将转化为激情。
en: ...will become Passion.

zh: ……将转化为理性。
en: ...will become Reason.

zh: 锻炼，或者其他更少见的东西，令我精神振奋。
en: Exercise, or something rarer, has invigorated me.

zh: 我已经准备好改变了。
en: I am ready to improve.

zh: 斯人抚育我们，造就我们；斯人在流放中等待，必将于我们带来拂晓之时再起。
en: The one who raised us; the one who made us what we are; the one who waits in exile, and will rise when we bring the Dawn.

zh: 塑形者言道：“我须得扮演铸炉。命你放置尸骸火花于引擎之正中心，用最高等级的奥秘将其唤醒，最猛烈的炼金素材为其燃料，找到在每重历史中名字都相同的大地边缘处的破绽，将装置带到那里，连带丽姬亚成员苏洛恰那所知的咬噬之钥。唤起铸炉最猛烈的热力，使用最强大的开启之秘；而后我将目送你融解。”
en: THE SHAPER SPEAKS: 'I must enact the Forge. Set a Carcass Spark at the engine's heart. Awaken it with the highest mysteries. Prepare the fiercest alchemy to fuel it. Find the flaw in the place at the

zh: 由弯曲金属构成的闪耀的牢笼，禁锢着远古光明的一个小小片段，柔光四射。它说不定可以做我们拂晓的起点，如果我们能用最猛烈的炼金素材再度点燃并净化之。
en: A shining cage of curved metal, enclosing a softly glowing fragment of ancient light. It might begin our Dawn, if we can rekindle it and purify it with the fiercest alchemy.

zh: 由破烂的金属胡乱缠成的圆球，禁锢着光的一点小小微粒，醉酒般上下沉浮着。我们必须在它完全崩解前将其修复！
en: A battered tangle of bent metal, enclosing a mote of drunkenly bobbing light. We must repair it before it collapses altogether!

zh: 由弯曲金属构成的发光的牢笼，囚系着太阳之火的一点小小微粒，光芒炽烈。如果我们能将其携至世界露出破绽，空气为之着彩的地方，便能带来拂晓。
en: A radiant cage of curved metal, imprisoning a blazing mote of solar fire. If we can carry it to the place where the world's flaw colours the air, we can bring the Dawn.

zh: 由爆燃的金属胡乱缠成的圆球。太阳之火的一点小小微粒在其内时断时续地颤抖着，光芒炽烈。我们必须在它完全崩解前将其修复！
en: A flaring tangle of bent metal. A blazing mote of solar fire quivers fitfully within. We must repair it before it collapses altogether!

zh: 这是绝顶狂暴的炼金素材：它致死的蓝焰比光更迅捷。人一触即死。
en: This is alchemy's savage summit: the deadly blue of fire outpacing light. Touch it and die.

zh: 一种中了毒，一直发出怪响的高能素材锭。我们必须安抚住它的怒火，要快！
en: A poisoned, stuttering ingot of energetic alchemy. We must soothe its rage, and quickly!

zh: 我们的塑形者选中他，用于航向世界的破绽处——位于永不改变之地的薄弱处。找到这个地方之后，他会抛弃自己的双眼，转而为他的“目的”而活。
en: Our Shaper has chosen this one to be a compass to the flaw in the world - the weak place in the place that does not change. When they find it they will surrender their eyes, and live for their Purpose

zh: 此人对一切尽皆盲目，只在对我们要寻找的世界破绽除外。维也纳有一处；不过离家近得多的地方也有一处，此地的名称始终如一，自凯尔·伊苏起便是。他能带领我们找到世界的瑕疵，用破晓器核心，施行我们的大功业。
en: This one is blind to everything except the flaw in the world that we seek. There is one in Vienna, but there is another much closer to home, in the place whose name has always been the same, even as C

zh: E夫人交给我一个小小的青铜喀迈拉头颅作为信物。待我们做好正式仪式的准备，她将怀揣她所持的钥匙前往世界的破绽处，好让漫宿在我带来黎明之时为我们的塑形者敞开大门。但她不会永远等待下去。
en: A little brass chimaera-head given by Mrs E. as a token. When we have prepared the proper rite, she'll bear her Key to the flaw in the world, so that when I bring the Dawn, the Mansus will open for ou

zh: 到时我们需要倚仗我们的塑形者的指引，用尸骸火花铸造出破晓器核心，将铸之原料精炼至最终形态，将一名追随者变为塑形者的罗盘，从名为苏洛恰那的丽姬亚成员处习得钥匙，而后完成功业。但如若我们不赶紧满足他的要求，恐惧将随之而至。
en: We will need our Shaper's guidance to forge the Dawnbreaker Core from a Carcass Spark; to refine Forge ingredients to their final form; to make of a follower the Shaper's Compass; to learn of the Key

zh: 我们以之为甘露和欢乐之源者，斯人静待于放逐之途，直到最盛大的筵宴——“谢肉祭”来临。
en: Our fountain, our joy, who rests in exile until the greatest of Feasts, the Vitulation.

zh: “愉悦者当给予生命；愉悦者当夺取生命；愉悦者当永无餍足。我们须采购隐秘的滋味，将其以司辰的赐物浸渍；我们须聚集宴主；须于大吉之时，以无人能敌的声音高呼不休之秘，并用低语之觚共饮。”
en: 'The Delight shall give life; the Delight shall take life; the Delight shall not be sated. We must purchase the secret savours and infuse them with the Hours' gifts; assemble the Host; at the most aus

zh: 我生了一种病。我的血液有恙，筋骨不适。我会死去。不过若得了我们愉悦者的圣血，我或许能延命无虞。
en: I have a disease. My blood is wrong. My bones are sick. I'll die. But perhaps I can preserve myself, long enough, with the holy blood of our Delight.

zh: 我现在和悦人者流着相同的血。只要我的血管有他滋味的庇佑，病症便不能侵蚀于我。若离了他的恩泽，我会死去。
en: I am of the same blood as the Delightful, now. As long as their savour graces my veins, my disease cannot touch me. Without their blessing, I'll die.

zh: 某稀奇古怪的“T.R.N.有限公司之香料与风味分部”一贯致力于为我们这样的顾客提供餐饮服务。他们将这些诱人的食材运来，点缀装盘则由我们亲手操办……在将食材送进密不透风、昏暗无光的厨房处理妥当之后。
en: The Spices and Savouries department of the eccentric firm of T.R.N. Ltd caters to endeavours such as ours. They have supplied these tempting adornments, with which we shall bedizen and prepare ourselv

zh: 我们钓到几个醉心食色性之人。他们为一场官能盛宴而来。
en: We have attracted a few gourmands and perverts. They will expect a feast of the senses.

zh: 消息传开。好奇心和食欲会驱使陌生人参加我们的谢肉祭。
en: Word spreads. Curiosity and appetite will drive strangers to attend our Vitulation.

zh: 零星前来的脏兮兮的精神病态之人与好打听之人。
en: Filthy draggles of the morbid and the inquisitive.

zh: 饥饿之人、贪婪之人、好奇之人，都收到了我们的请柬。
en: The hungry, the greedy, the curious: all these have received our invitation.

zh: 如今谢肉祭的大名在所有嗜肉的口舌间暗暗传扬。
en: Now the name of the Vitulation is whispered wherever mouths embrace meat.

zh: 时间一到，宾客会如鼠群般涌来。
en: When the time comes, our guests will throng like rats.

zh: 我们只等持钥匙者前来，于我们的愉悦者扮演赤杯时打开王皮之门。
en: We await only the Key-holder, who must open the Kingskin Gate when our Delight enacts the Grail.

zh: 一声令下，大批宴主即集合一处，死命瞪大眼睛——张大嘴巴——来飨食与被食——吸去我身上我们愉悦者的血。属于我们的愉悦将指引他们宴饮，操线仙娥则将拧转钥匙。
en: When the signal is given, a great Host will assemble, their eyes so wide - their mouths so wide - to feast and be feasted upon - to draw from me the blood of our Delight. Our very Delight shall lead t

zh: 我得到了一小瓶我们愉悦者的血液。我们的愉悦者在里面留下了以微小的细胞为文字的指示。我们可以口尝到他，如同他可以尝到我们。如果我们浪费这血，我们的愉悦者将震怒。
en: I have received a little vial of our Delight's blood, via courier. Our Delight's instructions are written herein, in the tiny tangles of cellular script. We can taste them, as they will taste us. If w

zh: 我们的物质源头，精神源头，现居于漫宿者。
en: Our source, our inspiration, who dwells now in the Mansus.

zh: “我们指引前路，我们照明驱暗，我们无有怜悯之心。令你寻找眼睛之门背后的十字路，在彼处育植诱饵，派遣银白色的死者带来凡人睡去的灵魂。待见证者数足，召来白日之钥，将三瓣之门‘向外’敞开，即，使它处于第三态。”
en: 'We shall navigate; we shall illuminate; we shall not be compassionate. Seek the Crossroads behind the Door of Eyes. Raise there the Allure. Send the silvered dead to bring the sleeping souls of morta

zh: 一双相互盘结的小路藏在树影之下，还有三尖之门的那只眼睛，在临近刀刃阶梯顶端的地方。是聚点的阴影？倒影？还是它的姐妹？
en: A pair of knotted roads beneath the shadow of a tree, and the eye of the Tricuspid Gate, close to the head of the Ascent of Knives. The Concursum's shadow? Its reflection? Its sister?

zh: 一旦被人瞧见，诱饵便会紧抓不放。没有几个活着的灵魂能如此深入漫宿。我必须找来一名镜中少女助我，先将诱饵拿给她看，她才会带灵魂来此。
en: Once seen, the Allure clings. Few living souls will ever venture this far into the Mansus. I must employ a Maid-in-the-Mirror and show her the Allure, so that she will know to bring them here.

zh: 一个孤影，在诱饵的金光与漫宿的蓝光笼罩下的十字路上游荡。
en: A solitary figure, swaying at the Crossroads in the golden light of the Allure, the blue of the Mansus.

zh: 一对见证者，在诱饵的金光与漫宿的蓝光笼罩下的十字路上游荡。
en: A Witness-pair, swaying at the Crossroads in the golden light of the Allure, the blue of the Mansus.

zh: 他们的面孔变得前所未有的空洞，然而他们执起了彼此的双手。
en: Their faces are blanker than ever, but they have taken each others' hands.

zh: 光明将他们的颜色漂尽，但他们的天性注定其将随某一时刻临近而生出斑纹。
en: The light bleaches them, but by their nature they will dapple as the moment draws near.

zh: 五名见证者等待着将会渐次长大，但永不会成熟的果实。
en: Five witnesses wait for the fruit that will swell, but never ripen.

zh: 气氛痛苦至极。他们无法移开视线。
en: The air is agony. They cannot look away.

zh: 七名无声的灵魂轻柔地向发光之源头致以祈求。
en: Seven silent souls softly supplicate shining source.

zh: 又是这个梦。这是给我，还有另外某人的指示，如果我能寻到他的话。它将领我进入漫宿。它将告诉我该做什么。对于那些对辉光有所了解的人来说，它是触及真正钥匙的前兆。
en: The dream, again. Instructions for me, and for someone else, if I can find them. It will guide me into the Mansus. It will show me what I need to do. It is a presentiment of the true Key, for those wh

zh: 悼歌诗人乃被剥夺至夺无可夺之司辰，一一唤亡者以姓名之司辰。每隔一段漫长的时间，他便会收取极大的代价，将亡者中的一人唤出虚界，使其得以续命，独行于世，毫发不伤，直至其迎来自己的终末之冬。
en: The Elegiast is the Hour from whom nothing more can be taken, and the Hour who calls each of the Dead by name. Once in a long ever, for a great price, he will call one of the Dead up out of Nowhere, t

zh: 某些夜晚，在观众面前，再进一步的念头振翅欲飞。我能再进一步变成什么呢？
en: On certain nights before the audience, there is the wing-lift of something more. What more could I become? [This card will allow you to win a Change victory, if you upgrade it far enough. Devote enoug

zh: 我随风起而颤抖。我表面之下的脉络清晰可见。我总是贴肤带着一颗石绿珠子。
en: I tremble when the wind comes. The veins beneath my surface are apparent. I always carry a bead of malachite next to my skin. [This card will allow you to win a Change victory, if you upgrade it far e

zh: 我的皮肤正片片剥落，皮肤之下的我更加暗淡，而我的眼睛却更加光亮。我身体的隐秘处藏着一颗海珍珠。有时在夜间，我会听到海的声音；有时在清晨，我的床单上沾着血。
en: My skin is peeling in patches, and beneath it I am darker, but my eyes are brighter. I carry a sea-pearl in a secret place. Sometimes at night I hear the sea; sometimes in the morning my sheets are ma

zh: 我的身体已达到我能达到的最暗。暗影是我的伴侣，且我的足音轻似密语。我手戴一只镶黑玉的戒指，不过黑玉被我朝内佩戴，藏于掌中避不示人。
en: I have grown as dark as I ever will. Shadows are my company, and my footfall is a whisper. I wear a ring set with jet, but I keep the stone turned inwards, in my palm invisible. [This card will allow

zh: 我的黑暗已淡化，我一切的色彩都已淡去。新叶瓣冒出在我最能容纳的那些部分。秘密在我的体表凝结。我淡白的舌下掩着一颗棱柱形的钙沸石。
en: My darkness has paled and all my colours are bleached. New lobes bud about my most receptive elements. I am dewed with secrets. I carry a scolecite prism tucked beneath my pale tongue. [This card will

zh: 我的眼白现在是黄色的，新的覆皮沿我的四肢生长出来，好在最终蜕变前收纳我的锋芒。私下里，我脚戴黄金趾环，耳戴金与黄玉制成的耳环，不过它们不可给旁人看到。
en: The whites of my eyes are yellow, and a new integument has developed along my limbs to sheath me before the final change. In private, I wear a gold toe-ring, and ear-rings of gold and topaz, but they

zh: 我的骨骼已变得不一样。它们承载了已失者的传说。如若我没挨过蜕变，这个传说便是我的传说，而我的白骨残片会被别人佩戴在身，用肌肤温暖。
en: My bones are different. They carry the tale of what is lost. If I do not survive, they will be my tale, and someone will wear fragments of my ivory to warm against their skin. [This card will allow yo

zh: 我突然意识到我手里握着前往智慧的钥匙。
en: It has occurred to me that I hold the key to wisdom.

zh: 我已奉身于追求更高层次的理解。我需要看透世界的本质，不论付出何种代价。
en: I have dedicated myself to the pursuit of higher understanding. I must see beneath the skin, whatever the cost. [This makes an Enlightenment victory possible. I must be a true initiate of the Mansus;

zh: 守夜人，即瞳中扉，触碰了我。现在每天我的好奇心都会增强：这是一种急迫的，生理上的需求，就像饥饿一样折磨我。我知道这就是第三印记。
en: The Watchman, who is the Door-in-the-Eye, has touched me. Every day now my curiosity grows stronger: an urgent, physical need, like gnawing hunger. I know this to be the Third Mark.

zh: 第四印记是眼中亮着的光，但是却难以发现。大多数情况下，人们只有在油灯熄灭时或者午夜的黑暗之中才能看到它；虽然当我瞪大眼睛的时候，会有更多的光开始溢出。我开始日渐消瘦了。
en: The Fourth Mark is the light that is found in the eyes, but it is very subtle. Most can see it only when the lamps are extinguished or the night is very dark; although when I open my eyes very wide, m

zh: 在第五印记时，光芒会从皮肤乌青或被剥皮之处泄露出来。我的皮肤开始逐渐松弛了，只因我日渐消瘦。我现在相当瘦弱。我总是很饥饿。
en: At the Fifth Mark, the light may leak out wherever the skin is bruised or frayed. And now the skin is beginning to hang loose on my body as the process gnaws at my flesh. I am very thin, now. I am alw

zh: 据说第六印记出现的标记就是虎口间的皮肤可以拉到小指关节处。我的皮肤已经变得非常非常松弛了。我觉得我的骨头也开始变得酥软了。我的双眼丧失了属于人类的色泽。
en: The Sixth Mark is said to occur when the flesh between finger and thumb can be pulled to the final finger-joint. My skin is very loose now. I think that my bones are beginning to soften. My eyes are n

zh: 石绿已教导我，裸露的自我并非真正的自我，不过这话不假。
en: The Malachite has taught me that the naked self is not the true self, but it is less of a lie.

zh: 孪生子与巨蛇之战，此战令我学到以痛苦为门的方法。
en: The Twins and the Serpent war, and in that war I have learnt how pain can be a gate.

zh: 丝毧已教导我，遭到揭露之物必得掩藏于先，以便让揭示引发后果，我已抛弃自己的足音。
en: The Velvet has taught me that what is revealed must first be hidden, for its revelation to be of any consequence. I have given up my footsteps.

zh: 制花人已赐予我万众渴求的东西，同时夺去了我的色彩。
en: The Flowermaker has given me what all desire, and taken my colours from me.

zh: 我将双眼奉与浪游旅人，以通过它们看到她所看的景象：通往红色蜕变之路。
en: I have given my eyes to the Vagabond, and through them I shall see what she would see: the road to the red change.

zh: 悼歌诗人已教导我要铭记，在我最后一部分蜕变的时候。我将带着这番教诲穿过王皮之门。
en: The Elegiast has taught me to remember, in the last part of me to change. I'll carry that lesson past the Kingskin Gate.

zh: 环杉命我袒露肉体，我听从了。我将不会一直听从环杉。
en: The Ring-Yew commanded I bare my flesh, and I obeyed. I will not always obey the Ring-Yew.

zh: 我行走在河边的漆黑色亚麻丛中，亚麻用千百根舌头品尝我的双脚，直到我再也无法抵抗它的碰触，而后我便只是一道影子了。
en: I walked in the black-flax at the river's edge, and it tasted my feet with its hundred tongues until I could withstand the touch of it no longer, and then I was only shadow.

zh: 我撞见制花之人所制之花，于是变做这副模样——我的色彩现在被他代为保管。
en: I came to the flowers the flower-maker made and that is what I became. He keeps my colours for me now.

zh: 蜈蚣给我讲了笑话，作为交换，我会让她为我雕刻黄金之眼，直至复眼现出。
en: The Centipede told me her jokes and in exchange I will let her carve me eyes of gold, until the ommatidia occur.

zh: 太阳遭到分裂，而且狼不会让我忘记它骨头的盐味——直到漫宿开裂为止。狼现在要求我带着这番滋味穿过林中之门。
en: The Sun was divided, and the Wolf will not let me forget the salt savour of its bones - not until the Mansus cracks. Now the Wolf requires that I carry that savour through the Door in the Wood.

zh: 我突然意识到我握着通往力量的钥匙。
en: It has occurred to me that I hold the key to power.

zh: 我已奉身于获取永恒的力量。
en: I have dedicated myself to the acquisition of immortal power, at any cost. [Upgrade this card further to make a Power victory possible. I must be a true initiate of the Mansus; I'll need to Work with

zh: 铸炉在我肉体上点燃了回应的火花。我极度渴求火焰的热力。我想很快我就将在睡梦中燃火。这是十分迫切的需求，就像生命对水的需求一样。我很确定这就是第三印记。
en: The Forge has kindled an answering spark in my flesh. I am desperate for the heat of fires. I think that very soon I may begin to set them in my sleep. It is an urgent need, like the need for water. I

zh: 虽然火焰让我遍体伤疤，但想炙伤我的肉体也是越来越难。我现在开始渐渐习惯火焰，如同一条龙或一块石头。这是第四印记。
en: I bear scars all over now from the fires, but it is harder each time to singe my flesh. I am beginning to grow accustomed to flame, like a dragon or a stone. This is the Fourth Mark.

zh: 第五印记：我的身体没有一根毛发。我变得像魔鬼一样强壮。如果我离开了火焰，我就会不停颤抖。
en: The Fifth Mark: no hair remains on my body. I am strong, devilishly so. I am always shivering, every moment I am away from the fire's side.

zh: 我满身伤疤，四肢都很难弯屈。我不愿将我的面容暴露在众人的目光下。我能手握炙热的煤炭而毫无知觉，虽然我能感到一丝气味和轻微的嘶嘶声。这是第六印记。
en: It is difficult to bend my limbs. I have so many scars. I prefer not to expose my face to the public gaze. I can hold a hot coal in my hand, and I feel nothing, although there is the faintest smell an

zh: 我能感受到那种几乎无人体会过的强烈冲动。
en: I could achieve experiences of an intensity few could imagine.

zh: 我已奉身于对感官的永恒追求。
en: I have dedicated myself to the immortal pursuit of sensation. [Upgrade this card further to makes a Sensation victory possible. I must be a true initiate of the Mansus; I'll need to Work with Grail-as

zh: 我将奉身于对感官的永恒追求。
en: I have dedicated myself to the immortal pursuit of sensation. [Upgrade this card further to make a Sensation victory possible. I must be a true initiate of the Mansus; I'll need to Work with Grail-asp

zh: 赤杯给予我温暖。它的干渴让我的嘴变干，手发痒。我知道这是第三印记。
en: The Red Grail has warmed me. Its thirsts have begun to dry my mouth and itch my hands. I know this to be the Third Mark.

zh: 第四印记是皮肤上断断续续的改善，从手臂到大腿的痕迹成补丁状生长。洁净，柔软，而透着红光，好似爬出山泉水的新生儿的皮肤。只是一块一块的。
en: The Fourth Mark is the intermittent improvements to my skin. In patches, along my arms or down my thighs, it is clear, soft, glowing, like the skin of a child new-stepped from spring-water. But only i

zh: “增生和出疹”是第五印记的昵称。我已经长出了两根新的手指，但只有一根长在我的手上。我的膝盖弯曲处出现了一个瘘管。压迫其边缘带来一种轻柔的快感。我认为我背上的小小隆起是一个新的器官。
en: 'Extensions and eruptions' is the nick-name for the Fifth Mark. I have developed two new fingers, and only one is on my hand. I have a fistula in the crook of my knee. Pressure upon its edges is a sof

zh: 我发现自己比以前高了一个手的长度后察觉了第六印记。我的每个部位，每个方向都肿胀着。我的皮肤是发光的丝绒。一对肉陷坑垫着我的眼珠。我非常干渴。
en: The Sixth Mark is identified as the point when I am a hand-span taller. I swell in every member and direction. My skin is radiant velvet. Pits of flesh cushion my eyes. I am very thirsty.

zh: 每次转变，我都会丢掉一些东西。
en: Each time I change, I leave something behind.

zh: 我可以向名为守夜人的司辰寻求永生与启明。他的触碰能为我带来独特的食欲，以及印记。
en: I may seek immortality and enlightenment in the Hour called the Watchman. His touch brings particular appetites, and Marks.

zh: 我在我们塑形者的左手上已等待太久。随我成就“大功业”之时已至。
en: For so long I waited at the left hand of our Shaper. Now the time has come for me to lead the Great Work.

zh: 赐我们愉悦者救我免于死亡，免于无趣。我们应为他献上一切。
en: Our Delight saved me from death, and also from boredom. We shall give them everything.

zh: 这些梦将我掏空，如一颗枯萎的橡树。现在我已大体做好准备。
en: The dreams have hollowed me out like a withered oak, and now I am almost ready.

zh: 随着时间推移，这名长生者将在城市中建立起关系网，增强他使用“阴谋”陷害我们的能力。
en: Over time, this Long will build up their network in the city, improving their ability to intrigue against us.

zh: 许多长生者为司辰效力。他们强烈反对晋升新具名者，只是因为他们将来可能，仅仅是可能，篡夺司辰之位。部分长生者则仇恨司辰。然而，他们对新晋具名者的反对可以说是更甚。
en: Many Long serve the Hours. They fiercely oppose the rise of new Names, who might, just perhaps, become usurping Hours some day. Some Long hate the Hours. Their opposition to new Names is, if anything,

zh: 我们永生不死的敌人会更认真地对付我们，他已做了更充分的准备。
en: Our immortal enemy is taking us more seriously, and has brought more resources to bear.

zh: 我们的敌手在观察和学习。
en: Our foe watches and learns.

zh: 我们永生不死的敌人会更认真地对付我们，他已做了更充分的准备。
en: Our immortal enemy is taking us more seriously, and has brought more resources to bear.

zh: 随着时间推移，这名长生者将在城市中拉拢盟友，积累资源，增强他袭击我们的能力。
en: Over time, this Long will build up allies and resources in the city, improving their ability to assault us.

zh: 我可以向名为白日铸炉的司辰寻求永生与力量。她的触碰能为我带来独特的食欲，以及印记。
en: I may seek immortality and power in the Hour called the Forge of Days. Her touch brings particular appetites, and Marks.

zh: 不完全是我，但也不完全是别的。
en: Not exactly me, but not exactly something else.

zh: 毒蛇盘踞在层层海水碰撞陆地的地方。我须得在水边寻找一个存有法力的去处。若我给苏洛恰那带去关于其他地点的秘密，她应该会帮助我。
en: The Viper coils where the sea folds itself against the land. I must find a place of power at the water's edge.  Perhaps Sulochana can help, if I bring her secrets about other places.  [Some part of yo

zh: 猫于夜间行走在黑色亚麻生长的河畔。
en: The Cat walks in the night by the river where the black flax grows. [Some part of you can roam in cat-shape. Send it to accompany your followers to a place where it can perform the dance you must danc

zh: 狐狸在山中草甸游荡，寻求着花制花人的触碰。
en: The Fox wanders the mountain meadows, seeking the flower-maker's touch. [Some part of you can roam in fox-shape. Send it to accompany your followers to a place where it can perform the dance you must

zh: 雕飞行于天路，其眼见如下：位于弥迦的是第一栖木，空中诸神灵首次交换秘密的地方。
en: The Eagle travels the sky's road, and here is what it sees: in Miah was the first Roost, where the powers of the air first exchanged their secrets. [Some part of you can roam in eagle-shape. Send it t

zh: 白鸽记得被其他所有人遗忘的事物。
en: The Dove remembers when all others forget. [Some part of you can roam as a pale bird. Send it to accompany your followers to a place where it can perform the dance you must dance. If you have problems

zh: 此形态中的我将臣服于持有王皮之门钥匙的人。
en: In this form, I will surrender myself to one who holds the key of the Kingskin Gate.

zh: 在海边一座静寂的大屋中，在借来的皮囊中，我们将寻求第二次蜕变。我须得在水边寻找一个存有法力的去处。若我给苏洛恰那带去关于其他地点的秘密，她应该会帮助我。
en: In a silent house beside the sea, in a borrowed skin, we will seek the second change. I must find a place of power at the water's edge. Perhaps Sulochana can help, if I bring her secrets about other p

zh: 在黑兽掘洞栖身的石林中，在眼之面具的背后，我们将寻求第三次蜕变。
en: Among the stones where the black beast burrows, behind the mask of eyes, we will seek the third change. [Some part of you can roam in cat-shape. Send it to accompany your followers to a place where it

zh: 在空气散发着诱惑的高处，我们将寻求第四次蜕变。
en: In the high places where the air is temptation, we will seek the fourth change. [Some part of you can roam in fox-shape. Send it to accompany your followers to a place where it can perform the dance y

zh: 在弥阿，蜈蚣看遍未来可能之地，我们将寻求第五次蜕变。
en: In Miah, where the Centipede saw all that might yet come, we will seek the fifth change. [Some part of you can roam in eagle-shape. Send it to accompany your followers to a place where it can perform

zh: 在他们记得太阳的遥远处，我将带着借来的恨意寻求第六次蜕变。
en: In the far place where they remember the Sun, I will bring my borrowed hate for the sixth change. [Some part of you can roam in wolf-shape. Send it to accompany your followers to a place where it can

zh: 为了最终的蜕变，我将穿过林中之门。献与美杜莎的祭品从来都是一头童子身的红鹿，所以我将作为一头雌鹿穿过此门。
en: For the final change, I will pass the Door in the Wood. Medusa's sacrifice is always a virgin red deer, and so I will pass the door as a hind.

zh: 我的灵将离开肉，化作一只头生巨角的兽。我将成为祭品？
en: My spirit will leave my body as a great horned beast. To be a sacrifice?

zh: 我可以向名为赤杯的司辰寻求永生与愉悦。她的触碰能为我带来独特的食欲，以及印记。
en: I may seek immortality and delight in the Hour called the Red Grail. Her touch brings particular appetites, and Marks.

zh: 山侧土黄、山顶盖雪，叫天天不应。
en: Tawny-flanked, snow-tipped, far from help.

zh: 撕祭部落将这组岩洞在原基础上进行扩张，并挖掘纵穴，设置陷阱。这里或许是个行刑场，又或许是个试验场。不论是前者后者，这里都是神圣的，部落民不希望我们进来。如果我们能逃过他们的追杀，狭窄的坑道里仍然还有最后一桩恐怖等待着我们。
en: The Tearing Tribes enlarged this cave system, digging shafts, preparing traps. It may have been a place of execution, or a proving ground. In either case it's sacred, and the tribes don't want us here

zh: 一座坐落在山顶，外观不吉的神庙。雪地上覆盖着片片闪烁宝石光泽的菌菇。这里已有一代人的时间没人造访了，但里面还是有什么东西在动。
en: A mountain-peak temple of ill-omened aspect. Jewel-bright fungus-slicks glow in the snow. None has come here in a generation, but still something moves within.

zh: 我们在书上读到八百年前，有一位苏丹必须在星空下才能睡着。他命人建造了这座观星台，以便将星空精准地画在他卧室的天花板上。可惜他们眼见的星星会对不加保护的人类心智造成威胁。苏丹的后继者下令封锁了这座观星台。
en: We have read of a sultan who, eight hundred years ago could sleep only beneath the stars. He ordered the construction of this observatory, so that the stars could be accurately depicted on the ceiling

zh: 这座冷冰冰的陵墓的建于远早于罗马陷落的时代。门口守卫着石狼的雕像。内有一间凿空岩石而成的石室，用来等待预言中为“太阳”送葬的队列带来其碎片。送葬者始终不见踪影，而死者仍被引来此处。
en: This chilly monument was built long before Rome fell. Stone wolves watch the door. A chamber within was carved from rock, to await fragments of the Sun at its prophesied funeral procession. That proce

zh: 在一方斜插出土，色若金狮的岩层中，劳工们为这片土地最初的众王凿刻出一片墓群。众王生前曾向往永生不死，或许其中确有一人实现了愿望。其他人则已躺进成堆的随葬品间。此处有代代相传的守卫团严密把守。
en: In a rearing outcrop of lion-coloured rock, labourers carved tombs for the first kings of this land. Those kings had hoped to be immortal, and perhaps one of them is. The others lie among their trinke

zh: 一本怪书，作者署名“阿伦”。记载了某些不为人知的教团派出暗杀者，追猎和进食据称永生不死的人们的经过。出版于十九世纪后期。
en: Erratic accounts, by one 'Arun', of the hunting and consumption of supposed immortals, by shadowy cults of assassins. Published in the late nineteenth century.

zh: 在无影众王时代，恩尼斯·拉扎里因预言太阳的分裂而被处以极刑。若抓捕他的人日后长成了长生者，现在大概会感到惭愧。
en: In the days of the Shadowless Kings, Ennis Lazali was executed for prophesying the Division of the Sun. If any of his captors grew Long, they probably feel rather abashed now.

zh: 一本忧郁伤感的预言书。
en: A book of melancholy prophecy.

zh: 1860年代中期，某位“伊女士”曾展示过一种娱乐方式，她称其源头来自本书。
en: In the mid 1860s one 'Madam Yi' presented an entertainment based, she claimed, on this book.

zh: 1860年代中期，某位“伊女士”曾展示过一种娱乐方式，她称其源头来自本书。
en: In the mid 1860s one 'Madam Yi' presented an entertainment based, she claimed, on this book.

zh: 亚历山大·普罗布斯特承袭杰·维苟财·詹森的基础性工作，修复了本书存于波斯某山中寺庙的一份抄本。本书在别处也时有发现，通常是烙刻在石头上。
en: Alexander Probst, following the foundational work of Hosty Moor, recovered a copy of this work from a mountain-temple in Persia. Other copies surface now and then, usually seared on rock.

zh: 亚历山大·普罗布斯特承袭杰·维苟财·詹森的基础性工作，修复了本书存于波斯某山中寺庙的一份抄本。本书在别处也时有发现，通常是烙刻在石头上。
en: Alexander Probst, following the foundational work of Hosty Moor, recovered a copy of this work from a mountain-temple in Persia. Other copies surface now and then, usually seared on rock.

zh: 雅典娜·凡娜对被名为浪游旅人的流浪司辰的研究手记；此司辰还有“笑鸫”、“蜈蚣”、“巡礼者”等许多别称——或依凡纳的说法，“面具”。
en: Athena Vana's work on the rogue Hour called the Vagabond, who is also known as the Laughingthrush, the Centipede, the Pilgrim, and by many other names - or as Vana has it, 'masks'.

zh: 雅典娜·凡娜对名为浪游旅人的流浪司辰的研究手记。
en: Athena Vana's work on the rogue Hour called the Vagabond.

zh: 一本无影帝国的传奇故事，由亚历山大·彼得汉斯誊自安纳托利亚一个隐秘神庙的年鉴。
en: A legend of the Shadowless Empire, transcribed by Alexander Peterhans from annals at a secret shrine in Anatolia.

zh: 一本无影帝国的传奇故事，由亚历山大·彼得汉斯誊自安纳托利亚一个隐秘神庙的年鉴。
en: A legend of the Shadowless Empire, transcribed by Alexander Peterhans from annals at a secret shrine in Anatolia.

zh: 圣多默派教徒卡西乌斯在本书中揭示了无尽军团——一支保卫罗马，抵抗无影帝国的秘密军队——的事迹。
en: Cassius the Thoman reveals the deeds of the Unnumbered Legion, the secret army that protected Rome against the Shadowless Empire.

zh: 由圣多默派教徒卡西乌斯所作。
en: A work by Cassius the Thoman.

zh: 由一只瞎眼白猫传递给戈尔迪翁的佩涅罗佩的故事。
en: Stories brought to Penelope of Gordion by a blind white cat.

zh: 由一只瞎眼白猫传递给戈尔迪翁的佩涅罗佩的故事。
en: Stories brought to Penelope of Gordion by a blind white cat.

zh: 一篇耸人听闻的花边文章，描述了关于“隐秘世界”的一知半解的传闻和当今道德观如何脱节，内附一份荒唐不经，大概为杜撰而出的纵欲行为概览。
en: A dreadful soufflé of half-digested rumour about the 'Secret Worlds' and the irrelevance of contemporary morality, including a catalogue of unlikely, and probably invented, debaucheries. [If I've boug

zh: 莫兰小姐存货里的次要品目尚在。随着时间经过，有陌生人带着书误闯进来售卖，有时候我们会满足他们。
en: The lesser elements of Miss Morland's stock yet remain. Over time, strangers drift in with books to sell, and sometimes we indulge them.

zh: 利米亚教团的指南书。该教团由类不死者组成，占据了罗马行省西斯班尼亚·加拉埃西亚，利米亚河源头一块隐秘的飞地。本指南的编者署名梅莱雷·伊夫尼，声称自己从“水域十字路”前来投奔教团。
en: A manual of the Ordo Limiae, an order of quasi-immortals who maintained a secret enclave at the source of the river Limia, in the Roman province of Hispania Gallaecia. This manual was compiled by one

zh: 利米亚教团的指南书。该教团由类不死者组成，占据了罗马行省西斯班尼亚·加拉埃西亚，利米亚河源头一块隐秘的飞地。
en: A manual of the Ordo Limiae, an order of quasi-immortals who maintained a secret enclave at the source of the river Limia, in the Roman province of Hispania Gallaecia.

zh: 利米亚教团的指南书。该教团由类不死者组成，占据了罗马行省西斯班尼亚·加拉埃西亚，利米亚河源头一块隐秘的飞地。本指南的编者署名红，声称自己逃离了“菊花朝廷的暴政”，漂洋过海前来投奔教团。
en: A manual of the Ordo Limiae, an order of quasi-immortals who maintained a secret enclave at the source of the river Limia, in the Roman province of Hispania Gallaecia. This manual was compiled by one

zh: 利米亚教团的指南书。该教团由类不死者组成，占据了罗马行省西斯班尼亚·加拉埃西亚，利米亚河源头一块隐秘的飞地。
en: A manual of the Ordo Limiae, an order of quasi-immortals who maintained a secret enclave at the source of the river Limia, in the Roman province of Hispania Gallaecia.

zh: 在翻译文献方面相当有用。
en: This is useful for translating texts.

zh: 一座光线昏暗的经营场所，属莫兰小姐所有；她买卖稀有，且有时会带来危险的书籍。
en: The dim premises of Miss Morland, who deals in rare and sometimes dangerous books.

zh: 这里是书商莫兰的旧址，如今我必须去别的地方寻找知识了。
en: This used to be the address of Morland's, the book dealer, but I'll have to look elsewhere for knowledge, now.

zh: 语焉不详的路线说明，教人如何去往某个不为人知的地址，找到名为莫兰的稀有书籍商贩。
en: Cryptic directions to the obscure address of a certain Morland, a dealer in the rarer sort of book.

zh: 利米亚教团的指南书。该教团由类不死者组成，占据了罗马行省西斯班尼亚·加拉埃西亚，利米亚河源头一块隐秘的飞地。本指南的编者署名布兹加什，声称自己放弃了侍奉骨白鸽，从“信雕之城”前来投奔教团。
en: A manual of the Ordo Limiae, an order of quasi-immortals who maintained a secret enclave at the source of the river Limia, in the Roman province of Hispania Gallaecia. This manual was compiled by one

zh: 利米亚教团的指南书。该教团由类不死者组成，占据了罗马行省西斯班尼亚·加拉埃西亚，利米亚河源头一块隐秘的飞地。
en: A manual of the Ordo Limiae, an order of quasi-immortals who maintained a secret enclave at the source of the river Limia, in the Roman province of Hispania Gallaecia.

zh: （《术士冯·布兰特的独家信实手记——记至高真圣域之真语言》）由某位绅士凭借一种超验主义的热情，亢奋地草草写成，不过无论如何，本书都是一本趁手的亚兰语入门指导。
en: Written excitably and in some haste by a gentleman in the grip of a Transcendentalist enthusiasm, but nevertheless a useful guide to Aramaic.

zh: 亚历山德罗·拉克罗切亲王耗费多年时间暗中钻研从意大利中部富奇诺沼泽获得的碑文。他的研究成果能助你解读富奇诺语文献。
en: Prince Alessandro LaCroce spent years in secret study of the tablets taken from the Fucine Marshes in central Italy. His work will allow you to decipher Fucine texts.

zh: 亚历山德罗·拉克罗切亲王耗费多年时间暗中钻研从意大利中部富奇诺沼泽获得的碑文。他的研究成果能够助你阅读富奇诺语文献……不过本书以一种简单的拉丁语密码写成，你需要先破译密码。
en: Prince Alessandro LaCroce spent years in secret study of the tablets taken from the Fucine Marshes in central Italy. His work will allow you to read Fucine texts... but it's written in a simple Latin

zh: 屈内与格特合著的《Ausführliche Grammatik der griechischen Sprache》——“希腊语语法详解”，是与这门颇具挑战性的语言短兵相接时的必备武器。
en: Kühner and Gerth's 'Ausführliche Grammatik der griechischen Sprache' - a Greek grammar. An essential weapon for a ferocious struggle with a challenging language.

zh: 最适于用来复习拉丁语知识。
en: Just what you need to refresh your knowledge of Latin.

zh: 硕大的碗状物，尺寸与锣相当，由黑色的玄武岩雕刻而成，碗身铭有对抗恶魔用的渊深曼达安语咒文。碗底则铭有同一咒文的亚兰语版本。
en: A tremendous bowl, big as a gong, carved from black basalt, bearing an incantation against demons in Deep Mandaic. The underside bears the same incantation in Aramaic. [If you speak Aramaic, you can u

zh: 硕大的碗状物，尺寸与锣相当，由黑色的玄武岩雕刻而成，铭有亚兰语和曼达安语的咒文。
en: A tremendous bowl, big as a gong, carved from black basalt, with matching incantations in Aramaic and Mandaic.

zh: “诗人有意无限地，长久地搅乱自身的所有感官，获得预见世事的能力……”可喜的是，这与智慧背道而驰。
en: 'The poet makes himself a seer by an immense, long, deliberate derangement of all the senses...' The opposite of wisdom, thankfully. [Study to gain a Glimmering: Lesson Learnt, which you may be able t

zh: 一部史诗，同时也是十足的艳诗，讲述了一个鲁莽的乐手和“赤红母亲”交合，最终被后者剥皮的故事——以希腊语和弗里吉亚语双语写就。由勇敢的卡尔·维尔朔恩在哈利卡纳苏斯某座神庙墙上发现，然而同样的铭文在它处亦有出现，且数目惊人。
en: An epic and notably erotic poem describing a rash musician's couplings with, and eventual flaying by, the Red Mother - in both Greek and Phrygian. The intrepid Cal Velshorn found these inscriptions on

zh: 抄自一面墙壁的数百行希腊语与对应的弗里吉亚语诗歌。类似于——甚至完全同于——在哈利卡纳苏斯发现的卡尔·维尔朔恩碑文。
en: Hundreds of matching lines in Greek and Phrygian poetry, copied from a wall. Similar - or identical - to the inscriptions Cal Velshorn discovered in Halicarnassus. [If you speak Greek, you can use thi

zh: 头脑如刀，此书如砥。
en: If the mind is a blade, this is a whetstone.

zh: 本书思路清晰，为学习这门浩如烟海，错综复杂的语言指出了一条明路。
en: A lucid book which outlines a careful path through the thickets of a vast and complex language.

zh: 一尊复杂到难以置信的象牙雕塑。表面交错盘绕着梵语与对应的伐诃语铭文。
en: An elaborate ivory sculpture of impossible intricacy. Around and across its surfaces, Sanskrit inscriptions spiral against matching lines of Vak. [If you speak Sanskrit, you can use this to learn Vak,

zh: 他们说，思想是自由的，然而思想是锁在肉体上的。
en: Thought, they say, is free, but thought is chained to the body.

zh: 非友，亦非敌
en: Not yet a friend, nor yet an enemy.

zh: 这只是对未来的一瞥。
en: A taste of what might be.

zh: 疾病令我虚弱。要想恢复，我需要休息和有营养的食物。
en: Disease has left me weak. I need rest and nourishment, if I am to recover.

zh: 《对绳结姐妹会与其堕落习俗之批判》。“伪寻爱绮梦式”耶罗尼米斯写下这篇狂怒的长篇大论，抨击姐妹会对他所属的“无敌太阳教会”的邪恶影响。

en: Dr Ibn al-Adim, called the Aleppine, has an interest in overlooked history. He has offered you a commission.

zh: 伊本·阿迪姆博士，人称阿勒颇人，对被忽视的历史有兴趣。眼下联系不上他，但他很快便会回来。
en: Dr Ibn al-Adim, called the Aleppine, has an interest in overlooked history. He's incommunicado just now, but will be back soon.

zh: 伊本·阿迪姆博士，人称阿勒颇人，对被忽视的历史有兴趣。他正需要有人协助。
en: Dr Ibn al-Adim, called the Aleppine, has an interest in overlooked history. He could use your assistance.

zh: 我赢得了他的信任。他会跟我一起离开此地。
en: I've won their trust. They'll follow me when I leave.

zh: 此配方用于复苏处于生人肚中的腐肉的回忆。这种药既然存在，必得到了制花人和悼歌诗人的允许……还有另一位司辰的协助。
en: That formula which quickens the memories of decaying flesh in the bellies of the living. Such a thing could not exist without the consent of both the Flowermaker and the Elegiast... and with the assis

zh: 我的眼中钉，肉中刺。或许我能想办法摆脱它。
en: Here is a thorn in my side. I may yet find a way to remove it.

zh: 某部失传歌剧的唱本，题材为阿波罗和马西亚斯之间的比试及此事的悲剧结局。唱词作者只留下了首字母缩写：“RKNJL”。
en: The libretto of a lost opera concerning the contest between Apollo and Marsyas, and its tragic outcome. The librettist is identified only by their initials: RKNJL.

zh: 有人越来越认可我这个人了。
en: Someone looks on me with increasing approval.

en: A concise disquisition on Winter.

en: You've said all that is necessary.

en: The deeper, the shorter.

en: To the next Mark.

en: This will end in a death. It could be my death.

zh: 此物不会拒绝被拥有。
en: Something that won't object to being owned.

zh: 一项资产，将被带上旅途
en: An Asset, ready for travelling

zh: 此人会和我一起工作。
en: They'll work with me.

zh: 退休士兵，时而以收费员为职业的“桥上的”乔伊写下了这首史诗，内容是亚历山大在与阿契美尼德王朝交战时，于伊苏斯做出无法解释的撤退决定，随后回师马其顿的故事。

zh: 奥克莱尔有着学者的面孔和病态的气质。
en: Auclair has a scholarly aspect and a morbid streak.

zh: 奥克莱尔沉静，做事细致而又冷酷。
en: Auclair is quiet, meticulous and remorseless.

zh: 奥克莱尔不怎么说话。她对自己的言语斟词酌句。她连动作也是能简则简，生怕浪费气力。还有，人们从没见她眨过眼。
en: Auclair rarely speaks. She chooses her words with care. Even her movements have become economical. She is never seen to blink.

zh: 空气凝霜，烛火奄奄，街上的人声静了下来。有谁在这里。
en: The air is frosty, the candles gutter, the street-sounds are hushed. Someone is here.

zh: 奥克莱尔不说话也不吃东西。
en: Auclair does not speak or eat.

zh: 奥克莱尔爱下象棋，现在比以往更甚。黑与白，错综迷离。
en: Auclair enjoys chess, now more than ever. The black, the white.

zh: 奥克莱尔爱下象棋，现在比以往更甚。黑与白，错综迷离。
en: Auclair enjoys chess, now more than ever. The black, the white.

zh: 奥克莱尔爱下象棋，现在比以往更甚。黑与白，错综迷离。

zh: 上面有制作者所做的标记。
en: Marked by its maker.

zh: 忠诚提升
en: A Positive Change in Loyalties

zh: 奥兰普·贝谢女士是《凯尔伊苏姆评论报》的编辑；该报刊登的是那些对其他文学杂志来说过于阴森或惊悚的题材。她向你提出了委托。
en: Mme Olympe Bechet edits the Kerisham Review, which publishes material too ghastly or thrilling for other literary magazines. She has offered you a commission.

zh: 奥兰普·贝谢女士是《凯尔伊苏姆评论报》的编辑；该报刊登的是那些对其他文学杂志来说过于阴森或惊悚的题材。眼下她正忙着出版的准备工作，但很快便会回来。
en: Mme Olympe Bechet edits the Kerisham Review, which publishes material too ghastly or thrilling for other literary magazines. Just now, she's busy preparing for publication, but she'll be available aga

zh: 奥兰普·贝谢女士是《凯尔伊苏姆评论报》的编辑；该报刊登的是那些对其他文学杂志来说过于阴森或惊悚的题材。她正需要有人协助。
en: Mme Olympe Bechet edits the Kerisham Review, which publishes material too ghastly or thrilling for other literary magazines. She could use your assistance.

zh: 对我的工作成果感兴趣之人，我与之走的愈发近了。
en: An aficionado of my work, with whom I have grown close.

zh: 在一瞬激情的推动下，我的金主提出结婚。如果我能抓住机会接受，我的人生将会改变……并失去其他的前路。
en: In a moment of passion, my benefactor has proposed marriage. If I seize the moment and accept, this will transform my life... and close other doors forever. [Talk to the Benefactor before their enthus

zh: 一位谨慎，聪慧的女性，有着窄间距的双目和以她的岁数来说极好的肌肤。她已经亲自打理财务多年，并义不容辞地向慈善事业捐款。
en: A cautious, clever woman with close-set eyes and excellent skin for her age. She has long managed her own financial affairs, and donates dutifully to charity.

zh: 在一瞬激情的推动下，最尊贵的尼科尔女爵提出结婚。如果我能抓住机会接受，我的人生将会改变……并失去其他的前路。
en: In a moment of passion, the Most Honourable Nicole has proposed marriage. If I seize the moment and accept, this will transform my life... and close other doors forever. [Talk to the Marchioness befor

zh: 听说最尊贵的斯坦福女侯爵已宣布她不太明自己看上我哪一点了。
en: The Most Honourable Marchioness of Stanford has been heard to declare that she doesn't quite understand exactly what she saw in me.

zh: 一位举止优雅的绅士，有着忧伤的眼神和后退的发际线，时不时发出阵阵友善、粗嘎、神经质的笑声。他不会再碰烈酒了。
en: An elegant gentleman with sad eyes, receding hair, and a tendency to fits of friendly, braying, nervous laughter. He will no longer touch strong drink.

zh: 在一瞬激情的推动下，提摩西勋爵提出结婚。如果我能抓住机会同意，如果我能抓住机会接受，我的人生将会改变……并失去其他的前路。
en: In a moment of passion, Lord Timothy has proposed marriage. If I seize the moment and accept, this will transform my life... and close other doors forever. [Talk to his Lordship before his enthusiasm

zh: 提摩西勋爵不再给我递小纸条了。
en: Lord Timothy no longer sends me his little notes.

zh: 某个爱我的人死了。或许他在遗嘱里给我留了东西。我该找个律师谈谈。
en: Someone who loved me has died. Perhaps they left me something in their will. I should speak to a solicitor. [Take action if you wish to pursue the matter... but there is a small chance that suspicion

zh: 我是多么的幸运。
en: How lucky I have been.

zh: 包裹中夹着我通信者的遗嘱执行人留下的诡异书页。我必须以理性或以激情来研究它。
en: A package of peculiar papers from my correspondent's executor. I must STUDY it, using either Passion or Reason.

zh: 我的金主颇有好感地看着我。
en: My benefactor looks fondly on me.

zh: 在拍卖行中出价。
en: A bid placed in an auction house.

zh: 《我的事迹、我的力量、我的成就和我所面临的不公》。波辛德的霍科博尔德——被有些人称为“遗忘家族”的不死者教团的前成员——在本书中反击了该教团。
en: 'My Deeds, My Powers, My Achievements and the Injustices Perpetrated Against Me'. Hokobald of Pocsind, formerly of the order of immortals called by some the House of Lethe, settles scores.

zh: 《我的事迹、我的力量、我的成就和我所面临的不公》
en: 'My Deeds, My Powers, My Achievements and the Injustices Perpetrated Against Me'

zh: 书中的四个神秘人物讲述了他们多年来在“栖木”——鸟型司辰的集会地——听到的故事。
en: In which four enigmatic figures tell the stories they've heard over the years at the Roost, the secret location where the aviform Hours congregate.

zh: 书中的四个神秘人物讲述了他们多年来在“栖木”——鸟型司辰的集会地——听到的故事。
en: In which four enigmatic figures tell the stories they've heard over the years at the Roost, the secret location where the aviform Hours congregate.

zh: 我的金主终会对我丧失兴味。
en: My benefactor's interest will eventually wane.

zh: 出类拔萃的猎人能够观察入微，将邪名直接转变为确凿证据。
en: Brilliant Hunters find meaning in the tiniest detail. They can convert Notoriety directly into Damning Evidence.

zh: 由波斯乡间的民间口传编集而成，编者为杰·维苟财·詹森。
en: Compiled by Hosty Tasmane from oral traditions in rural Persia.

zh: 一部译为弗里吉亚语的戏剧抄本，带评注，署名为“胜利者”米南德，此人可能是这个剧本的剧作家。
en: A transcription of the play by Menander the Victor, possibly the dramatist of that name, into Phrygian, with commentaries.

zh: 一部译为弗里吉亚语的戏剧抄本，带评注，署名为“胜利者”米南德，此人可能是这个剧本的剧作家。
en: A transcription of the play by Menander the Victor, possibly the dramatist of that name, into Phrygian, with commentaries.

zh: 我运笔必须非常小心。要是用力过重，我要表达的回忆会从画布上飞走。我会在亡者的肉体中寻见这些回忆，甚至也许，在我自己将死的肉体中。
en: I must go very carefully. The memories that I need to convey will take flight from the canvas if I depict them too strongly. I will find those memories in the flesh of the dead... and perhaps even, if

zh: 猎人将被囚禁。
en: Hunters will be imprisoned.

zh: 职位晋升或技能提升
en: A promotion or skill increase

zh: 相当大的一笔钱——足够打开一扇门，资助一项事业，或激起某人的贪婪。
en: A considerable sum - enough to open a door, fund a project, or excite avarice.

zh: 我已经预先送出资金来让这里的生活更轻松。
en: I've sent funds ahead to make life in this place easier.

zh: “卡特·卡罗”，她这样称呼自己，她平时是一个剧场灵媒兼算命师。但她可能还有更深层次的天赋。
en: 'Cat Caro', as she styles herself, earns a living as a stage medium and a fortune-teller. She may have deeper talents.

zh: 一个目光炯炯，精神警觉的女人，她能完美地回忆她的梦境。
en: A bright-eyed, watchful woman who recalls her dreams perfectly.

zh: 她有时仍在剧院表演。“卡特·卡罗，”海报上写着，“能找到通路。卡特·卡罗知道如何到达。”
en: She still performs in the theatres sometimes. 'Cat Caro,' the posters say, 'finds the path; Cat Caro knows the way.'

zh: 考古学家在墓穴中发现了宝藏。农民从地里收割庄稼。卡特·卡罗在梦中发现了什么呢？
en: Archaeologists find treasures in tombs. Farmers bring harvest from the fields. What does Cat Caro unearth in dreams?

zh: 曾称呼自己为“卡特·卡罗”的那个女人抓住一切时间睡觉。
en: The woman who used to call herself 'Cat Caro' sleeps whenever she can.

zh: 她叫自己“卡特·卡罗”。她以前是算命师。现在似乎没人弄得明白她是什么。
en: 'Cat Caro', she calls herself. Once she was a fortune teller. No-one seems to know exactly what she is now.

zh: 她叫自己“卡特·卡罗”。她以前是算命师。现在似乎没人弄得明白她是什么。
en: 'Cat Caro', she calls herself. Once she was a fortune teller. No-one seems to know exactly what she is now.

zh: 她叫自己“卡特·卡罗”。她以前是算命师。现在似乎没人弄得明白她是什么。
en: 'Cat Caro', she calls herself. Once she was a fortune teller. No-one seems to know exactly what she is now.

zh: “黑暗绅士”是这本抄本众多的书唯一被认出的，且认出时相当大张旗鼓的作者。
en: 'The Dark Gentleman' is the only, and rather grandiosely, identified author of this often-copied work.

zh: “黑暗绅士”是这本抄本众多的书唯一被认出的，且认出时相当大张旗鼓的作者。
en: 'The Dark Gentleman' is the only, and rather grandiosely, identified author of this often-copied work.

zh: 先锋派编舞家尼古拉斯·凯尔创作的气势汹汹的芭蕾舞中据说编入了此密续。
en: The avant-garde choreographer Nicholas Keirle supposedly incorporated this Tantra into his menacing ballets.

zh: 先锋派编舞家尼古拉斯·凯尔创作的气势汹汹的芭蕾舞中据说编入了该密续。
en: The avant-garde choreographer Nicholas Keirle supposedly incorporated this tantra into his menacing ballets.

zh: 我会记得。
en: I'll learn.

zh: 水注满杯，光注满头脑。
en: Water fills the cup; light fills the mind.

zh: 若付出时间与静思，有时答案会不请自来。
en: With time and peace, answers sometimes come unannounced.

zh: 这是一方缺失拼片的拼图。也许我会在学海的偏远之隅找到这些片段。
en: This is a puzzle with missing pieces. I might find those pieces in odd corners of scholarship.

zh: 这个课题需要投入超出安全范围的注意力。
en: This subject demands more attention than is safe. [To upgrade or subvert this Fragment, you'd need an HQ with a Library, the Never-No skill, or a Reason. There's a very small risk your Reason might be

zh: 真理是一条蛇。答案是喂饱它的饵食。
en: Truth is a serpent. Answers feed its appetites. [To upgrade or subvert this Fragment, you'd need an HQ with a Sanctuary, the Open Soul skill, or a Passion. There's a very small risk your Passion might

zh: 疼痛只在当下；知识则会被长久铭记。
en: Pain is only what happens now; knowledge is what we will remember.

zh: 一部以白雪女神在色雷斯的阿拜多斯城逗留九年为题材的戏剧，作者署名“阿波罗·织火者”。本剧的1892年版本在伦敦大获成功，该版本——似乎——是在那一年从更加古老的希腊语文本转译而来的。
en: A play dealing with the nine-year sojourn of a snow-goddess in the Thracian city of Abydos, by 'Apollo Fireweaver'. It enjoyed success in London in its 1892 edition, when it was republished from - app

zh: 一个散发着神秘主义者气息，留着一把没修剪过的胡须的声音粗旷的非正统天主教徒。
en: A bellowing and unorthodox Catholic with a mystic streak and an untrimmed beard.

zh: 克利夫顿最近正在即兴创作富有太阳隐喻的新祷告词。他仍然像个士兵一样大叫，像个水手一样酗酒。
en: Now Clifton improvises new prayers rich with solar metaphors. He still shouts like a soldier and drinks like a sailor.

zh: 克利夫顿的祀奉变得越来越复杂。他的眼睛瞪得越来越大，闪闪发光。他的嗓音更响亮了。
en: Clifton's devotions have grown complex. His eyes have grown wide and shining. His voice has become even louder.

zh: 克利夫顿仍在念诵他的祷告词，但这些话如旧镜子般变得怪异了。他每日清晨都要早起迎接黎明。他会直视它的面庞，而后准确地告诉别人他的所见。
en: Clifton still recites his prayers, but they are grown strange as old mirrors. He rises every morning to greet the dawn. He looks it full in the face, and tells you exactly what he sees.

zh: 克利夫顿怒吼着，诅咒着，但铁窗仍然存在，墙壁也依然很厚。
en: Clifton roars and curses, but the bars hold, and the walls are thick.

zh: 克利夫顿喜欢别人称他为“教皇陛下”。当有人不这么称呼时，他说不定就会大闹一场。
en: Clifton likes to be addressed as 'your Holiness'. When he is not so addressed, there may be a scene.

zh: 克利夫顿喜欢别人称他为“教皇陛下”。当有人不这么称呼时，他说不定就会大闹一场。
en: Clifton likes to be addressed as 'your Holiness'. When he is not so addressed, there may be a scene.

zh: 克利夫顿喜欢别人称他为“教皇陛下”。当有人不这么称呼时，他说不定就会大闹一场。
en: Clifton likes to be addressed as 'your Holiness'. When he is not so addressed, there may be a scene.

zh: 见证者聚齐！
en: Witnesses assemble!

zh: 克罗薇特的快乐是那种容不下不快乐的快乐。
en: Clovette is happy in a way that brooks no counter-argument to happiness.

zh: 克罗薇特那坚决的快乐已接近狂热。
en: Clovette's determined happiness now borders on the fanatical.

zh: 克罗薇特的乐观令人生畏。她像挥舞一把锤子一样挥舞她的欢乐。
en: Clovette's optimism is daunting. She wields her joy like a hammer.

zh: 克罗薇特总是兴致高昂。她爱笑。她的微笑像月牙的尖角。
en: Clovette is always in motion. She laughs easily. Her smile is the crescent at the edge of the moon.

zh: 克罗薇特在等待事情转圜。
en: Clovette is waiting for things to turn around.

zh: 即便人知道了克罗薇特的所作所为，也很难去讨厌她。
en: It's difficult to dislike Clovette, even when one knows what she's done.

zh: 即便人知道了克罗薇特的所作所为，也很难去讨厌她。
en: It's difficult to dislike Clovette, even when one knows what she's done.

zh: 即便人知道了克罗薇特的所作所为，也很难去讨厌她。
en: It's difficult to dislike Clovette, even when one knows what she's done.

zh: 随着一轮一轮地转动，它的纤毛有节奏地颠扑扭摆，它的身体由透明涨成猩红。它或许丑陋，但它却美丽好似玻璃迷宫褪去鲜血。它不会停歇，且它通体缠卷永无穷尽。转轮。
en: With each turn its cilia pulse and wriggle and its body flushes translucent to crimson. It might be ugly but it is beautiful like the withdrawing of blood from the labyrinths of glass. It does not cea

zh: 于低垂的红太阳下，沙丘连成的沙床上，一头怪兽与自己扭打着，身侧围拥着随同者们。它体型松弛肥大，色如不纯的硫磺；数条生着獠牙、触角般的脖颈像猫仔一样互相扭打着。它欢快地撕扯着自己的肉，淌下的血是金色的。它重重翻倒，摔成一滩肉褶山肉片海，压死了一团随同者，令余下的边是尖叫边是欢呼。
en: On a bed of dunes beneath a red low sun a monster wrestles itself while attendants crowd around its flanks. It is flabby and huge, the colour of dirty sulphur. Fanged tentacle-necks wrestle like kitte

zh: 它只是一块石头而已，尽管被打磨得光滑锋锐臻至午夜之境。然而待再细看：它的每个刻面都映现着一个光点。也许是火光的闪烁，也许每个都是一颗不同的星星。
en: This is only a stone, though it is smoothed and sharpened to a midnight point, but look closer. Each of its facets shows a single point of light. It might be the glint of firelight. It might be each a

zh: 我们的翅膀舒展而闪耀，我们的鳞片如黑曜石般泛着柔光，我们的双眼拥有宝石般的刻面，我们的脚爪黯黑到无懈可击。
en: Our wings unfurled and shining, our scales lambent as obsidian, our eyes faceted like jewels, our claws unimpeachable dark.

zh: 在雾气散去，太阳或被误认为是月亮之时，于天幕上的某些斑块可见一种黯淡的白金色。我们屏住呼吸，看着它转亮，直到每一重颜色与临近的分离开来，仿若一个个新铸的文字。逆孵之卵。
en: A faded pale white-gold seen in certain patches of the sky, when the mist is clearing but the sun might be mistaken for the moon. We hold our breath and watch it brighten, until each colour divides fr

zh: 当我回忆双角利斧，我想起了山楂花的香味。我想起了透射着金红霞光的紫色暮空。我想起了方做过活不久的她双刃淋漓的样子。
en: When I remember the Horned Axe, I recall the scent of hawthorn. I recall a purple evening sky shot with red gold. I recall that her edges dripped where she had lately been at work.

zh: 在漫宿蓝色的夜雾中，游弋着一个珊瑚所制的既为宫殿又为王冠之物，此物时不时让位于月亮。它用它的前沿吸进那些不显要的具名者，将自己的矿物和汁液涂裹其上；它从它的后沿排出他们，其已抛光磨亮仿若宝石余下的用于喂饱它那颗带刺的浪潮之心。
en: In a night-blue Mansus-haze swims a coral palace-crown. At its fore-edge it absorbs the lesser Names, coating them with its minerals and juices, and at its rear edge it expels some of them, polished l

zh: 玫红极光与蓝青电光争夺着天空。无夜晚亦无黎明，只存在预备为午的时辰和停滞于午的时辰。我们每一个人都向天空射下的金针敞开心胸。一切色彩在骄阳下皆显得更浓。
en: Auroral rose vies with electric blue. There is neither night nor morning but only the hours of noon anticipated and lingering noon. We each of us open our hearts to the golden needle of the sky. All c

zh: 没入黑暗前的林地与其莹绿好似带毒的果实、其色如肉红石髓的根茎。飞蛾的眼睛因饥饿而透出欢喜。他正在捕猎。
en: The Wood before its darkening, its fruits poison-green, its carnelian roots. The Moth's eyes are merry with hunger. He is hunting.

zh: 有些快乐是金钱也买不到的。这并非其中之一。
en: There are joys that even money can't buy. This is not numbered among them.

zh: 大马士革绳结姐妹会的头领“黑色”艾丽遭到了皇帝狄第乌斯·尤利安努斯囚禁。据记载，她曾命人将这些秘密教义记录成书，以保其免于遗失，永远存续。遗憾的是，据传说狄第乌斯·尤利安努斯命人将本作随她葬入了一个没有标记的墓室。
en: Black Elie, Damascene Matriarch of the Sisterhood of the Knot, was imprisoned by the emperor Didius Julianus. She reportedly instructed that these secret doctrines be recorded in writing to preserve a

en: An opportunity for profit. I should complete this and return it to a patron.
