zh: 波比愿意为她感兴趣的情报付出酬劳。这一次，她想要一些关于冬之奥秘的记录。
en: Poppy will pay for information of interest. This time, she'd like some notes on the corporal mysteries of Winter. [You'll need a matching Lore fragment of at least level 2 to complete this commission.

zh: 波比愿意为她感兴趣的情报付出酬劳。这一次，她想要一篇关于冬之奥秘的详尽报告。
en: Poppy will pay for information of interest. This time, she'd like Substantial research on the corporal mysteries of Winter. [You'll need a matching Lore fragment of at least level 4 to complete this c

zh: 波比愿意为她感兴趣的情报付出酬劳。这一次，她想要一篇关于冬之奥秘的、极其详尽的报告。
en: Poppy will pay for information of interest. This time, she'd like Very Substantial research on the corporal mysteries of Winter. [You'll need a matching Lore fragment of at least level 6 to complete t

zh: 你已经完成的任务。
en: You have completed a task.

zh: 你需要完成的任务。
en: A task awaits your attention.

zh: 隐秘世界的新晋者用斯宾特里亚古币支付各种费用。古铁币面值最低，背面画着凶杀的情景。
en: Initiates of the hidden world use spintriae to pay for services. Iron spintriae are the lowest denomination, and display scenes of murder on their reverse side. [Spintriae can be sold for more traditi

zh: 隐秘世界的新晋者用斯宾特里亚古币支付各种费用。古青铜币背面通常画着塑造的场景。
en: Initiates of the hidden world use spintriae to pay for services. Bronze spintriae usually display the act of shaping on their reverse side. [Spintriae can be sold for more traditional funds at an auct

zh: 隐秘世界的新晋者用斯宾特里亚古币支付各种费用。古币很容易卖出数值更高的普通货币。古银币更具价值，背面通常画着淫荡的情景。
en: Initiates of the hidden world use spintriae to pay for services. They can easily be sold for more traditional funds. Silver spintriae are more valuable, and usually display scenes of debauchery on the

zh: 隐秘世界的新晋者用斯宾特里亚古币支付各种费用。古币很容易卖出数值更高的普通货币。古金币背面画着凡人肉眼不可见的情形。
en: Initiates of the hidden world use spintriae to pay for services. They can easily be sold for more traditional funds. Gold spintriae display scenes that are not visible to mortal eyes on their reverse

zh: 斯宾特里亚古币是隐秘世界的通行货币。有的古币价值比其它的更高。不过可别指望花它能找零，它可不是寻常物件。
en: Spintriae are the currency of the hidden world. Some spintriae are worth more than others. But don't expect to get change. This is not a grocery.

zh: Used to mark opx events
en: Used to mark opx events

zh: 我的头脑疲累。
en: My mind is weary.

zh: 探险家及杀人犯拉尔斯·韦斯特格伦的日记。不巧的是，他为了防人耳目将其翻译成了弗里吉亚语。
en: The diaries of the explorer and murderer Lars Westergren. He's unhelpfully translated them into Phrygian, for privacy.

zh: 探险家及杀人犯拉尔斯·韦斯特格伦的日记。不巧的是，他为了防人耳目将其翻译成了弗里吉亚语。
en: The diaries of the explorer and murderer Lars Westergren. He's unhelpfully translated them into Phrygian, for privacy.

zh: 与一位博学的圣职者的互惠协定。
en: A mutually beneficial arrangement with a learned holy man.

zh: “我们读到我们应该宽恕我们的敌人；但是我们并没有读到我们应该宽恕我们的朋友。” —— 柯西莫·德·美第奇
en: 'We read that we ought to forgive our enemies; but we do not read that we ought to forgive our friends.' - Cosimo de'Medici

zh: 与权威人士的互惠协定。
en: A mutually beneficial arrangement with someone in authority.

zh: 与革命者或其他狂热分子的互惠协定。
en: A mutually beneficial arrangement with revolutionaries or other enthusiasts.

zh: 与实权人物的互惠协定。
en: A mutually beneficial arrangement with someone of real power.

zh: 与相关内行人的互惠协定。
en: A mutually beneficial arrangement with relevant professionals.

zh: 康妮找到了最后一条令她无法忍住不尝试的弃小搏大之道……又或者她只是在享受人生。
en: Connie's found one last gambit she couldn't resist... or perhaps she's just enjoying life.

zh: 侍奉不合康妮的天性。总有一天她会晋升，如河水涨升。但现下她为了征服而俯首。
en: It's not in Connie's nature to serve. One day she'll rise as a river rises. But for now, she stoops to conquer.

zh: 康妮……康妮正在重新考量情况。
en: Connie's... Connie's thinking things over.

zh: 康妮从来不能忍住不接受挑战。
en: Connie never could resist a challenge.

zh: 祖尔菲亚留下了一张字迹潦草的纸条，上面写着她还要“在林地里散会儿步”。
en: Zulfiya has left a scrawled note indicating that she will be 'walking the Wood' for some time yet.

zh: 祖尔菲亚的剪刀从不离身。
en: Zulfiya never goes anywhere without her scissors.

zh: “白天结束了。现在是夜晚。”
en: 'That was the day. Now this is the night.'

zh: 我的一个信徒对实体的起源和终结有了一种不可思议的理解。
en: One of my congregation has achieved a preternatural understanding of where entities begin, and where they end.

zh: 我的一个信徒渴望拥抱他自身的终结。
en: One of my congregation is eager to embrace their end.

zh: 为了恢复体力，欧索丽娜需要独处的时间，需要亲近大地。
en: To recover her strength, Orsolina needs time alone and close to the earth.

zh: 一个目光坚定、肌肉发达的女人。她曾为狮子匠服务，但在那之后她的食谱发生了变化。不过，她仍然保留了一些从前的力量。
en: A muscular woman with a steady gaze. Her diet has changed since her days in the Lionsmith's service, but she still retains some of her old strength.

zh: 瓦西尔已经耗尽了力气，体形也随之软化。现在他全身滴着水，呱呱地叫着，躲到温暖的地方去了。
en: Vasil's exertions have softened his shape. For now he has retreated, dripping and cawing, to somewhere warmer.

zh: 一个丝绒般俊美的男人。他血统中非人类的部分并不明显，除非你特别在意他的手指为什么异常灵活、或者为什么他总是在流鼻涕。
en: A smoothly handsome man whose unhuman ancestry is not apparent, unless you know to look for his abnormally flexible fingers, or wonder why his nose tends to run.

zh: 多明卡斯去火炉边补充体力了——还带上了一把铲子。
en: Dominykas has left to replenish himself, at the hearth and with the shovel.

zh: 多明卡斯的脸上随时都带着微笑。不幸的是，他有个总是剔牙的习惯。
en: Dominykas has a ready smile and an unfortunate habit of picking his teeth.

zh: 柴玛藏身于只有她自己知道的黑暗角落，正在舔舐伤口。
en: Chaima has gone wherever she goes to lick her wounds in darkness.

zh: 一个身躯灵巧、面容严肃的柏柏尔女人。灰棕色的条纹长袍为她增添了些法国-摩洛哥式的风情。
en: A lithe, hard-faced Berber woman in the grey-brown striped jellaba of a French-Moroccan auxiliary.

zh: 狻猊只吃来自天空的食物来保持它们的力量和地位。这只显然是被厄客德娜臭名昭著的乳汁诱惑了……这引出了许多问题，但最紧迫的问题是，我们现在该拿它怎么办？
en: Labhites maintain their power and their position as long as they consume only those things that are found in the sky. This one's apparently been seduced by Echidna's notorious milks... which raises a

zh: 属于这里的人。
en: Someone who belongs here.

zh: 一个老人，头戴以珠子装饰的便帽，两颊皱纹深深，脸上留着灰白的胡茬，眼里流露出古老的哀伤。
en: An elderly man in a beaded skullcap, with deeply seamed cheeks, a whitening stubbled beard, and old sorrow in his eyes.

zh: 一个老人，头戴以珠子装饰的便帽，两颊皱纹深深，脸上留着灰白的胡茬，眼里流露出古老的哀伤。
en: An elderly man in a beaded skullcap, with deeply seamed cheeks, a whitening stubbled beard and old sorrow in his eyes.

zh: 乔吉奥说自己必须躲起来，直到他的神经恢复。
en: Giorgiou is in hiding until (he says) his nerves have recovered.

zh: 一个神情忧郁、带着些哲学家气质的走私商人。他走私稀有的香烟，稀有的毒药和更稀有的武器。
en: A melancholy and philosophical smuggler of rare cigarettes, rare poisons and rarer weapons.

zh: 米蕾娅躲在床单底下瑟瑟发抖。
en: Mireya is hiding, shivering, beneath her coverlet.

zh: 一个胖乎乎的、有着明亮眼睛的女人。她的神情如在梦中一般恍惚，又总是吞吞吐吐，犹豫不决。
en: A plump, dreamy, hesitant woman with very bright eyes.

zh: 在得到新的补给品之前，雅宁斯暂时歇业。
en: Jannings has retired from the field until his new supplies arrive.

zh: 他是拆解和装配方面的专家。
en: A specialist in disassembly and also in assembly.

zh: 来信者记录了他对我的观察。他相信我有“潜能”。同样的，他也记录了他对另一个人的观察。经过一定的时间与学习，我应该能找出那人的下落。
en: My correspondent had recorded his observations of me. He believed I had 'potential'. He recorded his observations of someone else, too. With time and study, perhaps I can find that person.

zh: 密教社团，追奉抗争与征服之司辰。
en: An occult society dedicated to the Hours of struggle and conquest. [You can use Cults with the Talk verb to recruit or promote followers, and to send them out on errands to gather resources. A Believe

zh: 密教社团，追奉改变与再造之火。
en: An occult society dedicated to the fire that changes and remakes. [You can use Cults with the Talk verb to recruit or promote followers, and to send them out on errands to gather resources. A Believer

zh: 密教社团，追奉生育、鲜血与食欲之奥秘。
en: An occult society dedicated to the mysteries of birth, blood and appetite. [You can use Cults with the Talk verb to recruit or promote followers, and to send them out on errands to gather resources. A

zh: 密教社团，追奉永无止息的鼓点。
en: An occult society dedicated to the drumbeat which can never end. [You can use Cults with the Talk verb to recruit or promote followers, and to send them out on errands to gather resources. A Believer

zh: 密教社团，追奉洞开门扉的司辰。
en: An occult society dedicated to the Hours which open doors. [You can use Cults with the Talk verb to recruit or promote followers, and to send them out on errands to gather resources. A Believer can be

zh: 密教社团，追求解明泄露自更猛烈处的光芒。
en: An occult society dedicated to the understanding of the Light that leaks from a fiercer place. [You can use Cults with the Talk verb to recruit or promote followers, and to send them out on errands to

zh: 密教社团，追奉混沌，还有变幻莫测的司辰。
en: An occult society dedicated to chaos, and the unexpected Hours. [You can use Cults with the Talk verb to recruit or promote followers, and to send them out on errands to gather resources. A Believer c

zh: 密教社团，追奉将临的静默与终结万物的严寒。
en: An occult society dedicated to the silence that comes and the cold that ends. [You can use Cults with the Talk verb to recruit or promote followers, and to send them out on errands to gather resources

zh: 密教社团，追奉一切业已失去的与或将复还的事物。
en: An occult society dedicated to all that has been lost, and what may yet return. [You can use Cults with the Talk verb to recruit or promote followers, and to send them out on errands to gather resourc

zh: 一小撮这东西就能制造一场大爆炸，就像被闪电击中的铝热剂。
en: A pinch of this goes up like lightning-struck thermite.

zh: 如果它的味道不是那么美妙，我会说它尝起来像血。
en: I'd say it tasted like blood, if it didn't taste so good.

zh: 丑陋的小东西，却带有一种令人振奋的温暖。
en: Ugly little thing, but there's a cheering warmth to it.

zh: 一幅令人厌恶的小画，描绘着异教徒们曾经干过的勾当。也可能是教会干过的。我并不知道这段历史，但我知道这幅圣像对锁有着某种魔力。
en: A nasty little illustration of what the pagans used to get up to. Or the Church. I don't know the history. I do know this icon has some kind of power over locks.

zh: 带着华丽插图的手稿残页，为了分开售卖而从原来的装订册中撕了下来。我不懂上面的语言——波斯语？阿拉伯语？但不知何故，我能在足够明亮的灯光下读懂这些语句。司辰间的争端，被隐藏的血脉，摧毁忠诚的秘密……没有多少实用的信息，但是却有一些关于“磨砺灵魂的声音”的东西。
en: Gorgeously illustrated manuscript pages, ripped from the original binding to be sold separately. I don't know the language - Persian? Arabic? but somehow I can read the words in sufficiently bright li

zh: 它摆在书架上会很漂亮，如果我还能有书架的话。
en: This will sit handsomely on a shelf, if I ever have shelves again.

zh: “没有人需要它，再也没有了，所以我拿它做了这么个东西，既实用又迷人，不是吗？”
en: 'No-one had any use for it, not any more, so I have made from it something both practical and charming, no?'

zh: 它闻起来像是尘封的房间。不知何故，我总是觉得它在背着我改变自身的形状，但我从来没有亲眼看到此事发生。
en: It smells like unopened rooms. Somehow I keep thinking it's changing its shape when I don't look at it, but I never catch it in the act.

zh: 你必须破除的不利影响。
en: A malign influence you must defeat.

zh: 我们需要心或者杯来压制这种诅咒。否则，它虽然不会让我们偏离目标，却可能会在将来困扰我们。
en: We need Heart or Grail to suppress this curse. Otherwise, it will not keep us from our goal, but it may trouble us later.

zh: 我们需要心或灯来压制这种诅咒。否则，它虽然不会让我们偏离目标，却可能会在将来困扰我们。
en: We need Heart or Lantern to suppress this curse. Otherwise, it will not keep us from our goal, but it may trouble us later.

zh: 我们需要灯或者蛾来压制这种诅咒。否则，它虽然不会让我们偏离目标，却可能会在将来困扰我们。
en: We need Lantern or Moth to suppress this curse. Otherwise, it will not keep us from our goal, but it may trouble us later.

zh: 我们需要心或者冬来压制这种诅咒。否则，它虽然不会让我们偏离目标，却可能会在将来困扰我们。
en: We need Heart or Winter to suppress this curse. Otherwise, it will not keep us from our goal, but it may trouble us later.

zh: 比语言更古老，比生命更年轻。
en: Older than language, younger than life.

zh: 我在蜕衣俱乐部开业的夜晚表演。
en: I perform at the Ecdysis Club, on the nights when it's open.

zh: 我在蜕衣俱乐部表演更艰深的舞蹈。
en: I perform the more exacting dances at the Ecdysis Club.

zh: 我偶尔被聘以重金，为那些非常挑剔的鉴赏家表演非常特殊的舞蹈。
en: I am occasionally paid a generous consideration to perform very specific dances for very particular connoisseurs.

zh: 这是致另一名舞者的信，署名为#LAST_FOLLOWER#。写信人描述了他于梦中一片昏暗的林地里遇到该舞者的原委。他梦到了鼓声、拍翅声、影子的皮肤、月亮的血。怪不得信被扔掉了。但我看出这些话里有层层深意尚待揭开。
en: It's addressed to another dancer and signed by someone named #LAST_FOLLOWER#. The writer describes how they met the dancer in a dream, in a dark wood. Their dream featured drum-beats, wing-beats, the

zh: 我或许能找到同道中人。
en: I might find a kindred spirit.

zh: 我或许能够找到做好弄脏自己双手准备的人。
en: I might find someone prepared to get their hands dirty.

zh: 今日，我或许能路过一些有趣的地方。
en: I might stumble across somewhere interesting.

zh: 一部十八世纪史诗，作者托名为“索利浦西斯托斯”。
en: An eighteenth-century epic poem by the pseudonymous 'Solipsistos'.

zh: 拉丁语著。十八世纪史诗，作者托名为“索利浦西斯托斯”。
en: In Latin. An eighteenth-century epic poem by the pseudonymous 'Solipsistos'.

zh: 某人慷慨捐赠的十年，至少灰烬账簿上是这样写的。
en: Ten years of generously donated life, as entered in the Cindered Tally.

zh: 如果它挣脱了束缚，可能会让你或你的追随者陷入疯狂。
en: If this breaks free, it may try to drive you or your followers to insanity.

zh: “没有什么比一个显而易见的事实更具有欺骗性了。” ——亚瑟·柯南-道尔。
en: 'There is nothing more deceptive than an obvious fact.' - Arthur Conan Doyle.

zh: 终结某事。
en: Something is Ending

zh: 有些伤口永远不会完全愈合。
en: Some wounds never quite heal.

zh: 赤红的太阳低垂天际时的凉风。海浪原先的话音。在我们身边一同跋涉积雪的虚影。都压在时间的玻璃板下，变得粉碎。我须得重温失去的事物……尽管其味醇熟而怪异。
en: The cool wind that blew when the sun was red and low. The voice the waves once used. The shapes that loped beside us in the snow. All crushed behind the flat glass of time. I must revisit what was los

zh: 由靠做雇佣兵，也有时靠家里汇钱过活的帕维尔·舒加在希腊生活时煞费苦心地收集成书。
en: Painstakingly assembled by the soldier of fortune and sometime remittance man, Pavel Shulga, during his years in Greece.

zh: 由靠做雇佣兵，也有时靠家里汇钱过活的帕维尔·舒加在希腊生活时煞费苦心地收集成书。
en: Painstakingly assembled by the soldier of fortune and sometime remittance man, Pavel Shulga, during his years in Greece.

zh: 这位绅士更愿意独自跟他的烟斗和《伦敦新闻画报》待在一起，但是不行，显然哪里冒出来了些讨厌鬼需要人像逮疯狗一样去逮他们，而且显然其他人都没功夫去做。
en: This gentleman would much rather be left alone with his pipe and his Illustrated London News, but no, apparently some conjuror of abominations has to be hunted down like a mad dog, and apparently no-o

zh: 这位绅士更愿意独自跟他的烟斗和《伦敦新闻画报》待在一起，不过如今他给关进了某个倒霉的地洞里。
en: This gentleman would much rather be left alone with his pipe and his Illustrated London News, but now he is mewed up in some wretched hole in the ground.

zh: 狡诈可以回应力量，力量可以回应狡诈，但是我们该如何回应痛苦呢？
en: Cunning may answer Strength, Strength may answer Cunning, but how might we answer Agony? [As your Defiance rises, you'll draw the attention of the Edge Hours. If it reaches 7, you may be able to achie

zh: 一套隐秘诸神的名册，内容半通不通。这本是其中一卷的英译本，记述了林地诸神灵。
en: An occasionally coherent catalogue of the secret gods. This is an English translation of a volume dealing with the powers of the Wood.

zh: 拉丁语著。一本于四世纪成书于十四世纪被译为拉丁语的书籍在十九世纪的重印本（作者为不知疲倦的纳撒尼尔·达西-埃弗斯）。
en: In Latin. A nineteenth-century reprint (by the indefatigable Nathaniel Darcy-Evers) of a fourteenth-century Latin translation of a fourth-century work.

zh: 一套隐秘诸神的名册，内容半通不通。这本是其中一卷的英译本，记述了“狮子匠”和“伤疤护民官”之间的敌对关系。
en: An occasionally coherent catalogue of the secret gods. This is an English translation of a volume describing the enmity between the Lionsmith and the Tribune of Scars.

zh: 拉丁语著。一本于四世纪成书于十四世纪被译为拉丁语的书籍在十九世纪的重印本（作者为不知疲倦的纳撒尼尔·达西-埃弗斯）。
en: In Latin. A nineteenth-century reprint (by the indefatigable Nathaniel Darcy-Evers) of a fourteenth-century Latin translation of a fourth-century work.

zh: 我已经很长时间没有上班了。
en: I have spent too long away from work.

zh: 现在我想事情想得更简单了。
en: I understand things more simply now.

zh: 凡人将沦为疯子
en: Mortals will be replaced by Lunatics

zh: 如果新的食欲未能得到满足，我的印记将消失。
en: If I don't feed my new appetites, my Marks will fade.

zh: 我的终极目标。我势必要为它付出代价。
en: My ultimate aim. There will be a price.

zh: 我开始了解一支从干旱之地的山松下发源的舞蹈。
en: I am coming to know a dance that began beneath the mountain pines of the dry land.

zh: 我开始了解一支从第一大陆上雨水丰沛的森林中发源的舞蹈。
en: I am coming to know a dance that began in the sodden forests of the first continent.

zh: 我也许能在这里暂保此身……
en: I might find safety here for a little longer...

zh: 眼下，此人全心全意尽义祀奉。但此情很快便会消退，到那时他或许会质问自己都做下了什么。
en: Just now, this one's devotion is all-consuming. But soon it may fade, and then they may question what they have done.

zh: 有的时候，俗家人会在热忱的驱使下投身于较为不寻常的义务。
en: Sometimes, Fervour drives laypeople to devote themselves to more uncommon duties.

zh: 有的时候，俗家人会在热忱的驱使下投身于较为不寻常的义务。
en: Sometimes, Fervour drives laypeople to devote themselves to more uncommon duties.

zh: 有的时候，俗家人会在热忱的驱使下投身于较为不寻常的义务。
en: Sometimes, Fervour drives laypeople to devote themselves to more uncommon duties.

zh: 有的时候，俗家人会在热忱的驱使下投身于较为不寻常的义务。
en: Sometimes, Fervour drives laypeople to devote themselves to more uncommon duties.

zh: 有的时候，俗家人会在热忱的驱使下投身于较为不寻常的义务。
en: Sometimes, Fervour drives laypeople to devote themselves to more uncommon duties.

zh: 有的时候，俗家人会在热忱的驱使下投身于较为不寻常的义务。
en: Sometimes, Fervour drives laypeople to devote themselves to more uncommon duties.

zh: 有的时候，俗家人会在热忱的驱使下投身于较为不寻常的义务。
en: Sometimes, Fervour drives laypeople to devote themselves to more uncommon duties.

zh: 有的时候，俗家人会在热忱的驱使下投身于较为不寻常的义务。
en: Sometimes, Fervour drives laypeople to devote themselves to more uncommon duties.

zh: 外交官兼教士斯坦尼斯拉夫·约翰·沙勒从某位赤红长生者的藏书中誊来了此文。不过，他这篇手抄稿现在成了凯尔伊苏姆某人的私藏。
en: The diplomat-priest Stanislav John Schaller transcribed this from the library of a Red Long, but his manuscript remains in a private collection in Kerisham.

zh: 外交官兼教士斯坦尼斯拉夫·约翰·沙勒从某位赤红长生者的藏书中誊来了此文。不过，他这篇手抄稿现在成了凯尔伊苏姆某人的私藏。
en: The diplomat-priest Stanislav John Schaller transcribed this from the library of a Red Long, but his manuscript remains in a private collection in Kerisham.

zh: 如果挣脱了束缚，可能会攻击你或吞吃你的追随者。
en: If this breaks free, it may assault you, or consume your followers.

zh: “你派出的伪装者将变成我笔下一条挺不赖的注脚，不过在我看，他也就做得到这一步了。”
en: 'Your pretender is going to make a damned good footnote. But I wouldn't expect anything more than that.'

zh: 他的势力让人望而生畏；他的身份，暂时不为人知。
en: Their power is formidable; their identity, as yet unknown.

zh: 初窥奥秘。
en: A true initiate of the mysteries.

zh: 我已经悖离了我自己的准则和偏好。
en: I have gone against my own principles, or my own preferences.

zh: 内外相易，表里互替。将某事物融解为一种影响。
en: What is without, within; what is within, without. Dissolves something to an Influence.

zh: 只要利用得当，任何东西都可以成为武器。
en: At the right angle, anything can be a weapon.

zh: DO NOT TRANSLATE
en: DO NOT TRANSLATE

zh: 从第一次世界大战以来，共和国来了又去。现在，亚琛在法国的庇护下建立了自己的共和国。我应该在它崩溃前离开。
en: Republics come and republics go since the Great War. Just now, Aachen has its own republic under French patronage. I should be gone before it collapses.

zh: 莱茵分裂分子在大街上与忠于德国的人战斗。我该趁早离开。
en: Rhenish separatists are fighting German loyalists in the streets. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 证券交易所外的体面生意人头戴高顶红帽、身着一尘不染的白色西装；瓜果摊旁边靠着的英国士兵们在酒气中争论不休；阿克塔咖啡馆内的神智学者正于双陆棋盘上鏖战。萨菲亚札格卢尔街的里亚托正放映着关于蜡像的德国电影，但是电力时断时续。夜幕之后，隐形的塞拉皮雍等待着。
en: Dignified men of business in tall red hats and immaculate white suits outside the Bourse; British soldiers bickering drunkenly by a melon-slice stall; theosophists playing backgammon in the Cafe Al Ak

zh: 松香与九重葛，白墙与海面的粼光，热烈的风和革命的闷燃。
en: Pine scent and bougainvillea, white walls and ocean light, sirocco winds and the smoulder of revolution.

zh: 民族主义者正在大街上与法国军队战斗。我该趁早离开。
en: Nationalists are fighting French troops in the streets. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 黄金时代已经结束，但荣耀却同渐弱的音乐般萦绕不去。自行车在卵石路上咔哒作响。一名白衣女子拉开了高窗的百叶帘。薄雾笼罩的夜晚，丝毧无名的具名者经行此地。
en: The Golden Age is over, but glory lingers like fading music. Bicycles rattle across cobbles. A woman in white throws open the shutters of a high window. On misty evenings, the Nameless Name of the Vel

zh: 睡意朦胧的阿维尼翁，寒风过境。
en: Sleepy Avignon, where the mistral blows.

zh: 气温灼热难耐，橘树落满灰尘，方砖铺就的城市在优雅地腐朽。奥斯曼人不喜欢巴格达。现在，英国人扶持了一个叙利亚傀儡国王，一边修建医院，一边把当地人炸得血肉模糊，迫使他们服从。这座城市随时可能再次发生叛乱。
en: Baking heat, dusty orange trees, tiled and genteel decay. The Ottomans did not favour Baghdad. Now the British have imported a Syrian puppet king, and alternate between building hospitals and bombing

zh: 哈希姆武装势力正在街上与民族主义者战斗。我该趁早离开。
en: Hashemite forces are fighting nationalists in the streets. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 这里的光线格外充足——足以让一小群艺术家在此辛勤创作——但这名字对饱经风霜的波罗的海花岗岩来说还是过于乐观了。
en: The light is famously good - enough so that a small colony of artists toils here - but it's still an optimistic name for a storm-beaten tumble of Baltic granite.

zh: 布达佩斯的冬天，堆满积雪的多瑙河。二元君主制已经瓦解，但这座城市再一次成为了匈牙利王国的首都。国王两次试图复位，又两次遭到驱逐。政治啊。
en: Winter in Budapest, and the Danube is dusted with snow. The Dual Monarchy has collapsed, but somehow this city is once again the capital of the Kingdom of Hungary. The King has twice tried to return,

zh: 艰难的二十年。小亚细亚的灾难；先是宣告独立，又和希腊结盟；随后连国王都被猴子咬死了 。但克里特人和他们的岛屿已经忍耐了四千年，并且将一直忍耐下去。港口为布满防御工事的群山所围，而午后日光中的伊拉克利翁依然沉静。
en: A hard two decades. The Asia Minor Disaster; independence, and the Union with Greece; and then a monkey bit the King to death. But the Cretans and their island have endured four thousand years, and th

zh: 在西伯利亚的针叶林深处，一间日渐腐朽的狩猎小屋优雅地沉入一片冰蓝色的湖水——据说，这片湖泊是坠落而下的月球碎片在森林中留下的疤痕。
en: Deep in the taiga forests of Siberia, a hunting lodge decays gracefully into an ice-blue lake - a lake, it's said, that a falling moon-fragment scarred from the forest.

zh: 日落群山中坐落着天使长米迦勒之城。我记得最清楚的是：我在那里喝了太多的李子酒，还和砌砖工打了一架。我们都年轻过。
en: Nestled in the mountains of sunset lies the city of the Archangel Michael. I remember it mostly as the place where I drank too much tsuica and got into a fight with bricklayers. We've all been young.

zh: 在最辽阔的沙漠中，莫扎比特人在遥远的石灰岩高原上建造了五座城堡，用作其人民的避难所。现在其中之一将为我提供庇护。
en: On a high far limestone plateau in the greatest of deserts, the Mozabites built five citadel villages to be a refuge for their people. Now, one is a refuge for me.

zh: 冰冷而洁净的空气，寂静的白色街道，苍绿点缀的露台和俯视一切的群山。我大概会在这里歇口气。
en: Cold clean air and hushed white streets and green-splashed patios and the mountains looking down. I might find something like respite.

zh: 七座山，三个名字，两个大洲和十万只猫。熙熙攘攘的大巴扎和金角湾沉默中突如其来的耀眼闪烁。
en: Seven hills, three names, two continents and a hundred thousand cats. The bustle of the Grand Bazaar and the sudden silent dazzle of the Golden Horn.

zh: 革命者和反革命者正在巷战。我该趁早离开。
en: Revolutionaries and counter-revolutionaries are fighting street to street. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 立陶宛的一半已经落入波兰手中，和平岌岌可危，但现在这里是首都。咖啡馆和舞厅里灯火通明，人们叫它“小巴黎”。但新总统上任，现在铁狼联盟的利齿越咬越紧。
en: Half of Lithuania has fallen to Poland, and the peace is desperately fragile, but this is the capital now. They call it Little Paris, and the lamplight glows in the cafes and the dance halls. But ther

zh: 黑土之城与黄金之门。在一小段历史中，它曾是自由的，但先是元帅，然后是红军结束了这一切。
en: The city of the black earth and the golden gate. For a little while it was free, but first the Marshal and then the Red Army put an end to that.

zh: 革命者和反革命者正在巷战。我该趁早离开。
en: Revolutionaries and counter-revolutionaries are fighting street to street. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 城堡伫立在巨龙死亡的山丘上，凝望着沉润在十月阳光中的城市。不要说元帅的坏话。
en: The castle on the hill where the dragon died looks out across a city mellow with October sunlight. Just don't speak ill of the Marshal.

zh: 列宁去世了，而现在旧都以他命名。它背负着革命与红色恐怖造成的旧伤，九月的洪水又给它添了一道伤痕。街道上仍旧飘着涅瓦河泥的臭味。在塔夫利宫高处一间窗帘遮掩的昏暗办公室里，季诺维耶夫等待着斯大林的下一步行动。
en: Lenin is dead, and the old capital now carries his name. It carries, too, the scars of the Revolution, of the Red Terror, and now of the September Deluge. The streets still stink of the Neva mud. In a

zh: 革命者和反革命者正在巷战。我该趁早离开。
en: Revolutionaries and counter-revolutionaries are fighting street to street. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 本月的大规模罢工和戒严令让雾都陷入瘫痪。隆隆作响的坦克驶过蓓尔美尔街。煽动者在这座城市致命的浓雾中密谋。也许无名的具名者会用她的迷雾遮蔽我……
en: Mass strikes and martial law have paralysed the big smoke this month. Tanks rumble along Pall Mall. Agitators conspire in the city's lethal fogs. Perhaps the Nameless Name will shield me with her mist

zh: 警察正在街上与工人们激战。我该趁早离开。
en: Police are fighting pitched battles with workers in the streets. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 五百英里之外，里夫共和国对法属殖民地发动了游击战。但是马拉喀什仍然有薄荷茶和遮阳伞。
en: Five hundred miles away, the Republic of the Rif wages guerilla war against the French Protectorate. But here in Marrakech it's still mint tea and parasols.

zh: 我暂时居于此地。
en: I'm here, for now.

zh: 温柔的夕阳在马什哈德青绿色的圆顶上落下，灯光沿着卡亚姆大街逐一亮起。装甲车呼啸而过：新任国王十分警惕。 一千年前，菲尔多西在这片群山之间写道：“人怎能躲避命中注定之事，他怎能逃避自己的命运？”实际上这还真是个好问题。
en: A mild and turquoise sunset in Meshad, and the lights along Khayam Boulevard flickering to life. Armoured cars growl past: the new Shah is wary. A thousand years ago, here between the mountains, Ferdo

zh: 穿越致命的沙漠，抵达绿洲。在此生息的村民有超常的健康体质。日光洒落，高地的山丘上立着拥有三开窗户的石壁小屋。
en: Across the deadly sands, an oasis where peculiarly healthy villagers thrive. In the hills above, a sunny, rocky cell of three windows.

zh: 在西海域的门关，被称作莫尔比昂的小片海域上有一座以其水手而闻名的岛屿。而在岛屿的最西端有一座安静的修道院。僧侣们享受与世隔绝的感觉，但据说他们也向来者提供庇护。
en: In the little sea called Morbihan, the gate to the western ocean, there's an isle famed for its sailors. On its westmost tip lies a quiet priory. The monks enjoy their privacy, but they've been known

zh: 慕尼黑仍然是一座优雅的城市，春日的栗子树依然惹人喜爱。 但是新仇旧恨和第一次世界大战带来的贫困让此地的生活变得困难起来，自从去年鲁登道夫和那个可恶的小奥地利佬在啤酒馆搞了回勾当之后，情形更是每况愈下。
en: Munich is still a gracious city, and the chestnuts are very lovely in the spring. But hatreds old and new, and the privations of the Great War, make it a difficult place just now. More so since the be

zh: 共产主义者和民族主义者正在巷战。我该趁早离开。
en: Communists and and nationalists are fighting street-to-street. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 在这个伏尔加河与奥卡河交汇的地方，一百年以来，昔日俄国内外所有财富都会在尼日涅的大集市上汇聚。现在红军进驻了回声萦绕的老地方，但是货品依然会被运到这里。铜，盐，谷物，茶，羊毛，煤，钉子，肉。
en: For a hundred years, here where the Volga and the Oka meet, all the riches of old Russia and beyond have met at Nizhny Great Fair. Now the Red Army garrisons the echoing old spaces, but still the good

zh: 革命者和反革命者正在巷战。我该趁早离开。
en: Revolutionaries and counter-revolutionaries are fighting street to street. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 在今年的“光之城”，社会主义者与共产主义者争论不断，地铁以轰隆声不断抱怨，憔悴的艺术家们在左岸的书店里徘徊。Plus ça change（世事多变）。
en: This year in the City of Lights, the socialists battle the communists, the Métro grumbles, gaunt artists prowl bookshops on the Other Bank. Plus ça change.

zh: 在永恒积雪的高地上，金刚乘寺院攀附着陡崖，像极了苔藓开出的花儿。
en: High in the abode of eternal snows, a monastery of the Diamond Vehicle clings improbably to a cliff face like flowers on lichen.

zh: 百塔之城在马萨里克和他“廷臣”的亲切注视下展示着它的共和力量。雕像每晚都守望着桥梁。这里是欧洲的心脏，它还在充满活力地跳动，至少还能再跳动一阵。
en: The City of a Hundred Spires, flexing its republican muscles beneath the benign gaze of Masaryk and his Castlemen. Statues watch the bridges each night. This is Europe's heart, and for a while longer,

zh: 在煤油发动机喧嚣的大港口外，在出产琥珀的漆黑海水岸边，耸立着由古老发条装置驱动的瓦尔内明德灯塔。它的灯光每夜都在海平线上闪烁。
en: Beyond the kerosene bustle of the great port, here by the dark sea where the amber comes to light, ancient clockwork drives the Warnemünde Lighthouse. Each night it winks at the horizon.

zh: 征服者帖木儿的大都会，丝绸贸易的十字路口，拥有上百种信仰的城市。在曼格特部埃米尔们被驱逐之后，此地先后处于沙皇和布尔什维克的统治之下。
en: The metropolis of the conqueror Timur, the crossroads of the silk trade, the city of a hundred faiths. The Tsars and now the Bolsheviks have ruled here since the Manghit emirs were ousted.

zh: 苏联军队和扎吉德派正在巷战。我该趁早离开。
en: Soviets and Jadidists are fighting street to street. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 在挪威最北端的覆冰岬角上，一座孤独的灯塔傲然挺立，背朝大海。
en: On an icy promontory in northernmost Norway, a lonely lighthouse stands proudly with its back to the ocean.

zh: 午夜的斯大林格勒。月色皎洁的街道上处处都贴着那位格鲁吉亚人的画像，散发着慈爱的威慑。
en: Midnight in Stalingrad. Everywhere, posters of the Georgian radiate avuncular menace across the moonlit streets.

zh: 革命者和反革命者正在巷战。我该趁早离开。
en: Revolutionaries and counter-revolutionaries are fighting street to street. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 将查理曼大帝的国土划分为东西两部的伟大誓言正是在此地被宣读。作为其结果，这片土地被争夺了一千年；作为其结果，双角利斧的阴影在这里久久地盘踞不去。顺带一提，这里有家讨人喜欢的小餐馆会卖一种我喜欢的炖菜，里面加了杜松子和白葡萄酒。
en: Here were sworn the great oaths that divided the empire of the great Charles between east and west. In consequence, the land has been contested for a thousand years; in consequence, the shadow of the

zh: 这里一直是珠宝商人和石绿矿工的城市。如今布尔什维克正在把它改造成重工业的熔炉。
en: This was always the city of the jewel-merchants and the malachite miners. Now the Bolsheviks are remaking it as a crucible of heavy industry.

zh: 革命者和反革命者正在巷战。我该趁早离开。
en: Revolutionaries and counter-revolutionaries are fighting street to street. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 鹰王发现了硫磺温泉，并在这里建造了他的城市。如今它是外高加索苏维埃社会主义联邦共和国的首都，而且这里已经有了汽车和电报局。这就是进步。
en: The falcon-king found the sulphur-springs, and built his city here. Now it's the capital of the Transcaucasian Socialist Federative Soviet Republic, and there are motor-cars, a telegraph office. Progr

zh: 革命者和反革命者正在巷战。我该趁早离开。
en: Revolutionaries and counter-revolutionaries are fighting street to street. I should leave while I still can.

zh: 花团锦簇的地拉那。索古派士兵群聚的咖啡馆里充满了醉醺醺的哄笑，制花人在嗡嗡作响的蜂影中哼着梦幻般的歌曲。
en: Flower-thronged Tirana, where Zogist soldiers crowd the cafes with drunken laughter, where the Flowermaker hums his dreamy songs in the bee-droning shadows.

zh: 西班牙人从柏柏尔人手中夺取了的黎波里，圣约翰骑士团又从西班牙手里夺来了它，奥斯曼再从骑士团处把它夺走，而此地现在归意大利管辖。柏柏尔人管的黎波里叫作“海上的新娘”，而如果我能相信我的大敌的话，我的母亲称这里为“家乡”。
en: The Spanish took Tripoli from the Berbers, the Knights of St John took it from the Spanish, the Ottomans took it from the Knights, and now the Italians run the place. The Berbers called Tripoli 'the B

zh: 阳光明媚的马耳他皇家殖民地所在地。英国水手漫步在倾斜的街道上。战舰皇家玫瑰号像一座铁教堂一样雄踞于港口之中。
en: Sunny seat of the Crown Colony of Malta. British sailors saunter along the sloping streets. The battleship Royal Rose dominates the harbour like an iron cathedral.

zh: 浓雾使威尼斯成了一座黯淡的城市，人们听到的远比看到的更多：海浪的拍击声，蒸汽船的呼噜声，伴以海藻和污水的味道。太阳是一只盲眼。如果丝毧那位无名的具名者也会在白日出行，那她想必会在此地出没。
en: Deep fog has made Venice a pale city more heard than seen: the plash of waves, the purr of vaporettos, the scent of seaweed and sewage. The sun is a blind eye. If ever the Nameless Name of the Velvet

zh: 这是少数几个在每重历史中名字都相同的城市之一……如果你相信那种事的话。在很长一段时间里，它是双元帝国的首要核心；而在更长的时间里，学徒们称其为白城。但现在它是第一共和国治下的红色维也纳，街道逐渐被黎明点亮。
en: One of those few cities which has the same name in every History... if you believe that sort of thing. For a very long time it was the first heart of the Dual Empire, and for the longest time the adep

zh: 总是有选择的：我可能会渴死，冻死，或者饿死。
en: There are always choices. I could die of thirst, or cold, or hunger.

zh: 多萝西是个热心的人，有着涉猎广泛的读书习惯。她为我所暗示的世界表皮下的形状而着迷。
en: Dorothy is an enthusiastic sort with an eclectic reading habit. She's fascinated by my hints about the shapes beneath the world's skin.

zh: 多萝西是个热心的人，而我已让她的热心为我们所用。
en: Dorothy is an enthusiastic sort, and I have turned her enthusiasm to our advantage.

zh: 多萝西的热心已变为走火入魔。她不会停歇；看她现在多爱跳舞啊。
en: Dorothy's enthusiasm has become obsession. She will not cease; and how she loves to dance.

zh: 多萝西走动时，我们能听见鼓声。多萝西说话时，我们能看见舞蹈。她的决心是她的盾牌和武器。
en: When Dorothy moves, we hear the drum. When she speaks, we see the dance. Her determination is her shield and her weapon.

zh: 多萝西没完没了的劝说消磨人的神经。
en: Dorothy's constant expostulations wear on the nerves.

zh: 多萝西不会停歇，永远不会停歇。她已发誓如此。
en: Dorothy will not cease. She will never cease. She has vowed as much.

zh: 多萝西不会停歇，永远不会停歇。她已发誓如此。
en: Dorothy will not cease. She will never cease. She has vowed as much.

zh: 多萝西不会停歇，永远不会停歇。她已发誓如此。
en: Dorothy will not cease. She will never cease. She has vowed as much.

zh: 道格拉斯把武力交换成了铸力。
en: Douglas has exchanged the Force for the Forge.

zh: 道格拉斯不喜欢锤子。这让他想起一起事故。但他会使用必用的器具。
en: Douglas doesn't like hammers. They remind him of an Incident. But he uses what instruments he must.

zh: 道格拉斯改变甚大。他的眼睛发光如雾中铁匠铺的窗洞。他的嗓音像煤块哗啦哗啦滚下输煤管的声音。他的手臂很长。
en: Douglas has changed so much. His eyes glow like smithy windows in fog. His voice is the rattle of coal in the chute. His arm is long.

zh: 道格拉斯伤心地摇着头。“从未想过我会反落到铁窗的另一面，我本该听我老娘的话的。”
en: Douglas shakes his head sadly. 'Never thought I'd end on the wrong side of the bars. Should have listened to me old mum.'

zh: 道格拉斯已忍无可忍。该去看看他在大惊小怪些什么了。
en: Douglas isn't having any more of it. Time to see what all the fuss is about.

zh: 道格拉斯已忍无可忍。该去看看他在大惊小怪些什么了。
en: Douglas isn't having any more of it. Time to see what all the fuss is about.

zh: 道格拉斯寻见了火焰。
en: Douglas has found the fire.

zh: 有产生恐惧的危险。
en: Risk of generating Dread.

zh: 我获得了一份在蜕衣俱乐部的舞台上演出的工作。
en: I have won a place on the stage of the Ecdysis Club.

zh: E夫人，苏洛恰那的“姐妹”，有着与外表不相称的“怪物之母”之称。她的老巢在哪里不清楚，但肯定不在海的这一边。她得花些时间才能抵达此处。
en: Mrs E., Sulochana's 'sister', goes by the unlikely title of 'the Mother of Monsters'. It's not clear where she usually lairs, but it's not this side of the sea. She'll take a while to get here.

zh: 一位自封“怪物之母”的，活泼、矮胖的女性，身着一身灰绿色、松垮垮的奥地利农妇裙。
en: The self-styled Mother of Monsters. A cheery, tubby woman in a green-grey garment like a baggy dirndl.

zh: 一切征服都在刃处开始。有一位居于此处之人目盲且不受创伤。而另一位，则是强力无匹，且越战越强。
en: All conquest occurs at the Edge. One who dwells there is blind, and cannot be wounded. Another is strong, and grows stronger.

zh: 一个拥有不雅习惯的优雅男人。
en: An elegant man of inelegant habits.

zh: 埃尔里奇已基本能管住自己在谈话中不用刀剔牙了。但他还不能把刀一直放在靴子里不拔出来。
en: Elridge has almost learnt not to pick his teeth with his knife in conversation. He has not yet learnt to leave his knife in his boot.

zh: 埃尔里奇正在耐心等待再次捅人刀子的时机。
en: Elridge is waiting patiently until it's time to knife someone again.

zh: 埃尔里奇从没被人看到过身沾血迹。一滴也没有。
en: Elridge is never seen with a spot of blood upon his person. Not a spot.

zh: 我们不给埃尔里奇用勺子或盘子。给他根尖利点的蔬菜都可能是不明智的……
en: Elridge is not permitted a spoon, or a plate. It is probably unwise to permit him even the sharper vegetables.

zh: 埃尔里奇的鞋上总溅着血迹。
en: Elridge's shoes are often spotted with blood.

zh: 埃尔里奇的鞋上总溅着血迹。
en: Elridge's shoes are often spotted with blood.

zh: 埃尔里奇的鞋上总溅着血迹。
en: Elridge's shoes are often spotted with blood.

zh: 我已经完成了一天的工作。
en: I've done a day's work.

zh: 探险家兼企业家T·埃弗莱特·杜普兰提斯曾经试图劫掠这篇经文所在的寺庙。他的命运被记在了附录里。
en: The explorer and entrepreneur T. Everett Duplantis once attempted to plunder the temple where this text was held. His fate is recorded in an appendix.

zh: 探险家兼企业家T·埃弗莱特·杜普兰提斯曾经试图劫掠这篇经文所在的寺庙。他的命运被记在了附录里。
en: The explorer and entrepreneur T. Everett Duplantis once attempted to plunder the temple where this text was held. His fate is recorded in an appendix.

zh: 这里的光线格外充足——足以让一小群艺术家在此辛勤创作——但这名字对饱经风霜的波罗的海花岗岩来说还是过于乐观了。
en: The light is famously good - enough so that a small colony of artists toils here - but it's still an optimistic name for a storm-beaten tumble of Baltic granite.

zh: 在西伯利亚的针叶林深处，一间日渐腐朽的狩猎小屋优雅地沉入一片冰蓝色的湖水——据说，这片湖泊是坠落而下的月球碎片在森林中留下的疤痕。
en: Deep in the taiga forests of Siberia, a hunting lodge decays gracefully into an ice-blue lake - a lake, it's said, that a falling moon-fragment scarred from the forest.

zh: 在最辽阔的沙漠中，莫扎比特人在遥远的石灰岩高原上建造了五座城堡，用作其人民的避难所。现在其中之一将为我提供庇护。
en: On a high far limestone plateau in the greatest of deserts, the Mozabites built five citadel villages to be a refuge for their people. Now, one is a refuge for me.

zh: 无处可逃之时将会到来。然后我必须作出选择：在我能找到的最偏僻荒凉的地方消失…… 或者在此地转身面对我的大敌。
en: The time may come when I can run no further. Then I must choose whether to lose myself in the loneliest place I can find... or choose that place to turn and face my Foe. [It's not possible to leave an

zh: 穿越致命的沙漠，抵达绿洲。在此生息的村民有超常的健康体质。日光洒落，高地的山丘上立着拥有三开窗户的石壁小屋。
en: Across the deadly sands, an oasis where peculiarly healthy villagers thrive. In the hills above, a sunny, rocky cell of three windows.

zh: 在西海域的门关，被称作莫尔比昂的小片海域上有一座以其水手而闻名的岛屿。而在岛屿的最西端有一座安静的修道院。僧侣们享受与世隔绝的感觉，但据说他们也向来者提供庇护。
en: In the little sea called Morbihan, the gate to the western ocean, there's an isle famed for its sailors. On its westmost tip lies a quiet priory. The monks enjoy their privacy, but they've been known

zh: 在永恒积雪的高地上，金刚乘寺院攀附着陡崖，像极了苔藓开出的花儿。
en: High in the abode of eternal snows, a monastery of the Diamond Vehicle clings improbably to a cliff face like flowers on lichen.

zh: 在挪威最北端的覆冰岬角上，一座孤独的灯塔傲然挺立，背朝大海。
en: On an icy promontory in northernmost Norway, a lonely lighthouse stands proudly with its back to the ocean.

zh: 讨嫌的人，比如烦人的上司，会被解决掉。
en: Annoyances like difficult bosses will be resolved.

zh: 伊妮德说话轻声细语，有时她的目光不聚焦到任何有形的事物上。大概你们俩进入了同样的梦境吧。
en: Enid speaks softly, and sometimes her gaze doesn't focus on anything visible. It may be that you've dreamed the same dreams.

zh: 伊妮德说话轻声细语，有时她的目光不聚焦到任何有形的事物上。她能看见他人不视之物。
en: Enid speaks softly, and sometimes her gaze doesn't focus on anything visible. She sees things that others don't.

zh: 伊妮德能看见他人无视之物，这给她带来很多麻烦，但非常有用。
en: Enid sees what others do not. It is becoming difficult for her, but it is very useful.

zh: 伊妮德能看到事物之间的间隙，她所学的技艺允许她有时将指尖置入那间隙中。
en: Enid sees the gaps between things, and the arts that she has learned permit her, sometimes, to place her fingers in that gap.

zh: 伊妮德再不说话。
en: Enid doesn't speak any more.

zh: 伊妮德总能提前发现敌人来袭。次次如此。
en: Enid always sees her enemies coming. All the time.

zh: 伊妮德总能提前发现敌人来袭。次次如此。
en: Enid always sees her enemies coming. All the time.

zh: 伊妮德总能提前发现敌人来袭。次次如此。
en: Enid always sees her enemies coming. All the time.

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 提升我的理解力。
en: My understanding's increased.

zh: 该猎人经常没法将邪名转化为证据。话说回来，他有时却能在没有邪名的情况下造出证据来。
en: This Hunter often fails to create Evidence from Notoriety. Conversely, they'll sometimes create Evidence even when they can't find Notoriety.

zh: 一名猎人发现了我的罪证——或许为真实，或许为臆造。
en: A hunter has found evidence - real or imagined - of my sins.

zh: “大地啊，开裂吧！哦，不，它不会收容我的。”
en: 'Earth, gape!  O, no, it will not harbour me!' [With Damning Evidence of occult activities in the city, the Suppression Bureau will be able to convict you. Evidence may survive the death of the Hunter

zh: 有些证据更为有力。
en: Some forms of proof are stronger than others.

zh: 此人无法再进一步晋升……至少在我麾下不能。
en: This one cannot rise higher... at least, not while I lead.

zh: 过一段时间即会恢复。
en: With a little time, this will recover.

zh: 总有一些提升的空间。
en: There's always something you don't know yet.

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 健康将转为病痛，可能对人造成潜在影响
en: Health will become affliction; this could also affect people potentially

zh: 有产生入迷的危险。
en: Risk of generating Fascination.

zh: 此卡不可使用，将会在六十秒后复原。我能通过入梦令其更快恢复。
en: This card can't be used, but it will recover after sixty seconds. I can recover it faster by Dreaming.

zh: 发生了一些事。
en: Something has occurred.

zh: 我掌握了某些让人欠我情的东西，只管一次用。
en: I've learnt something that will earn me a favour, once only.

zh: 我会众们的激情未必深刻，但在持续高涨。
en: My congregation's passions run strong, if not deep.

zh: 西欧已知现存的唯一一份抄本在1892年于大英图书馆失盗。一个叫做哈维·哈丁顿的人承认这是自己的犯行，并称已在无法控制的奇异冲动下，就着黄油和大蒜吃掉了本书。
en: The one known extant copy of this in Western Europe was stolen from the British Library in 1892. One Harvey Hattington confessed to the crime and claimed to have eaten it, with butter and garlic, in a

zh: 西欧已知现存的唯一一份抄本在1892年于大英图书馆失盗。一个叫做哈维·哈丁顿的人承认这是自己的犯行，并称已在无法控制的奇异冲动下，就着黄油和大蒜吃掉了本书。
en: The one known extant copy of this in Western Europe was stolen from the British Library in 1892. One Harvey Hattington confessed to the crime and claimed to have eaten it, with butter and garlic, in a

zh: 游学者肖黑在本书中讨论了五种人类可能的起源，并佐以一个蒙面的，他称之为“拾滩鸦”的形象。肖黑之后又称这些描述已全部被证伪，但无论如何，讨论本身还是被记录了下来。
en: The itinerant scholar Shohei debated five possible origins for humanity with a hooded figure since claimed to be the Beach-Crow. Shohei later claimed that all these accounts had proven false, but the

zh: 游学者肖黑在本书中讨论了五种人类可能的起源，并佐以一个蒙面的，他称之为“拾滩鸦”的形象。肖黑之后又称这些描述已全部被证伪，但无论如何，讨论本身还是被记录了下来。
en: The itinerant scholar Shohei debated five possible origins for humanity with a hooded figure since claimed to be the Beach-Crow. Shohei later claimed that all these accounts had proven false, but the

zh: 据记载，该密续由一个人称“褪皮”的神秘人物口述而来，介绍了神话中的那伽大蛇的很多习性。《剥皮密续》在亚历山大·斯派鹭那本充满被害妄想的小册子里占很大比重。
en: This Tantra is recorded as being spoken by an enigmatic individual identified as 'Peel', with many of the qualities of the mythological naga. The Flayed Tantra played a notable part in Alexander Sparr

zh: 据记载，该密续由一个人称“褪皮”的神秘人物口述而来，介绍了神话中的那伽大蛇的很多习性。《剥皮密续》在亚历山大·斯派鹭那本充满被害妄想的小册子里占很大比重。
en: This Tantra is recorded as being spoken by an enigmatic individual identified as 'Peel', with many of the qualities of the mythological naga. The Flayed Tantra played a notable part in Alexander Sparr

zh: 回首某个瞬间。下个瞬间即告消逝。
en: A moment in time. In another moment, it'll be gone.

zh: 奥马尔，又名“炽焰”，为了清偿自己熄灭圣焰的罪过，在推罗看了七年灯塔。而在这场苦役最后，他对火焰产生了莫大的恐惧，甚至无法与烛焰共处一室。写这本书是他苦修的一部分。
en: Omar, the Blaze, spent seven years manning a lighthouse in Tyre, in atonement for the extinguishing of a sacred flame. By the end of his stint, he was so terrified of fire that he could not stand to s

zh: 由“炽焰”奥马尔在苦修中所作。
en: The penance-work of Omar, the Blaze.

zh: 我曾经看到过这样一句话，“火是严冬，却是带有温度。火是暖春，却会耗尽一切。”
en: 'Fire', I once read, 'is the winter that warms and the spring that consumes.'

zh: 富兰克林·班克罗夫特勋爵通过“玫瑰女巫”菲奥娜·艾尔郡接触了制花人之术。从此他青云直上，以为上流社会提供密教法事和秘密享乐为生，直到在1790年突然音讯全无为止。
en: Lord Franklin Bancroft was initiated into the arts of the Flowermaker by the 'rose-witch' Fionna Ayrshire. He rose to become a Society provider of occult services and secret pleasures, before his abru

zh: 一本臭名昭著的绅士所写的臭名昭著的手记，以拉丁语写成，无疑是为了安全起见。
en: The notorious journal of a notorious gentleman, written in Latin, no doubt for the sake of safety.

zh: 足够供我舒舒服服地生活一小段时间。
en: Enough to support me for a little while in adequate comfort.

zh: 通说原文是由雷电在橡树树皮上烧灼而成，后被灵媒兼烟火技师雅各布·奥利弗·贵玛玽斯通发现并誊写下来。
en: As later transcribed by the medium and pyrotechnician Jacob Oliver Topp-Mugglestone. Tradition has it that he found it seared in the bark of an oak by lightning.

zh: 通说原文是由雷电在橡树树皮上烧灼而成，后被灵媒兼烟火技师雅各布·奥利弗·贵玛玽斯通发现并誊写下来。
en: As later transcribed by the medium and pyrotechnician Jacob Oliver Topp-Mugglestone. Tradition has it that he found it seared in the bark of an oak by lightning.

zh: 一本脆弱易破的手抄书，里面最引人瞩目的是一对同体孪生、光彩照人的忧郁美人形象和完整月相的图例。本书或能追溯到七世纪，而书中所用的语言——富奇诺语——则更加古老。书封面饰有珊瑚，书页边则十分锋利，足以割破咽喉。
en: A fragile manuscript, illuminated with twinned shapes of sad and luminous beauty, and with all the phases of the moon. It might be as old as the seventh century, but the language - Fucine - is much ol

en: A fragile manuscript, illuminated with twinned shapes of sad and luminous beauty, and with all the phases of the moon. Coral decorates its cover, and its page-edges are sharp enough to slash a throat.

zh: 可塑之才，但大概永远发挥不了什么价值。
en: Usefully malleable, but they'll probably never amount to much.

en: This one is loyal, but lacks initiative.

en: This one is loyal, but lacks initiative.

zh: 忠诚且肌肉发达。
en: Loyalty, and muscles.

zh: 忠诚且技艺高超。
en: Loyal, and good with their hands.

zh: 忠诚却贪婪无餍。
en: Loyal, but greedy.

zh: 忠诚且歌不离口。
en: Loyal, and always ready with a song.

zh: 忠诚且擅长开锁。
en: Loyal, and clever with locks.

zh: 忠诚且眼尖目明。
en: Loyal, and keen-sighted.

zh: 忠诚却又异乎寻常；即使在我们中也算是个怪人。
en: Loyal, if peculiar; peculiar even for one of our kind.

zh: 忠诚且静如落雪。
en: Loyal, and quiet as falling snow.

zh: 此人疲弱地控诉着自己的命运。
en: This one rails weakly against their fate.

zh: 完全失了分寸之人。
en: Someone who's lost all sense of proportion.

zh: 完全失了分寸之人。
en: Someone who's lost all sense of proportion.

zh: 被你玩弄于股掌的家伙，迟早要完。
en: Someone has fallen into your clutches. They won't survive forever.

zh: 被你玩弄于股掌的家伙，迟早要完。
en: Someone has fallen into your clutches. They won't survive forever.

zh: 如今我已尝过了墓地的果实，这种饥饿便时常来烦扰我。闻见腐败的芳香我的嘴就流涎水，肚肠像狗一样嚎叫。
en: Now that I have tasted the graveyard fruit, the hunger comes often to trouble me. My mouth waters at the fragrances of decay, and my belly growls like a dog. [Reduce this by feeding on Remnants. Do no

zh: 有时候，当我特别灵验时，头一排的观众会昏倒。
en: Sometimes, when I am particularly effective, the front row faints.

zh: 有时我会用出乎我自己所料的嗓音说话。
en: Sometimes I speak in voices that even I do not expect.

zh: 将恐惧转化为入迷，还可将其他元素转换为更加辉煌的同等元素。
en: Converts Dread to Fascination, and potential other elements to more Gloryful equivalents.

zh: 那个叫奥尔登的人已经不在了，等我回到岗位上时，情况会变得更好。
en: The man called Alden is gone. I may find matters easier when I return to work.

zh: 现在我是一名记录在案的职员，直接领导人是奥尔登先生。工资多了一点，下班也晚了一点。而奥尔登先生也一直把我看得紧紧的。
en: A position in Records under Mr Alden. I earn a little more; I stay a little later. Mr Alden always watches me closely.

zh: 工资多了一点，下班晚了一点，而坐在我先前桌位上的高层职员，眼中正闪灭着嫉妒的火焰。
en: I earn a little more; I stay a little later. Envy glints in the eye of the junior clerk who occupies my old desk.

zh: 旷工的事情暴露了。我也因此降职。我需要尽力说服上司来保住职位。
en: My absence from the office has been noted. I've been demoted, and I'll need to argue persuasively to keep my job.

zh: 工资多了一点，下班晚了一点，而坐在我先前桌位上的高层职员，眼中正闪灭着嫉妒的火焰。
en: I earn a little more; I stay a little later. Envy glints in the eye of the junior clerk who occupies my old desk.

zh: 旷工的事情暴露了。我也因此降职。我需要尽力说服上司来保住职位。
en: My absence from the office has been noted. I've been demoted, and I'll need to argue persuasively to keep my job.

zh: 我的才智必须被用到更合适的地方去！不过至少……现在的工资比干苦力的要多一些。
en: My intellect should be put to better use! But the pay is better than labourer's work.

zh: 旷工的事情暴露了。我也因此降职。我需要尽力说服上司来保住职位。
en: My absence from the office has been noted. I've been demoted, and I'll need to argue persuasively to keep my job.

zh: 我的才智必须被用到更合适的地方去！不过至少……现在的工资比干苦力的要多一些。
en: My intellect should be put to better use! But the pay is better than labourer's work.

zh: 旷工的事情暴露了。我也因此降职。我需要尽力说服上司来保住职位。
en: My absence from the office has been noted. I've been demoted, and I'll need to argue persuasively to keep my job.

zh: 理论上讲，假如有上司死了，我就还能再继续升职。
en: I suppose, theoretically, I could rise higher, if someone died.

zh: 随着小格洛弗的离去，我应该能升到更高的职位了。
en: With the younger Glover gone, I might rise even higher.

zh: 小格洛弗的消失在董事会中留下了空席，而我得到了它。
en: The younger Glover's disappearance left a board seat empty. I now occupy it.

zh: 我对怪力乱神感兴趣的传闻进到了老格洛弗耳中。这可能会导致我的职位不稳。
en: Rumours of my esoteric interests have reached the ears of the elder Glover. This has left my position uncertain.

zh: 公司对我这个职位的人还是比较宽容的，然而我旷工的事情已经被发觉了。我得想点办法跟老格罗弗套套近乎了。
en: Considerable latitude is allowed to someone in my position, but my absence has been noted. I will have to charm the elder Glover.

zh: 一本关于弥赛亚拿撒勒和他事迹的极其异端的记录，描述了他通过洞开肉体飞升入漫宿的故事。
en: An extremely heterodox account of the Nazarene Messiah and his works, describing his ascension to the Mansus through the opening of his body.

zh: 失传的基督教早期文献？以希腊语书写。
en: A lost early Christian text? It's in Greek.

zh: 食、色、性，有溺而无还者也。
en: Hunger, lust, the drowning waters.

zh: 草莓、肉桂、橙花。
en: Strawberries, cinnamon, orange-blossom.

zh: 当功业竟成，我们的创教者，我们的灵感会升入更高的境界。
en: When the Work is complete, our founder, our inspiration, will rise yet higher.

zh: 大功业岌岌可危。
en: The Great Work is imperilled.

zh: 一切都会结束。
en: Everything ends.

zh: 冷峻的猎人不会因辉光的诱惑而动摇……然而他自己的绝望或许能摧毁他。
en: Grim Hunters cannot be swayed by the allure of the Glory... but their own despair may destroy them.

zh: 负责阻止你入内的存在。
en: An entity that has been charged with preventing entry.

zh: 不是所有亡者都能轻易安息。这里的亡者不喜欢我们打扰。我们可以用刃战胜他们；他们也可能服从以冬之名来访的人。
en: Not all the Dead rest easy. These ones dislike our intrusion. We can prevail against them with Edge; or they may obey one who comes in the name of Winter.

zh: 不是所有亡者都能轻易安息。这里的亡者不喜欢我们打扰。我们可以用刃战胜他们；他们也可能服从以冬之名来访的人。
en: Not all the Dead rest easy. These ones dislike our intrusion. We can prevail against them with Edge; or they may obey one who comes in the name of Winter.

zh: 杀死一个已经通过三尖门的人是非常困难的。而且这里有很多这样的人。不过也许我们可以欺骗一个，或者投其所好。
en: It is very hard to kill anyone who's passed the Tricuspid Door. And here are quite a lot of anyones. But perhaps we can deceive one, or prey upon someone's appetites.

zh: 被赏赐或被强迫在这里侍奉的沉思者们。被他们那奇妙的誓言所约束。我们可以杀死他们，欺骗他们，但无法诱惑他们。
en: Contemplatives blessed or condemned to service here. They're bound by their curious vows. We can murder them, or deceive them, but they will not be seduced.

zh: 被赏赐或被强迫在这里侍奉的沉思者们。被他们那奇妙的誓言所约束。我们可以杀死他们，欺骗他们，但无法诱惑他们。
en: Contemplatives blessed or condemned to service here. They're bound by their curious vows. We can murder them, or deceive them, but they will not be seduced.

zh: 蚁母诞生于蛇群之中。她拥有许许多多的幼妹。你可以在战斗中将其打败；否则它将回应蚁母的神力。
en: The Mother of Ants was born among serpents. She has so very many younger sisters. It can be defeated in battle; or it will respond to the Mother's power.

zh: 蚁母诞生于蛇群之中。她拥有许许多多的幼妹。你可以在战斗中将其打败；否则它将回应蚁母的神力。
en: The Mother of Ants was born among serpents. She has so very many younger sisters. It can be defeated in battle; or it will respond to the Mother's power.

zh: 直到他们脱皮前一刻，人们都可能会把索奎焰特误认成年老的妇女。这个地方被索奎焰特占满了。他们强壮如扼颈的根，至少算得上半个长生者，但是他们可能受骗，并且对光很谨慎。
en: One might mistake them for old women, until they discard their skins. The place is aswarm with them. They are strong as strangling roots, and at least half-Long, but they can be deceived, and they are

zh: 为了保护我们所知世界的表皮，不息之心无尽地搏动着。
en: The Heart Relentless beats to protect the skin of the world we understand.

zh: 铜、没药、锈迹。
en: Copper, myrrh, rust.

zh: 《吾太阳神教核心厅室之极密》。本书记载了无敌太阳教会的秘密仪式，该教至少在两重历史上掌握过权势。
en: 'The High Mysteries of the Innermost Chamber of our Church Solar'. Secret rites of the Church of the Unconquered Sun, which held power in at least two Histories.

zh: 《吾太阳神教核心厅室之极密》。本书记载了无敌太阳教会的秘密仪式，该教至少在两重历史上掌握过权势。
en: 'The High Mysteries of the Innermost Chamber of our Church Solar'. Secret rites of the Church of the Unconquered Sun, which held power in at least two Histories.

zh: 延长雇工的时限。
en: Extend a hireling's period of service.

zh: 关于路权战争的预言，记录于兜颈贵胄还未出现时的次大陆。
en: A prophecy of the Wars of the Roads, recorded in the Subcontinent in the days before the Great Hooded Princes.

zh: 一本愁云惨雾的预言书。
en: A book of bleak prophecy.

zh: Forget it, J.N.
en: Forget it, J.N.

zh: 即便在礼拜日清晨，礼拜程序里也会出些怪事，但大部分外人注意不到。我们不邀外人参加在更冷冽的时段举办的典礼。
en: Even on Sunday morning, there are quirks in the order of service, but most outsiders don't notice. Outsiders are not invited to the ceremonies we hold at chillier hours.

zh: 一天晚上，祭司消失了，走得十分突然，还带去了他的亲信和教堂最珍贵的宝物。人们普遍猜测他去了更好的地方：一个靠近粼粼海波的阳光明媚之地。我们可以招揽他身后群羊无首的信众，把此处作为我们的活动基地。
en: The minister disappeared, one night, quite abruptly, with his inner circle and the church's chiefest treasures. He is widely assumed to have gone to a better place: possibly a sunny place beside a shi

zh: 我们将墙壁漆成同蓝紫色厅堂的浓重颜色。我们打扫了蛛网并给所有窗户安装上不惹眼的百叶窗。我们还换掉了所有的锁头。
en: We've painted the walls within the rich colour of the Violet Chamber. We've cleaned the cobwebs and placed unobtrusive shutters behind all the windows. And we've replaced all the locks.

zh: 幽灵——邻居们说——仍在楼内余音回荡的厅堂里走动。如果我们占据此地，当然了，它们会离去，又或者我们可以令之为我所用。
en: Phantoms - the neighbours say - still walk the echoing halls here. If we take possession, of course, they will leave, or we'll turn them to our use.

zh: 进行谈话与召开集会的地方。
en: For conversation and convocation.

zh: 新晋之人在此处赞美奥秘已近两千年。赞词改变，神明更迭，然而最终，不变的是血的存在。
en: Initiates have celebrated the Mysteries here for almost two thousand years. The words change, the gods change, but always, eventually, there is blood.

zh: 此处原为敬拜之地。我们可以让它重回旧用。
en: This was a place of worship, once. We could make it so again.

zh: 我们办的是私人事务。我们可以搬去更隐秘的地方。
en: Our business is private. We could use somewhere more secret.

zh: 我们总是先拉严窗帘，再把地毯卷起来，在地板上用粉笔写写画画。
en: We always close the curtains tight before we roll back the rugs and mark the floor with chalk.

zh: 椽子上爬过成排的老鼠。它们散发出的气味几乎淹没在河水的臭气中。这里离人们愿意去的任何地方都相距甚远。此类情况，在某种意义上，全都是优势。
en: The rafters are rank with rats. Their smell is almost lost beneath the reek of the river. It's a long way from anywhere anyone would want to go. All these things are, in their way, advantages.

zh: 腐臭河流旁的一具腐臭空壳。谁会追我们追到这儿来？
en: A rotting shell beside a rotting river. Who would follow us here?

zh: 萨缪尔·萨维奇所著的讽刺喜剧，主要角色包括身体抱恙然却狡诈多端的约翰·泰扬，他的情妇琅优，琅优的情人施梓，还有暴发户乌亚。
en: Samuel Savage's satirical comedy, on the intrigues of the ailing, but cunning John Sonne, his mistress Maevelin, her lover Leo, and the upstart Corvino.

zh: 要想恢复，我需要休息和有营养的食物。但是，当我涎水直淌，视线游离之际，我看见了那个赤色的杯子；它时不时便会同我讲话。
en: I need rest and nourishment, if I am to recover. But when my mouth waters, when my vision swims, I see the red cup; and now and then it will speak to me. [Dreaming with Hunger before the card fades wi

zh: 虽然文明世界的法律出现晚于司辰的法则，但是它们共通之处在于，有时需要献上牺牲。这个祭品是法律的代行人。
en: The laws of civilisation are younger than the Laws of the Hours, but they, too, sometimes require a sacrifice. This one is an agent of the law.

zh: 已被耗尽之物。
en: Something's been used up.

zh: 理想主义者不会有意志消沉——虽然有时他们的理想反可能阻滞他们。
en: Idealists cannot be demoralised - although sometimes their ideals can be turned against them.

zh: 为人古怪的钢铁大亨迈克尔·布洛克利喜欢引用该密续的语句。此人死于工厂火灾。
en: Michael Blockley, the eccentric iron magnate, was fond of quoting from this tantra. Blockley died in a factory fire.

zh: 为人古怪的钢铁大亨迈克尔·布洛克利喜欢引用该密续的语句。此人死于工厂火灾。
en: Michael Blockley, the eccentric iron magnate, was fond of quoting from this tantra. Blockley died in a factory fire.

zh: 按着自己的步调研习无形之术的人。
en: A student of the invisible arts, with an agenda of their own.

zh: 本书的作者只留下姓名首字母“C.R.O.D”，不清楚他是否为由不死者学徒组成的“忘却教团”的新成员。本书成书于十六世纪，在该教团——如果相信作者的说法——的权势从巅峰跌落之后。
en: It's not clear whether 'C.R.O.D', who is identified only by his initials, was in fact an initiate of the Obliviate order of immortal adepts. He wrote in the sixteenth century, after the order - if he

zh: 本书同时以其语调中近乎语无伦次的愤怒，以及它所含有的知识而闻名。拉丁语著。
en: This book is as famous for the near-incoherent fury of its tone, as for the knowledge it contains. In Latin.

zh: 此物可能导致绝望。
en: This may cause Despair.

zh: 我可能会看到不存在的东西。
en: I may see things.

zh: 要想恢复，我需要休息和有营养的食物。不过蚁母在和善地看着人的伤口。
en: I need rest and nourishment, if I am to recover. But the Mother of Ants looks kindly on wounds.

zh: 洞见奥秘。
en: An insight into this mystery.

zh: 工作时间长，但酬劳优厚。
en: Long hours; but good pay.

zh: 我们永生不死的敌人有所计划。
en: Our immortal enemy has a plan.

zh: 我们永生不死的敌人有所计划。
en: Our immortal enemy has a plan.

zh: 我们永生不死的敌人有所计划。
en: Our immortal enemy has a plan.

zh: 《山中亦如平川，许无前人未达之途》。拉格什的宁娜伽勒在本书中描述了山中有待发现的秘密。
en: 'In The Mountains As Upon The Plain There May Not Be A Path Where None Has Passed'. Ninegala of Lagash describes the secrets to be found in mountains.

zh: 《山中亦如平川，许无前人未达之途》，作者是拉格什的宁娜伽勒。
en: 'In The Mountains As Upon The Plain There May Not Be A Path Where None Has Passed.' A work by Ninegala of Lagash.

zh: 由十七世纪的神秘主义者兼古董商人克劳德·海索所著，描述了五大历史中的分歧事件。
en: The seventeenth-century mystic and antiquarian Claude Hersault describes divergent incidents in five major Histories.

zh: 医院搬工之职，并不稳定。虽悲惨，却也是你当下唯一的选择。
en: A precarious position as a hospital porter. Miserable, but it's all you can find just now.

zh: 降级证据。
en: Downgrades evidence.

zh: 升级证据。
en: Upgrades evidence.

zh: 扎迦利·韦克菲尔德（意为醒时之境）：一名悒悒不乐却又冷酷无情的督察警官。他最近被借调到了防剿局。
en: Zachary Wakefield: a melancholy, but remorseless, inspector of police. He has recently been seconded to the Suppression Bureau.

zh: 扎迦利·韦克菲尔德：一名悒悒不乐却又冷酷无情的督察警官。让我们确保他死在牢中。
en: Zachary Wakefield: a melancholy, but remorseless, inspector of police. Let us be sure he dies in captivity.

zh: 霍布森当过记者，私家侦探和——时间极短的——教士。现在他是防剿局的一名干员。
en: Hobson has been a journalist, a private detective and, rather briefly, a priest. Now he's an agent of the Suppression Bureau.

zh: 霍布森当过记者，私家侦探和——时间极短的——教士。让我们确保他死在牢中。
en: Hobson has been a journalist, a private detective and, rather briefly, a priest. Let us be sure he dies in captivity.

zh: 女继承人，女性参政论者兼冒险家。她最近把她那堂吉诃德式的热忱和大笔财富投入到为防剿局办事中。
en: The heiress, suffragist and adventurer. She's recently put her quixotic enthusiasms and considerable wealth at the service of the Suppression Bureau.

zh: 女继承人，女性参政论者兼冒险家。让我们确保她死在牢中。
en: The heiress, suffragist and adventurer. Let us be sure she dies in captivity.

zh: 她在分析多重历史时遭到嘲笑，被排挤出了学术界，这却让她变得更加坚定。她现在在和防剿局协作。
en: She was laughed out of academia for her analyses of alternate histories, and it only made her more resolute. Now she works with the Suppression Bureau.

zh: 她在分析多重历史时遭到嘲笑，被排挤出了学术界，这却让她变得更加坚定。让我们确保她死在牢中。
en: She was laughed out of academia for her analyses of alternate histories, and it only made her more resolute.  Let us be sure she dies in captivity.

zh: 我的投资所得供我度日绰绰有余。
en: My investments provide more than enough to live on.

zh: 这笔收入可供我舒服度日。
en: Enough of an income to live in comfort.

zh: 我手头十分宽裕。
en: I am well provided for.

zh: 《高贵之举社团那无可指摘的传承》。高贵之举社团的成员一经入团便继承了高贵的名字：“帕西瓦尔”、“罗兰”、“欧辛”。他们的灵感源自受控之火，在某些重历史中，不列颠蒙后者恩赐获得了超乎想象的力量。一个名叫萨缪尔·帕特里吉记录下了他们的事迹。
en: The Irreproachable Traditions of the Society of the Noble Endeavour. The members of the Society of the Noble Endeavour adopted noble names at their initiation: 'Parsival', 'Roland', 'Oisín'. They drew

zh: 《高贵之举社团那无可指摘的传承》。拉丁语著。
en: The Irreproachable Traditions of the Society of the Noble Endeavour. In Latin.

zh: 戈特洛布·雅宁斯伯爵是大陆上一个医师和决斗者协会的代表。他向你提出了委托。
en: Count Gottlob Jannings represents a Continental confraternity of physicians and duellists. He has offered you a commission.

zh: 戈特洛布·雅宁斯伯爵是大陆上一个医师和决斗者协会的代表。眼下他出国去了，但很快便会回来。
en: Count Gottlob Jannings represents a Continental confraternity of physicians and duellists. Just now, he's out of the country, but he'll be back soon.

zh: 戈特洛布·雅宁斯伯爵是大陆上一个医师和决斗者协会的代表。他正需要有人协助。
en: Count Gottlob Jannings represents a Continental confraternity of physicians and duellists. He could use your assistance.

zh: 一种以生命换取金钱的方式。
en: An arrangement to exchange one's life for money.

zh: “不会再有具名者。到此为止了；抱歉；我没时间和人谈话。”
en: 'No more Names. That's it; I'm sorry; I don't have time to make conversation.'

zh: 他的势力让人望而生畏；他的身份，暂时不为人知。
en: Their power is formidable; their identity, as yet unknown.

zh: 一堆旧衣服与没什么价值的首饰。或许它们能在哪换得几个便士，但对我没有什么用处。
en: Old clothes, near-worthless accessories. It might pick up a few pennies somewhere, but it's of little use to me.

zh: 猎人将变作死尸。
en: Hunters will become corpses.

zh: 召唤物变作调味影响或阻碍召唤之人。
en: Summonings will be replaced by flavour influences or summon blockers

zh: 凡人将变作死尸
en: Mortals will be replaced by corpses

zh: 一本插图华美的大书，内含数篇关联松散的儿童故事，由署名N·K·菲尔德之人收集成册。里面没有几个故事适合儿童阅读。
en: A lusciously illustrated tome of loosely connected children's stories, collected by one N.K. Field. Few of them are remotely suitable for children.

zh: 特蕾莎·加尔米耶的“笔友”，苏洛恰那的“姐妹”，白日之钥的持有者，正从维也纳前来。也许我应该在车站见见她。
en: Teresa Galmier's 'correspondent', Sulochana's 'sister', the holder of the Key of Days, is on her way here from Vienna. Perhaps I should meet her at the station.

zh: 一个精神矍铄，魄力十足的女人，衣着黑白分明，脸上满布皱纹。没人见过她眨眼。一只小小的黑毛狗崽从她裙底探出头来。
en: A spry,forceful woman in severe black and white, tightly wrinkled. She is never observed to blink. A tiny black puppy peeps from her skirts.

zh: 启不允许封闭和孤立存在。它欢快地将我们推出无知的庇护。
en: The Knock permits no seal and no isolation. It thrusts us gleefully out of the safety of ignorance.

zh: 作者卡洛斯·加西亚·绍里，比利牛斯山中痴狂的神秘主义者，以会用三种语言背诵本文而闻名，尽管他从未造访过那片次大陆。
en: Carlos Garcia Sauri, the ecstatic mystic of the Pyrenees, was known to recite this in three languages, though he had never visited the Subcontinent.

zh: 作者卡洛斯·加西亚·绍里，比利牛斯山中痴狂的神秘主义者，以会用三种语言背诵本文而闻名，尽管他从未造访过那片次大陆。
en: Carlos Garcia Sauri, the ecstatic mystic of the Pyrenees, was known to recite this in three languages, though he had never visited the Subcontinent.

zh: 莱德劳是一名补锅匠兼机修工，身负不可思议的绝活。
en: Laidlaw is a tinkerer and mechanic with an uncanny knack.

zh: 莱德劳已经开始明白手再巧也有极限，而牺牲能带来力量。
en: Laidlaw has begun to understand the limitations of ingenuity, and the power of sacrifice.

zh: 莱德劳已学会了更巧妙地使用火与铜，蜡与酸。
en: Laidlaw has learnt the cleverer uses of fire and copper, wax and acid.

zh: 莱德劳能将破损之物塑造成必需之物。
en: Laidlaw can shape what is broken into what is necessary.

zh: 莱德劳的门是闩上的而不是锁上的。我们知道上锁没用。
en: Laidlaw's door is bolted, not locked. We know better than that.

zh: 莱德劳的计策如蕨叶之影般错综复杂。
en: Laidlaw's schemes are intricate as fern-shadow.

zh: 莱德劳的计策如蕨叶之影般错综复杂。
en: Laidlaw's schemes are intricate as fern-shadow.

zh: 莱德劳的计策如蕨叶之影般错综复杂。
en: Laidlaw's schemes are intricate as fern-shadow.

zh: “生命是束纯净的火焰，我们内心都拥有一颗无形的太阳。”——托马斯·布朗。
en: 'Life is a pure flame, and we live by an invisible Sun within us.' - Thomas Browne.

zh: 克里斯托弗·拉奎伯斯声称自己从马尔马拉海岸边一个女巫家庭买到了两本本书的手抄复制品。第三本“献给了大海”。
en: Christoph Larquebus claimed to have purchased two hand-written copies of the Codex from a family of witches on the shores of the Sea of Marmara. A third was 'given to the Sea'.

zh: 克里斯托弗·拉奎伯斯声称自己从马尔马拉海岸边一个女巫家庭买到了两本本书的手抄复制品。第三本“献给了大海”。
en: Christoph Larquebus claimed to have purchased two hand-written copies of the Codex, in the supposedly-extinct Phrygian tongue, from a family of witches on the shores of the Sea of Marmara. A third was

zh: 出于兴趣，可以涉猎写作，也可以涉猎绘画，但生活，毕竟靠的还是稳定的津贴。
en: One dabbles with writing and one dabbles with painting but one lives, above all, in the certainty of one's allowance.

zh: 出于兴趣，可以涉猎写作，也可以涉猎绘画，但生活，毕竟靠的还是相对稳定的津贴。
en: One dabbles with writing and one dabbles with painting but one lives, above all, in the near-certainty of one's allowance.

zh: 我在欢腾剧院中的舞蹈队任职。在我的好运，抑或美貌，消逝之前，这个职位都将归我所有……又除非我找到了更好的活儿。
en: I have a position in the chorus at the Gaiety. It's mine until my luck or my looks run out... or until I find something better.

zh: 我错过了太多的演出。如果我够真情实意地恳求，也许还能挽回工作。
en: I've missed too many performances. If I plead convincingly enough, I might get my job back.

zh: 一份有关神秘的#PREVIOUSCHARACTERNAME#的档案文件。我亲自将其加封。我对它的内容再熟悉不过了。如果我再去读它，很可能又会做梦。
en: A file on the enigmatic #PREVIOUSCHARACTERNAME#. I sealed it myself. I am all too familiar with its contents. If I were to revisit them, the dreams might begin again.

zh: 证据中包含了让人不禁舔嘴唇的那种细节，如果那人这样的事物感兴趣的话。我不应该读他。那些梦境也许会重现。
en: The evidence includes the kind of details that might make one lick one's lips, if one were interested in such things. I should not read it. The dreams might begin again.

zh: 快烧光了！也许我能把它火里捡出来——
en: It's almost gone! I could pluck it from the flames -

zh: 我是首都警察局的高层职员。
en: I have a senior position in the capital's police force.

zh: 我丢掉了职位，我不太可能重新回去上班了。我需要在这个文明的世界里自谋出路。
en: I've lost my position. I'm unlikely to regain it. I must find my own way in the civilian world.

zh: 我是首都警察局的高层职员，但我被临时调派到了防剿局。
en: I have a senior position in the capital's police force, although I have been seconded to the Suppression Bureau.

zh: 我旷工的事情被注意到了。我已因此被停职。
en: My absence has been noted. I've been suspended.

zh: 这本日记详细记录了名为#PREVIOUSCHARACTERNAME#之人越陷越深的痴迷。
en: This details a growing obsession with an individual by the name of #PREVIOUSCHARACTERNAME#.

zh: 今天，我将回到工作岗位。
en: Today I return to work.

zh: 我亲眼见证了#PREVIOUSCHARACTERNAME#被病魔压垮的可怖过程。我手写的病历也越来越潦草了。
en: My own eyewitness account of the ghastly decline of #PREVIOUSCHARACTERNAME#. My handwriting deteriorates as the account progresses.

zh: 我是首都警察局的高层职员。
en: I have a senior position in the capital's police force.

zh: 里奥是个真诚的人，有着对密教的兴趣。他对你所暗示的无形之术很在意。
en: Leo is an earnest sort with an interest in the occult. He hangs on your hints about the invisible arts.

zh: 里奥是个真诚的人，但他也准备好去做必要之事。
en: Leo is an earnest sort, but he's prepared to do what he must.

zh: 里奥仍然渴盼使美丽的、有价值的事物存续。但他也明白为了保护理想，人有时必须去做不光彩的事情。
en: Leo still yearns to preserve what is good and valuable; but he understands that to protect one's ideals, one must sometimes do questionable things.

zh: 里奥会不惜一切代价保持存续。他不会停歇。
en: Leo will preserve, at all costs. He will not cease.

zh: 里奥不明白他做错了什么。
en: Leo doesn't understand what he did wrong.

zh: 里奥已选定了他的事业，他将采取一切必要措施使它存续。
en: Leo has chosen his cause, and he will do what is necessary to preserve it.

zh: 里奥已选定了他的事业，他将采取一切必要措施使它存续。
en: Leo has chosen his cause, and he will do what is necessary to preserve it.

zh: 里奥已选定了他的事业，他将采取一切必要措施使它存续。
en: Leo has chosen his cause, and he will do what is necessary to preserve it.

zh: 在《狮子密续》最为臭名昭著的译本中，阿什利·菲利普斯被列为翻译者，但书的序言明确否定译者的存在。这种令人困惑之处正是提及兜颈贵胄的书籍的特征。
en: In the most notorious translation of the Leonine Tantra, Ashley Phillips is listed as the translator, but their existence is explicitly denied in the prologue. This confusion is characteristic of book

zh: 在《狮子密续》最为臭名昭著的译本中，阿什利·菲利普斯被列为翻译者，但书的序言明确否定译者的存在。这种令人困惑之处正是提及兜颈贵胄的书籍的特征。
en: In the most notorious translation of the Leonine Tantra, Ashley Phillips is listed as the translator, but their existence is explicitly denied in the prologue. This confusion is characteristic of book

zh: 内藏各色小部头书籍，残章及零散纸品，或可有助研究。
en: A collection of minor volumes, partial copies and ephemera that may help with research.

zh: 证件上的名字不完全是我的，但也差不多。
en: The name on the licence isn't exactly mine, but it's close enough.

zh: 证件上的名字不完全是我的，但也差不多。
en: The name on the licence isn't exactly mine, but it's close enough.

zh: 来自他人生命的岁月：购买，偷窃或勒索而来。
en: Years from the lives of others: bought, stolen or extorted.

zh: 我不知道丽姬亚俱乐部到底是什么；但是所有的清算人都知道它的成员比我们的任何同伙都更年长，更饥渴。我们已经学会了避开她们。
en: I don't know precisely what the Ligeia Club is; but all reckoners do know that its members are older and hungrier than any of our mobs. We've learnt to avoid them.

zh: 亚琛大教堂旁边有一座小礼拜堂，纪念的是一位与波斯人作战的勇士圣徒。 在特定的日子里，会有一位助祭锁上门，为老兵举行异端仪式。
en: There's a side chapel in Aachen cathedral to a warrior saint who fought against the Persians. On certain days, a deacon will lock the door and perform unorthodox ceremonies for veterans.

zh: 在一块突出的岩石下，一座古老的铸铁雕像伫立在粉刷成白色的避难所中。它的脸已伤痕累累，无法辨认，但一只手仍然握着长矛。
en: Beneath a rocky overhang, an ancient cast-iron statue stands in a whitewashed shelter. Its face has been scarred to unrecognisability, but one hand still holds a spear.

zh: 出乎意料的是，这里居然有一座有着漆过的门和彩色玻璃窗的佛寺。但油漆已经剥落，大部分窗户也都被木板封住了。某一队红军已经占据了此处——据说是为了储藏物资，但我们这些知道暗号的人则被允许加入在佛陀那张满是伤疤的面孔前举行的严肃仪式。
en: Unexpectedly, here's a Buddhist temple with painted doors and stained glass windows. But the paint is peeling and most of the windows have been boarded up. A certain Red Army battalion commander has t

zh: 在国家歌剧院最深处的地窖里，立着一尊用黑森林橡木雕刻而成的严肃雕像。六十年前，天鹅王为他最喜爱的作曲家的新歌剧举行了盛大的首演。这场演出进行得并不顺利。舞台上的装饰被抛弃并遗忘在这里。现在，这尊雕像的眼睛已经被划满了疤痕以使他盲目，而那些知道此处力量的人在它面前践行他们严苛的信仰。
en: In the deepest cellar of the Nationaltheater stands a stern image carved of Black Forest oak. Sixty years ago the Swan King staged a grandiose première of his favourite composer's new opera. It didn't

zh: 在小镇边缘一间被烧毁的狩猎小屋里，烧焦的骨头被雕刻成棋子，壁炉的石头上刻着一块棋盘。
en: In a burnt-out hunting lodge at the edge of town, charred bone has been carved into chess-pieces, and a chess-board scored in the stone of the hearth.

zh: 一处废弃的谷仓，占据这里的秘密教团崇拜着一位古怪的英雄：那个独臂的、曾经单独一人驾着一辆坦克为俄国人攻陷了这座城市的英国人。也许他现在是个具名者。也许他只是死了。但那些崇敬上校的人都记得他。
en: An abandoned grain silo has been adopted by a secret cult honouring an odd hero: the one-armed Briton who stormed the city for the White Russians, alone in a single tank. Perhaps he is now a Name. Per

zh: 马耳他是地中海的疗养院，士兵们来到这里接受治疗，而治疗师中有一些人会实施无形之术。他们在医院中紧锁的房间里设置神龛，用以拜请治疗之司辰。没有人向上校祈祷治愈。只有在死亡将会是一种仁慈的时候，他们才会到屋檐下这个正在腐烂的房间里来拜请他。
en: Malta is the Nurse of the Mediterranean, where soldiers come to be healed, and there are those among the healers who practice the invisible arts. They keep shrines in locked rooms in their hospitals t

zh: 此处的“铁砧”是一处供奉神牛阿匹斯的青铜祭坛。青铜并不是用来做铁砧的好料子，但祭司们说，在这个铁砧上锻造的唯一材料是人类的灵魂。
en: The 'anvil' here is a bronze altar of Apis, the Bull. Bronze is not a good metal for anvils, but the only material forged on this one is, the priests say, the human spirit.

zh: 有时候剑齿兽会变得贪婪，开始吃他们无法在土地中找到的东西，然后退化成与人类相似的样貌。我从惨痛的教训中认识到，要杀死一只仍保持着全部原有力量的剑齿兽要困难得多。但曾有一只剑齿兽在此地战死，这神祠供奉的就是它的尸骨。
en: Sometimes a skaptodon becomes greedy, starts eating things they don't find in the earth, and degenerates to something like human shape. I learnt the hard way that it's much more difficult to kill a sk

zh: 隐士纳维尔大部分时间都待在这里，虽然他否认自己为狮子匠效力。“能战胜自身的狮子才是最伟大的，” 他热心地坚持道。
en: The marabout Navil spends much of his time here, although he denies being in the service of the Lionsmith. 'The greatest lion is the lion who has overcome himself,' he helpfully insists.

zh: 我的诗人朋友给我讲了这个地方的故事：在天空还是一块由最为坚硬的石头磨光而成的屋顶的时候，狮子匠用他的锤子击打了它，这块巨石便由此落到了地上。在我看来，它像是一块铁镍陨石，但是这并不意味着她的故事是虚构的。这也是我尽量不去对诸史过分深究的原因。
en: My poet friend told me the story of this place: that in the days when the sky was a polished roof of the hardest stone, the Lionsmith struck it with his hammer so that this great rock would fall to ea

zh: 执政党将教众从这座火之庙宇中驱逐了出去。现在，一小批军事工程师在这里工作。但是他们投入了相当大的精力来维护为永恒之火提供燃料的煤气管道，而且他们的活动非常古怪。
en: The Party drove the worshippers out of this fire temple. Now a small cadre of military engineers operates here. But they've put considerable effort into maintaining the gas pipes that fuel the eternal

zh: 巴巴里人更为世所知的称呼是柏柏尔人，他们尊奉安泰俄斯为英雄王。当然，大地之子、反叛者安泰俄斯，一直是狮子匠的劲敌。但是狮子匠向来尊重他的劲敌——至少安泰俄斯的门徒们是这样说的。
en: The Imazighen - the people the world knows as the Berbers - venerate Antaios as a hero-king. Of course Antaios the Opponent, the Son of Earth, was a great enemy of the Lionsmith, but the Lionsmith has

zh: 圣马可骑士团在大约一个世纪之前正式解散，但是这也没有阻止其中信仰坚定的成员在暗中会面，继续进行那些基督教色彩不那么强烈的仪式，并为无形之术的施展者有偿服务。他们的礼拜堂只向他们足够喜欢的人开放，而我们一直相处得很好。
en: The chivalric Ordine di San Marco was formally dissolved a century or so ago, but that didn't stop their more determined members from meeting in secret, continuing their less Christian rituals, and se

zh: 也许蛇亲吻过我，也许刀子啜饮过我，也许是我把梦做得太锋利。
en: Perhaps a serpent has kissed me, perhaps a knife has drunk of me, perhaps I dreamt too sharp a dream.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将在利刃的触摸下开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open beneath a blade's touch.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将为炙铁的触摸而开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open to the touch of hot iron.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将为热吻开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open to a fervent kiss.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将在鼓响时开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open at the beat of the drum.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将在曙光下开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open at the flush of dawn.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将对剪子起反应。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will answer to the scissors.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将在最后开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open last.

zh: 长生者有可能死去吗？让我们来试试。
en: Is it possible for Long to die? Let's put it to the test.

zh: 长生者将变作其留下的奖赏
en: Long will be replaced by their rewards

zh: 过于强烈的感受攫夺了此人的心智。他不久便会消失，永远离开人们的视线。
en: Some overwhelming experience has robbed this individual of their senses.

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 不受欢迎的东西。
en: Something unwelcome.

zh: 此灵体正在进入现世。这比分娩要容易，但不像穿过一扇门那么容易。
en: The spirit is entering the world. This is simpler than a birth, but not so simple as passing through a door.

zh: 1782年，阿纳尔·维迪尔以本书为据在巴黎科学院进行了宣讲。他于次日清晨被不经审判直接处决。
en: In 1782, Anaël Verdier used this text as the basis for a presentation to the Academy of Sciences in Paris. He was executed in secret the following morning without trial.

zh: 一本仿炼金术文风的文献，伐诃语著。
en: A quasi-alchemical text, in Vak.

zh: 作者自称杰卡杜，为圣河流域的兜颈贵胄之一。文风并不正式，甚至可说像闲聊。
en: The author, who identifies itself as the Jekadu, claims to be one of the Great Hooded Princes of the Land of the River. The style is informal, even chatty.

zh: 作者自称杰卡杜，为圣河流域的兜颈贵胄之一。
en: The author, who identifies itself as the Jekadu, claims to be one of the Great Hooded Princes of the Land of the River.

zh: 苏洛恰那的“姐妹”，对她人们通常不称名字，只称“操线仙娥”。她很快便会抵达，在黑夜中。
en: Sulochana's 'sister', who is not usually named save as 'Our Lady of Wires'. She'll be here, soon, by night.

zh: 它散发着腐败血液的恶臭，每一说话下颚骨便咔嚓作响。蜷缩在这层衣服和骨骸下的也许是个女人，又也许是某种操线木偶。
en: It reeks of rotten blood, and it clacks its jaw each time it speaks. Perhaps there is a woman, huddled beneath the cloth and the bones. Perhaps it's some puppeteer's trick.

zh: 一本对密教爱情的记录。一个名叫“亚伦之子帕拉”的人宣称他在一昼一夜间写出了本书，作作为加入“遗忘家族”大马士革分部的投名状的一部分——该教团戒律森严，由抛弃前尘后事的不死者组成。
en: An account of an occult romance. A certain Para, son of Aaron, claims to have written this in a single day and a single night as part of his initiation into the Damascene branch of the House of Lethe

zh: 书中的图解和插图在数世纪后依然优美精致，催人困意，偶尔还催人肉欲。亚兰语著。
en: The diagrams and illustrations, even across the centuries, are elegant, hypnotic, occasionally erotic. In Aramaic.

zh: 一本对密教爱情的记录。一个名叫“梅克”的人宣称他在一夜间写出了本书，作为加入“遗忘家族”底比斯分部的投名状的一部分——该教团由以水的功用掩盖自己行迹的不死者组成。
en: An account of an occult romance. A certain Mek claims to have written this in a single night as part of his initiation into the Theban branch of the House of Lethe - that order of immortals who used t

zh: 书中的图解和插图在数世纪后依然优美精致，催人困意，偶尔还催人肉欲。亚兰语著。
en: The diagrams and illustrations, even across the centuries, are elegant, hypnotic, occasionally erotic. In Aramaic.

zh: 一本对密教爱情的记录。一个名叫“伊亚沃苟斯”的人宣称他在一夜间写出了本书，作为加入“遗忘家族”阿勒颇分部的投名状的一部分——该教团由不献身于任何司辰的不死者组成。
en: An account of an occult romance. A certain Iyavogos claims to have written this in a single night as part of his initiation into the Aleppine branch of the House of Lethe - that order of immortals who

zh: 书中的图解和插图在数世纪后依然优美精致，催人困意，偶尔还催人肉欲。亚兰语著。
en: The diagrams and illustrations, even across the centuries, are elegant, hypnotic, occasionally erotic. In Aramaic.

zh: 描写了名为七蟠的司辰，又称“母亲之父”的死亡。
en: On the death of the Hour called Seven-Coils, 'the Father of the Mother'.

zh: 描写了名为七蟠的司辰，又称“母亲之父”的死亡。
en: On the death of the Hour called Seven-Coils, 'the Father of the Mother'.

zh: 某个并非其制造者所希冀之物的遗体。
en: The remains of someone who was not what their maker intended.

zh: 这是我在尘世的第一件衣服。
en: This was my first garment in the world of air.

zh: 一开始我觉得它们余温尚存。过了一会儿，我才意识到它们总会保持温暖。
en: At first I think they're still warm. After a little while, I realise they'll always be warm.

zh: 在一个充满力量与分裂的时间和地点，这支香烟曾经与一位叛变的启之长生者的手指与嘴唇十分熟稔。而至少在一重历史中，他吸完了它。
en: Once, at a place and time of power and division, this knew the hands and mouth of a renegade Knock-Long. In at least one History, he finished smoking it.

zh: 一份礼物，来自某个不复从前之人。
en: A gift from someone who is not as he was.

zh: 有一只轮子，只在月亮下转动，而它从前在太阳下转动。但是为何只在一者下？为何只在两者下？
en: There is a Wheel that only turns beneath the Moon, where once it turned beneath the Sun. But why only one? Why only two?

zh: 愤怒仍在继续，即便其缘由早已消逝。这是对早在铁被人驯服前的一桩错事的回忆。
en: Long after the cause for rage has passed, the rage endures. Here is a memory of a wrong done before ever iron was tamed.

zh: 大地的一切宝藏都于火中揭示。
en: In fire are all the treasures of the earth revealed.

zh: 从前在我们行走于林间路时，我们背生翅膀，眼含警觉，那时我们也不需要花蜜。
en: When we walked in the ways of the wood we were winged and watchful, and we had no need of nectar.

zh: 太阳原曾更加温暖——非是更加明亮，但那时它的碰触是一种仁慈。
en: The Sun was warmer once - no brighter, but its touch was a kind of mercy.

zh: 愉悦不能长久持续，但有时候，它持续的比我们要长久。
en: Delight does not endure for long, but sometimes, it endures longer than do we.

zh: “我们每一个人都有在某一刻世事永久改变了的回忆，虽然当时我们不可能自知。也许世界也有这样的回忆。”
en: 'We each of us have a memory of a moment when things changed forever, though at the time we never knew it. Perhaps the world also has those memories.' [Paint with this Memory to bring it to full aware

zh: 欢愉一如大海，源远而流长；欢愉一如大海，其存在先于人类的意识；但亦如大海，欢愉在每个时代、每个时辰不尽相同。
en: Pleasure, like the sea, is ancient; pleasure, like the sea, precedes the conscious human soul; but like the sea, pleasure is different in every age and every hour. [Paint with this Memory to bring it

zh: 我们铭记死者，直至将其遗忘。
en: We remember the dead, until we forget them.

zh: 梦境的大仁之处在于它们不常侵扰醒时的生活。
en: The great mercy of dreams is that they do not often intrude into waking life.

zh: 一道带来商机的灵光；一个微小的机会；人行道上一张印着画儿的五磅纸钞。
en: A ray of commercial light; a miniature opportunity; a figurative five-pound note on the pavement.

zh: 不是所有的私人娱乐都适合公开讨论。
en: Not all private pleasures are suitable for public discussion.

zh: 太阳原曾更加明亮——非是更加温暖，但那时它的光含有我们后再无从得见的色彩。
en: The Sun was brighter once - no warmer, but its light held colours we no longer see.

zh: 每一桩人生都有其令人意想不到之隅。
en: Every life has its impossible corners.

zh: 有时我们记得失去的东西，即使我们忘记了它已不在。
en: Sometimes we remember what was lost even when we have forgotten that it is gone.

zh: 我记得一段苦乐交织的时光。
en: I could recall a complex time.

zh: 作者是一个无名的高卢人学徒“坟茔之子”，属于名为利米亚教团的组织。此人带着遗憾之情讲述了先于人类的司辰——石源诸神的毁灭。
en: 'The Barrowchild', a nameless Gaulish adept who joined an organisation identified as the Ordo Limiae, writes regretfully of the destruction of the gods-who-were-stone - the Hours who preceded humanity

zh: 作者是“坟茔之子”，一位无名的高卢人学徒，曾加入名为河泽教团的组织。在书中，作者带着遗憾之意记述了在人类存在之前的时枢神，石源诸神的毁灭。
en: A work by 'the Barrowchild', a nameless Gaulish adept who joined an organisation known as the Ordo Limiae.

zh: 我记得一段更为快乐的时光。
en: I could recall a happier time.

zh: 我们的秘密统治者，握有我们忠诚之人，我们会用我们的作为将他托得更高。
en: Our secret ruler, who commands our loyalty, who shall rise still higher through our works.

zh: 该猎人能将他发现的所有邪名成功升作证据。
en: This Hunter always succeeds at creating Evidence from Notoriety.

zh: 这是一个难关，但这种程度的刃可以解决它。
en: There is a complication, but this much Edge would resolve it.

zh: 这是一个难关，但这种程度的铸可以解决它。
en: There is a complication, but this much Forge would resolve it.

zh: 这是一个难关，但这种程度的杯可以解决它。
en: There is a complication, but this much Grail would resolve it.

zh: 这是一个难关，但这种程度的心可以解决它。
en: There is a complication, but this much Heart would resolve it.

zh: 这是一个难关，但这种程度的启可以解决它。
en: There is a complication, but this much Knock would resolve it.

zh: 这是一个难关，但这种程度的灯可以解决它。
en: There is a complication, but this much Lantern would resolve it.

zh: 这是一个难关，但这种程度的蛾可以解决它。
en: There is a complication, but this much Moth would resolve it.

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 这是一个难关，但这种程度的冬可以解决它。
en: There is a complication, but this much Winter would resolve it.

zh: 到头来，这并不是你要寻找的人。他无足轻重，不会让你留下任何印象。或许已经没有什么人值得你去见了。
en: This isn't who you hoped for, after all. They're no-one of particular importance, and eventually they'll pass from your memory. Perhaps there is no-one else available that you'd care to meet.

zh: 绳结姐妹会的一位女祭司在本书中宣称自己领悟了轰雷之皮降生的真意。
en: In which a priestess of the Sisterhood of the Knot claims to have discerned the true meaning of the Thunderskin's birth.

zh: 绳结姐妹会的文献，由埃文·墨菲发掘。
en: A text of the Sisterhood of the Knot, unearthed by Evan Morphy.

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: “我们如此习惯活着，以至于不愿意死亡。”——托马斯·布朗
en: 'The long habit of living indisposeth us for dying.' - Thomas Browne

zh: 我知道有一个人会将蛾子捉来关在玻璃罩下。然后在如今夜一般的夜晚，将它们逐个放出，令其死于火烛。
en: I knew a man who captured moths in a bell-jar. On nights like this, he would release them one by one to die in the candle.

zh: 无花果、坚果、麦芽。
en: Figs, nuts, malt.

zh: Recipe aspect
en: Recipe aspect

zh: 直接对抗总是不明智的。但万不得已时，若不得不战，我将陷入高度的危险。
en: Direct confrontation is always unwise. But in the last resort, if it comes to a struggle, I am very dangerous.

zh: 我掌握了我的猎物所进行活动的某些证据。
en: I have some evidence of my quarry's activities.

zh: 我掌握了某些令人信服的证据，直指我的猎物进行的某些活动。
en: I have compelling evidence of my quarry's activities.

zh: 该猎人通常能在漫宿生物的攻击下逃出一命，即便此类攻击对其他人而言是不可阻挡的。
en: This Hunter will usually survive attacks by creatures of the Mansus, even if those attacks would otherwise be unstoppable.

zh: 离奇之感残留不去。
en: Uncanniness lingers.

zh: 一幅画作，画中肤色深暗的女人拥有雪色的双眸和剪得很短的骨白色头发，站在一个长满白花藤的小型大理石墓前。署名为“乔纳斯·B·蒙克，作于1811年。”
en: A painting of a dark-skinned woman with snow-coloured eyes and close-cropped ivory-coloured hair. She stands before a small marble tomb overgrown with star jasmine. The painting is signed 'Jonas B. Mu

zh: 纳塔丽娅从不不加质疑地追从他人——但现下她追从于我。
en: Natalia never follows unquestioningly - but for now, she follows.

zh: 纳塔丽娅踏上了她能找到的最光明的路途。
en: Natalia has aligned herself with the brightest path she can find.

zh: 纳塔丽娅已经知道了深藏已久的真相。于是她调整了她的日程安排。
en: Natalia has learnt truths that were too long hidden. In consequence, she has adjusted her agenda.

zh: 纳塔丽娅已经知道了如此之多，付出了如此之大的代价。如今她不会收手。
en: Natalia has learnt so much, and at such cost. She won't stop now.

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 内维尔是一个在开口之前犹犹豫豫，容易退缩的人。他会在他认为你在开玩笑的时候配合着笑。
en: Neville pauses before he speaks, and flinches easily. He laughs whenever he thinks you've made a joke.

zh: 内维尔是一个在开口之前犹犹豫豫，容易退缩的人。他能够敏感地体察到更深层的问题。
en: Neville pauses before he speaks, and flinches easily. He's sensitive to deeper matters.

zh: 内维尔的目光变得鬼鬼祟祟，他的声音也时不时听起来尖锐刺耳。他现在毫无保留地地吐露他的梦想。
en: Neville's gaze has grown furtive, and his voice is sometimes shrill. He speaks freely of his dreams.

zh: 在梦中，内维尔看见那些能打开世界与其道路的破绽。
en: In his dreams, Neville sees the flaws where the world and its ways can be opened.

zh: 内维尔在流泪。
en: Neville is weeping.

zh: 内维尔知道他这么做不能久长。但只要他还能，上神保佑，他就会打开出现在面前的一切门扉。
en: Neville knows this can't last. But while it does, by God, he will open whatever doors present themselves.

zh: 内维尔知道他这么做不能久长。但只要他还能，上神保佑，他就会打开出现在面前的一切门扉。
en: Neville knows this can't last. But while it does, by God, he will open whatever doors present themselves.

zh: 内维尔知道他这么做不能久长。但只要他还能，上神保佑，他就会打开出现在面前的一切门扉。
en: Neville knows this can't last. But while it does, by God, he will open whatever doors present themselves.

zh: 事件效果
en: Recipe Effect

zh: 有些行为会长久留存于人们的记忆中。
en: Certain acts are not quickly forgotten.

zh: 这可能是对抗清算人首领的唯一防御。它也可能压根就算不上是种防御。
en: This might be the only defence against a reckoner lord. It might be no defence at all.

zh: 不太显眼，也不过于偏僻。
en: Not too obvious, and not too far out of the way.

zh: 本书大概是翻成英语的“那迦大蛇的预言”，其插图由“威廉·米德奈特”在1880年代绘制于巴黎；但本书与该预言又显出多处不同。以伐诃语文献的标准来看，本书的年代显然是很近的。
en: This was probably the 'Prophecies of the Naga' rendered into English, and illustrated, by 'William Midnight' in Paris in the 1880s; but it shows a number of divergences from that text. It's certainly

zh: 本书大概是翻成英语的“那迦大蛇的预言”，其插图由“威廉·米德奈特”在1880年代绘制于巴黎；但本书与该预言又显出多处不同。以伐诃语文献的标准来看，本书的年代显然是很近的。
en: This was probably the 'Prophecies of the Naga' rendered into English, and illustrated, by 'William Midnight' in Paris in the 1880s; but it shows a number of divergences from that text. It's certainly

zh: 所罗门·赫舍著，书的内容也许能解读为关于冬季的寓言，以及它与太阳漫长的，缓缓迎来毁灭的罗曼史。本书的引言是“午时日落”。
