en: Solomon Husher writes, perhaps allegorically, of Winter, and its long slow doomed romance with the Sun. The epigraph is 'Sunset at Noon'.

zh: 一部可怕的动物寓言集，讲述了现于镜面的恐怖。由三世纪署名“牧人者”所著，这本是近年新出的版本。
en: A dire bestiary of the horrors to be found in mirrors. This is a recent edition of a third century text by 'Poemander'.

zh: 署名“牧人者”所著的动物寓言集。希腊语著。
en: A bestiary by 'Poemander'. In Greek.

zh: 我发现了一个地方，那儿到处都是兴奋的猫。
en: I've found a place bristling with excitable cats.

zh: 寒霜行于此地。
en: Frost walks here.

zh: 慈善能买来清白。
en: Charity brings clarity.

zh: 更多的朋友总是于我有利的。
en: I could always use more friends.

zh: 这可能会产生些后果。但我想我也不打算在这里待太久了。
en: This might have consequences. But I'm probably not planning to stick around.

zh: 一个认识真正重要的人的机会。
en: A chance to get acquainted with someone of real importance.

zh: 我在这里混得很熟了，而如果你在某个地方混得很熟的话，你总会认识一些“专业人士”。事情就是这样。
en: I've been around, and when you get around, you get to know a certain kind of professional. That's just how it is.

zh: 如果有足够多的人知道它的存在，它就会成为违禁品。
en: This would be illegal, if enough people knew it existed.

zh: 又黏又甜的东西。
en: Something viscous and sweet.

zh: “真的，”商家向我保证，“和真东西一模一样。”
en: 'Authentic,' I'm assured, 'just like the real thing.'

zh: 这种东西是我不去教堂的原因之一。
en: This kind of thing is one reason I don't go to church.

zh: 一大把被胡乱糟蹋过的书页。
en: A fistful of badly mistreated pages.

zh: 一大把被胡乱糟蹋过的书页。
en: A fistful of badly mistreated pages.

zh: 一大把被胡乱糟蹋过的书页。
en: A fistful of badly mistreated pages.

zh: “它会回来的，”卖家沮丧地告诉我，“在那之前你要尽量利用它。”
en: 'It'll be back,' the seller informs me, gloomily. 'Do what you can with it in the meantime.'

zh: 世界净洁之时，日出。
en: Sunrise, when the world is clean.

zh: 在这沙漠的边缘地带，厄客德娜有时已不再费心让自己保持人形。运气好的话，我就永远不会知道她更偏好什么样的形态。
en: Here at the desert's edge, Echidna doesn't always bother with human form. With luck, I'll never see what shape she prefers.

zh: 我经过了一个宗教场所。
en: I've passed a place of worship.

zh: 空气像裸露的火炉一样闪烁着微光。蛇出没的天气。
en: The air shimmers like a naked furnace. Serpents' weather.

zh: 高处。不要往下看。
en: A high place. Don't look down.

zh: 山楂的香味，夕阳被阴影一分为二的色彩。双角利斧在这里很强大。
en: The smell of hawthorn, and the colour of sunset bisected with shadow. The Horned Axe is strong here.

zh: 据我所知，早在围城之前，施鲁塞尔女士就在这儿住了很久。只要不引起她的注意，我或许就是安全的。
en: I know that Frau Schlüssel has lived here since long before the siege. If I don't call her attention, perhaps I'll be safe.

zh: 某些货品的交易通常受到限制；但我现在有朋友了。
en: Certain goods are normally restricted; but I have friends now.

zh: 我可以利用这个机会，以极快的速度取得行医的资格。
en: I could take this opportunity to qualify, extremely rapidly, as a doctor.

zh: 无论“操线仙娥”究竟是什么，今年它都选中了基辅作为它的猎场。
en: Whatever 'Our Lady of Wires' might be, it has chosen Kyiv for its feeding-grounds this year.

zh: 未实现的潜力。
en: Unrealised potential.

zh: 在这个城市里，没有一座雕像是睁着眼睛的。这是为什么？
en: In all of this city, there is not one statue whose eyes are open. Why is that?

zh: 教众渴望更多的东西。
en: The congregation are hungry for something more.

zh: 平旦夫人的游艇蓝宝石号在此停泊。传闻平旦夫人极其危险。
en: Mme Matutine's yacht, the Sapphire, lies at anchor here. Mme Matutine has a lethal reputation.

zh: 夜在凝视，而我再次失眠。
en: The deep watches of the night, and again I can't sleep.

zh: 我可以租个安静的地方工作。
en: I can lease a quiet place to work.

zh: 我听说，他们是在一座熔炉里找到它的。
en: They found it in a furnace, I'm told.

zh: “上帝造水，而人类造酒。”——维克多·雨果。
en: 'God made only water, but man made wine' - Victor Hugo.

zh: 某个音乐家陷入了困境。卖掉这件宝物会让她伤心，但是人总得吃饭。
en: A certain musician has fallen on hard times. It will break her heart to part with this treasure, but a woman must eat.

zh: 总会有路的。
en: There's always a way.

zh: 一扇开向“非”的窗户。
en: A window on to what wasn't.

zh: 我听说那位艺术家在完成它的前一晚就去世了。
en: I'm told the artist died the night before he completed it.

zh: 我们身处海边，就是这里，海洋献出了它的礼物：安宁，或是死亡，或是逃脱。
en: We're by the sea, here, and the sea bears gifts: peace, or death, or escape.

zh: 苏洛恰那·阿摩伐舍隐于蜕衣俱乐部，但这周她在城里四处活动。我应该避开她。
en: Sulochana Amavasya lairs at the Ecdysis Club, but she's abroad in the city this week. I should avoid her.

zh: 灯火渐明之时，日落。
en: Sunset, when the light is richer.

zh: 我可以获得房产。
en: I can acquire property. [A Townhouse provides significant Space. As a good place to take refuge or entertain guests, it provides both Heart and Grail. If you plan to retire here, it will also provide

zh: 我可以获得房产。
en: I can acquire property.

zh: 我可以租个偏僻的地方。
en: I can lease space somewhere out of the way.

zh: 一团不洁的火焰在世界表皮之下闷燃。我已经唤醒了狼，不管你喜不喜欢，我都在为他工作。
en: An unclean flame smoulders beneath the world's skin. I've invoked the Wolf, and like it or not, I'm doing his work.

zh: 那气味既不是焦油，也不是血，也不是臭氧。裂分之狼的狂怒在空气中沸腾。
en: That scent which is neither tar nor blood nor ozone. The Wolf-Divided's rage boils beneath the air.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将在利刃的触摸下开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open beneath a blade's touch.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将为炙铁的触摸而开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open to the touch of hot iron.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将为热吻开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open to a fervent kiss.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将在鼓响时开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open at the beat of the drum.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将在曙光下开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open at the flush of dawn.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将对剪子起反应。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will answer to the scissors.

zh: 我为门关时，这道伤疤将在最后开启。
en: When I am Threshold, this scar will open last.

zh: recipe aspect: populates an operation with first escalation event spawns
en: recipe aspect: populates an operation with first escalation event spawns

zh: recipe aspect: populates an operation with second escalation event spawns
en: recipe aspect: populates an operation with second escalation event spawns

zh: recipe aspect: populates an operation with third escalation event spawns
en: recipe aspect: populates an operation with third escalation event spawns

zh: 一部玫瑰十字会式寓言的合集，推测为罗伯特·福路德所著，丛书第一卷。本书为后来的英译本。
en: The first volume of a compilation of quasi-Rosicrucian allegories, supposedly by Robert Fludd. This is a later English translation.

zh: 本书记录了十六世纪的炼金术狂想，据称为罗伯特·福路德所著，经常因其插图令人意乱神迷而遭禁。拉丁语原著。
en: A sixteenth-century alchemical fever-dream, attributed to Robert Fludd, frequently banned for the disturbing allure of its illustrations. This is in the original Latin.

zh: 一部玫瑰十字会式寓言的合集，推测为罗伯特·福路德所著，丛书第二卷。本书为后来的英译本。
en: The second volume of a compilation of quasi-Rosicrucian allegories, supposedly by Robert Fludd. This is a later English translation.

zh: 本书记录了十六世纪的炼金术狂想，据称为罗伯特·福路德所著，经常因其插图令人意乱神迷而遭禁。拉丁语原著。
en: A sixteenth-century alchemical fever-dream, attributed to Robert Fludd, frequently banned for the disturbing allure of its illustrations. This is in the original Latin.

zh: 一部玫瑰十字会式寓言的合集，推测为罗伯特·福路德所著，丛书第三卷。本书为后来的英译本。
en: The third volume of a compilation of quasi-Rosicrucian allegories, supposedly by Robert Fludd. This is a later English translation.

zh: 本书记录了十六世纪的炼金术狂想，据称为罗伯特·福路德所著，经常因其插图令人意乱神迷而遭禁。拉丁语原著。
en: A sixteenth-century alchemical fever-dream, attributed to Robert Fludd, frequently banned for the disturbing allure of its illustrations. This is in the original Latin.

zh: 漫宿的常客，睿智而又痴狂的索哈姆宣称自己与拾滩鸦当面对谈过。索哈姆随后坚决要求自己死后执行天葬。
en: The sage and ecstatic Soham, a frequent visitor to the Mansus, claimed to have discoursed with the Beachcrow in person. Soham later insisted on a sky-burial.

zh: 一本伐诃语著作，内或有关于隐秘诸神的不同起源的流言。
en: A Vak work that may contain gossip about the various origins of the secret gods.

zh: 观众都是喜新厌旧的。
en: All audiences are fickle.

zh: The Owl says that our last character was #PREVIOUSCHARACTERNAME#, our last desire was #LAST_DESIRE#, our last best follower was #LAST_FOLLOWER# and our last book was #LAST_BOOK#.
en: The Owl says that our last character was #PREVIOUSCHARACTERNAME#, our last desire was #LAST_DESIRE#, our last best follower was #LAST_FOLLOWER# and our last book was #LAST_BOOK#.

zh: 我用钱解决了一个问题。
en: I've paid off a problem.

zh: 此物曾经被用于一个不同的目的。
en: Once, this was intended for a different purpose.

zh: 错误已经酿成。但这并不是原本的打算，至少在最初的时候不是。
en: Mistakes were made. But that was not what had been intended, not at the beginning.

zh: 也许某物从另一重历史中漂流至此。
en: Perhaps something strayed in from another History.

zh: 有些人并不是他们应该成为的样子。
en: Someone wasn't what they were meant to be.

zh: 选择了生命，还是死亡？
en: Was the choice life, or death?

zh: 先前的打算并非如此，痕迹存留了下来。
en: Something different was intended; and a trace remains.

zh: 也许这道伤口的作者在发现我是谁的时候改变了她的意图。
en: Perhaps the author of this wound altered her intentions when she discovered who I was.

zh: 有些事情本可能有所不同。
en: Something might have been different.

zh: 此物曾经被以一种完全不同的角度悬挂起来。
en: Once, this was strung in a very different configuration.

zh: 曾经，有些事情本可能有所不同。
en: Once, something might have been different.

zh: 我记得“褪皮”告诉过我：“据说无名的具名者在醒时寻找秘密，并把它们作为礼物带给林地的丝毧。同样，猫也会把老鼠带到家里作为礼物送给主人，但我不认为这总能得到相同程度的认可。”
en: I remember something Peel told me: 'The Nameless Name has been known to hunt out secrets in the Wake, and to bring them as gifts to the Velvet in the Wood. Also, cats have been known to bring mice ind

zh: 一种阻碍、危险或是为时过早的结局。
en: An obstacle, a danger, or a premature conclusion.

zh: 这片广袤的土地犹如一个几达天边的土黄色火炉。到了夜晚，莽莽星空撒下冷冽的光。我们必须小心前行，或以铸铺就前路，或以杯避免干渴。
en: An immense and tawny furnace barely contained by its horizons. At night, a chilly wilderness of stars. We must go carefully, with Forge to shape our way or Grail to secure us against thirst.

zh: 很久以前，原始树木的领地遍布整片土地。他们统治过痕迹仍然存留着——比如这密不透风的根与刺的领土。铸的实用或者蛾的奥秘可能会帮我们渡过难关。
en: Long ago, the primal trees marched from sea to sea. Some traces of their reign still survive - like this dense and choking territory of root and thorn. The practicalities of Forge, or the mysteries of

zh: 荒凉的灰绿色山坡将目光引向远方白雪镶边、仿佛撕裂天空的山峰……这对于探索来讲将会是一场噩梦。铸将支持我们，冬将庇护我们。
en: Bleak and grey-green slopes lift the eye to distant snow-edged sky-straining peaks... it'll be the devil of a job to find anything in there. Forge will sustain us, Winter will shield us.

zh: 这里的大多数岛屿都在诸历史中为人所熟知，但我们的目的地却在未经标明的偏远水域。我们需要心或铸或才能生存下来。
en: Most of these islands are well-known to the Histories, but our destination lies in wild uncharted reaches. We'll need Heart or Forge to survive.

zh: 坑洼，泥沼和峭壁。这里的土地不可信任。我们需要强健的双手或者锐利的目光——铸或灯。
en: Pits and quags and sudden tumbles. The earth here cannot be trusted. We will need strong hands or clear sight - Forge, or Lantern.

zh: 一口无光且无人陪伴的深井，我能在此体会严酷的真知。
en: A lightless well of solitude where I may experience grim insights.

zh: 可能会获得宽赦。
en: Mercy may be shown.

zh: 长生者打造梦境来侵扰我。彻底忘掉这些梦会更稳妥，但不去想它则更明智。
en: The Long has set dreams to bedevil me. It would be safer to banish them, but wiser not to think of them. [If you Dream away this card or use it in a ritual, the Long will learn from you, increasing th

zh: 长生者送给我一个甜蜜的梦境。我必须管住自己不去想它。
en: The Long has sent me a sweet dream. I must not think of it.

zh: 如果你想要谁活下去，就千万别把这个用在他身上。
en: Under no circumstances should you put this in anyone you want to keep.

zh: 这名追随者的秘密已被曝光。只要他还在外活动，新闻媒体就可能对他产生兴趣。
en: This follower's secrets have been revealed to the public. Any time they're active, the press might take an interest.

zh: 一个和蔼可亲的富有女人。
en: An amiable woman of property.

zh: 一个和蔼可亲的富有女人。她现在不接见访客。
en: An amiable woman of property. She's not currently receiving visitors.

zh: 一个和蔼可亲的富有女人。她向你提出了委托。
en: An amiable woman of property. She has offered you a commission.

zh: 一个和蔼可亲的富有女人。她想要你的帮助。
en: An amiable woman of property. She'd like your help with something.

zh: 一个和蔼可亲的富有女人。她想要和你谈谈。
en: An amiable woman of property. She'd like to talk to you.

zh: 一项你可以带走的资产。
en: An asset that might travel with you.

zh: 在卷入诉讼案件前，波特曾是一名理发师。
en: Porter was a barber, until the court case.

zh: 波特为他朋友们的利益一刻不停地挥舞着剃刀和剪子。
en: Porter plies his razor and his scissors, to the great advantage of his friends.

zh: 波特一直保持低调无闻，直到他做好出手的准备。
en: Porter goes unnoticed, until he's ready.

zh: 波特像蜘蛛一样，总是藏在最意想不到的地方。
en: Porter, like a spider, is found in the most unexpected places.

zh: 波特纹丝不动地等待着，静止如一条蜥蜴。
en: Porter waits, still as a lizard.

zh: 波特已开始收集名字。
en: Porter has begun to collect names.

zh: 波特已开始收集名字。
en: Porter has begun to collect names.

zh: 波特已开始收集名字。
en: Porter has begun to collect names.

zh: 卓越的印记？
en: The mark of greatness?

zh: 一位富有的女士有时会来观看演出。人们都知道她对歌舞队队员抱有强烈的兴趣。
en: A lady of means sometimes attends performances. She has been known to show a keen interest in chorus members.

zh: 一位富有的男士有时会来观看演出。人们都知道他喜欢在演出结束后向歌舞队队员致以敬意。
en: A gentleman of means sometimes attends performances. He has been known to pay his respects to chorus members after the show.

zh: 有时她坐在第二排，为她的特殊团体寻找新血。被她雇佣的人则很少出现在城里。
en: Sometimes she sits in the second row, seeking new talent for her peculiar establishment. Those she employs are rarely seen about town.

zh: 我取得了一个秘密配方——蜂蜜、冥府百合、象牙骨粉，再加一种只在埋葬死者处生长的斑点菌菇。
en: I have here a secret - a recipe of honey, asphodel, powdered ivory, and a mottled fungus that grows only where the dead are buried.

zh: 有些东西太过珍贵而不能用金钱衡量。少有东西太过珍贵而不能用心跳衡量。
en: Some things are too precious to buy with money. Fewer things are too precious to buy with heartbeats.

zh: “代价，名词。价值，再加上一笔合理的钱，以补偿要求这笔钱时良心所受的折磨。” ——比尔斯。
en: 'PRICE, n. Value, plus a reasonable sum for the wear and tear of conscience in demanding it.' - Bierce.

zh: 只要我不因渎职或丑闻引起主教的注意，我便可以在这里讨上一份生活。
en: As long as I do not draw the attention of the Bishop through dereliction or scandal, I can draw a living here.

zh: 被我攥在手心之人。
en: I have this one in the palm of my hand.

zh: 研究课题的提纲
en: Research project agenda

zh: 我将承认耐心胜于蛮力，把我的刃之密传拗转为冬之密传。
en: I am acknowledging the victory of patience over strength, subverting my Edge lore to Winter lore.

zh: 我将把我的铸之密传拗转为刃之密传。
en: I am subverting my Forge lore to Edge lore.

zh: 我将把我的杯之密传拗转为蛾之密传。
en: I am subverting my Grail lore to Moth lore.

zh: 我将把我的心之密传拗转为杯之密传。
en: I am subverting my Heart lore to Grail lore.

zh: 我将把我的灯之密传拗转为铸之密传。
en: I am subverting my Lantern lore to Forge lore.

zh: 我将把我的蛾之密传拗转为灯之密传。
en: I am subverting my Moth lore to Lantern lore

zh: 我将把我的冬之密传拗转为心之密传。
en: I am subverting my Winter lore to Heart lore

zh: 我希望提升我的刃之密传。
en: I hope to improve my Edge lore.

zh: 我希望提升我的铸之密传。
en: I hope to improve my Forge lore.

zh: 我希望提升我的杯之密传。
en: I hope to improve my Grail lore.

zh: 我希望提升我的心之密传。
en: I hope to improve my Heart lore.

zh: 我希望提升我的灯之密传。
en: I hope to improve my Lantern lore.

zh: 我希望提升我的蛾之密传。
en: I hope to improve my Moth lore.

zh: 我将加深我对启之密传的了解。
en: I am deepening my understanding of Knock lore.

zh: 我希望提升我的冬之密传。
en: I hope to improve my Winter lore.

zh: 对结局的承诺。
en: A commitment to a conclusion.

zh: 这是我们崇敬的真正奥秘。
en: These are the true mysteries of our veneration.

zh: 这是我们崇敬的真正奥秘。
en: These are the true mysteries of our veneration.

zh: 这是我们崇敬的真正奥秘。
en: These are the true mysteries of our veneration.

zh: 这是我们崇敬的真正奥秘。
en: These are the true mysteries of our veneration.

zh: 这是我们崇敬的真正奥秘。
en: These are the true mysteries of our veneration.

zh: 这是我们崇敬的真正奥秘。
en: These are the true mysteries of our veneration.

zh: 这是我们崇敬的真正奥秘。
en: These are the true mysteries of our veneration.

zh: 这是我们崇敬的真正奥秘。
en: These are the true mysteries of our veneration.

zh: 该让他发下更进一步的誓言了。
en: Time to take the deeper oaths.

zh: 凭借正确的文件，你可以证明任何事情。
en: With the right papers, you can prove anything.

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: Used to mark rkx internal components
en: Used to mark rkx internal components

zh: 一部超现实风格的当代戏剧，作者是名为莫妮卡·麦地那的神秘人物。剧中的诸位河川女王一一为人所杀。
en: A surreal contemporary play by the enigmatic Monica Medina, in which the Queens of the Rivers are murdered, one by one.

zh: “当心耐心之人的愤怒。”——德莱顿，《押沙龙与阿齐托菲尔》
en: 'Beware the fury of a patient man.' - Dryden, 'Absalom and Achitophel'

zh: 很小，看起来很脆弱，但是砸起来可真带劲儿。
en: Tiny, fragile-looking, but it packs a hell of a wallop.

zh: 从大枯萎之前充满秘密的风土中榨出的好酒。只要有自制力、耐心和决心，就有可能只品尝一杯这种酒，而不是灌完整瓶。
en: A great vintage wrung from a terroir ripe with secrets in the times before the Grand Blight. With discipline, patience, and resolution, it is possible to taste a glass of this wine and not to finish t

zh: 一把很久以前从斯特拉迪瓦里家族买来的吉他，由有着柔和光泽的伊利里安枫木制成，镶嵌着杏仁叶和石榴的图案。我听说去年有一把这样的琴被从格里斯伯爵夫人的藏品中盗走了。在那时存留下来的乐器中，这恐怕是还能用于演奏的最后一把了。
en: A guitar purchased long ago from the House of Stradivari, shaped from softly glowing Illyrian maple and inlaid with a design of almond leaves and pomegranates. I'd heard one had been stolen from the G

zh: 它最初只是一本欧洲城市的地图集。几十年来，它被十几个人增补和注释过——学徒，窃贼，士兵，间谍——为了标记出秘密的路线和隐藏的角落。
en: This began life as an atlas of the cities of Europe. Over decades it's been extended and annotated by a dozen hands - adepts, burglars, soldiers, spies - to show secret routes and hiding-nooks.

zh: 一小瓶攻击性很强的唾液，就像一把黏黏的万能钥匙。
en: A little jar of aggressively invasive saliva, like a viscid skeleton key.

zh: 在这些图像中有一个被遗忘的黄金真理——一个来自骄阳裂分之前时代的真理。我弄不明白它。它也不适合我。但即使只是真理的反光，也算是一种启迪。
en: There's a forgotten and golden truth in these images - a truth from the times before the Sun was divided. I don't understand it. It's not meant for me. But even the reflected light of that truth is an

zh: 一幅描绘最终地平线的油画，主调是灿烂的绿色与金色。
en: An oil painting of a final horizon, in green-and-gold and glory.

zh: 一幅描绘着一座没于雪中之城的油画。在城中最阴暗的塔楼后面，一镰弯月正在升起。
en: An oil painting of a city foundered in snow. Behind its bleakest tower, a sickle moon rises.

zh: 追随者将变为对手
en: Followers will become Rivals

zh: 要想见到光明，人不能盲目，但有时，正是光明使我们盲目。
en: To see the light, one cannot be blind; but sometimes, it's the light that blinds us.

zh: 组织的宗旨已经开始动摇。我能够施加压力来调整它的方向。
en: This organisation has begun to question its tenets. I could apply pressure to alter its direction.

zh: 演出召唤物的召唤时限。
en: Extend a d's period of service.

zh: 忠诚生变
en: A Change in Loyalties

zh: 泥土之下是骸骨。骸骨之中是回忆。回忆之内是色彩。
en: Beneath the soil, bones. In the bones, memory. In the memory, colours.

zh: 别人曾用的肉身。
en: Once another wore this flesh.

zh: 这将是一段漫长而艰难的旅程。但是，在我目前的情况下，这一点可能会让它变得更诱人。
en: It'll be a long hard journey. But, in my current circumstances, perhaps that should make it more attractive.

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 芮妮拉在三个不相邻的辖区里被判三次无罪释放。她很可能永远不会因为任何事获罪。
en: Renira has been found not guilty three times in three separate jurisdictions. She will likely never be found guilty of anything.

zh: 尽管她的野心已经壮大，芮妮拉仍然在追寻食欲时保持谨慎。
en: Renira continues to pursue her appetites discreetly, although her ambitions have grown.

zh: 无论去哪，芮妮拉都能引人爱慕。她只会将注意力放在那些值得报以爱慕的人身上。
en: Renira commands admiration wherever she goes. She reserves her attentions  for those who merit admiration in return.

zh: 芮妮拉几乎每天都会收到来自爱慕者的礼物。有些礼物是花，有些是情诗。有时候她收到的是珠宝，又有时候是紧紧包裹在血迹斑斑的绉纸里的充满绝望的礼物。
en: Renira receives tokens from admirers almost daily. Some gifts are flowers, others poetry. Sometimes she receives jewels; sometimes, desperate gifts wrapped tightly in blood-stained crepe paper.

zh: 芮妮拉总有办法消磨时间。
en: Renira always finds ways to pass the time.

zh: 芮妮拉喜欢将别人对她的爱慕转为崇拜。
en: Renira likes to convert admirers into worshippers.

zh: 芮妮拉喜欢将别人对她的爱慕转为崇拜。
en: Renira likes to convert admirers into worshippers.

zh: 芮妮拉喜欢将别人对她的爱慕转为崇拜。
en: Renira likes to convert admirers into worshippers.

zh: “有很多人担心自己的声名，很少有人担心自己的良心。”——普林尼
en: 'Many fear their reputation, few their conscience.' - Pliny

zh: 太多的不满会损害忠诚度。
en: Too many grievances can compromise loyalty.

zh: 我为一种躁动的向往所俘。我似乎产生了某种……企图。究竟是何企图？
en: A restless longing has me. I feel that I want to do... something. What, exactly?

zh: 一切如故
en: All is as it was

zh: 揭穿一个未知的敌人
en: Reveals an unknown enemy

zh: 揭穿一个暗地里的阴谋
en: Reveals a concealed strategy

zh: 有些问题只有一个答案；而有些问题从来没有答案。
en: Some questions have only one answer. Some have no answer at all.

zh: 虽说对手飞升到日之居所的可能性微乎其微。但如果他成功了，我或许会失去飞升的机会。
en: Opportunities to ascend to the House of the Sun are rare. If another achieves it, I may miss my chance. [If this reaches seven, your Rival has a chance to win the game instead of you at the next Seaso

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 有人一直在打听我。我很熟悉对那个人的描述。
en: Someone has been asking after me. I recognise the description of that someone, too well.

zh: 今晚我的追兵很活跃。
en: My pursuers are active tonight

zh: 我的追兵在犹豫。
en: My pursuers are hesitating

zh: 这颗心脏跳动了多少次？这些心跳又有多少是从别人那里偷来的？而它现在正在枯萎。
en: How many times did this heart beat? How many of those beats were stolen from another? It is withering now.

zh: 如橡树般历尽风霜，如海崖般疤痕累累。我见识过他的力量，如果我不害怕，我就是个傻子。
en: Seasoned like oak and scarred like sea-cliffs. I have seen his strength, and I would be a fool not to fear it.

zh: 追捕我的人已经在这里出现了。
en: My pursuers have already established a presence here.

zh: Used to mark rkx internal components
en: Used to mark rkx internal components

zh: 我在街上认出了一张脸。我们曾经一起工作过。我暴露了吗？
en: I recognised a face in the street. We worked together once. Was I spotted?

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 低等级的清算人只是被雇来做坏事的人。 高等级的清算人则既狡猾又有广阔的人脉，并以偷来的岁月武装自身。
en: Lower-ranked reckoners are just people paid to do bad things. Higher-ranked reckoners are cunning, well-connected and armoured in stolen years.

zh: 清算人暴徒把注意力转向了这座城市。
en: The reckoner mob has turned its attention to this city.

zh: 一个因凶猛狡诈而被提拔的清算人高层，靠着借来的岁月扮出一副圆滑光鲜的模样。
en: A reckoner lieutenant, winnowed from the ranks by ferocity and guile, grown sleek on borrowed years.

zh: 如果我能发现这个弱点，它就能为我所用。
en: If I can uncover this weakness, it'll give me an advantage.

zh: 我的大敌害怕猫！长久以来，哪怕只是有人暗示着提到了一星半点，他都恨不得杀死对方。现在我知道原因了。（不过他并不回避圣地或高处。）
en: My Foe fears cats! He's long been ready to murder anyone who even hinted as much. Now I know why. (But he doesn't avoid holy places, or heights.)

zh: 如果我能发现这个弱点，它就能为我所用。
en: If I can uncover this weakness, it'll give me an advantage.

zh: 传言是真的——风雪使我的大敌头脑愚钝、行动迟缓。（不过炎热和海洋不会给他造成麻烦。）
en: The rumours are true - snow makes my Foe slow and stupid. (Heat and the sea don't trouble him, though.)

zh: 如果我能发现这个弱点，它就能为我所用。
en: If I can uncover this weakness, it'll give me an advantage.

zh: 那是宗教教育的缘故？还是因为长久以来的迷信？我的大敌不愿亵渎圣地。（不过他并不回避猫或高处。）
en: A religious upbringing? A persistent superstition? My enemy is reluctant to desecrate holy places. (But he doesn't avoid cats, or heights.)

zh: 如果我能发现这个弱点，它就能为我所用。
en: If I can uncover this weakness, it'll give me an advantage.

zh: 传言是真的——只要天气够热，我的大敌就会变得头脑愚钝、行动迟缓。（不过寒冷和海洋不会给他造成麻烦。）
en: The rumours are true - when the weather is hot enough, my foe becomes slow and stupid. (Cold and the sea don't trouble him, though.)

zh: 高处使我的大敌头晕目眩，意志消沉。（不过他并不回避圣地或猫。）
en: High places dizzy and demoralise my Foe. (But he doesn't avoid cats, or holy places.)

zh: 如果我能发现这个弱点，它就能为我所用。
en: If I can uncover this weakness, it'll give me an advantage.

zh: 双角利斧不眷顾我的大敌。它的影子不论落在哪里，都会让他犹豫不前。（但他不会特别忌惮其他司辰。）
en: The Horned Axe does not favour my Foe, and where its shadow falls, he hesitates. (But he doesn't have any especial fear of the other Hours.)

zh: 如果我能发现这个弱点，它就能为我所用。
en: If I can uncover this weakness, it'll give me an advantage.

zh: 所有清算人面对海洋都十分谨慎。但我听说的事情是真的：我的大敌尤其害怕海洋的触碰。（不过炎热和寒冷不会给他造成麻烦。）
en: All reckoners treat the sea with caution. But it's true what I've heard: my Foe fears its touch. (Heat and cold don't bother him, though.)

zh: 如果我能发现这个弱点，它就能为我所用。
en: If I can uncover this weakness, it'll give me an advantage.

zh: 如果关于这东西的故事有一半是真实的，那么它以几乎同等的热情憎恨着一切。但对于我的大敌，狼的怒火燃烧得更为炽热。（但他不会特别忌惮其他司辰。）
en: If half the stories of this thing are true, it hates everything with near-equal incandescence. But in my Foe's case, the Wolf's rage burns a fraction hotter. (He doesn't have any especial fear of the

zh: 这是一个对我产生了爱慕之意的人。
en: This is someone who has developed a romantic interest in me.

zh: 萝丝是个狡黠的姑娘，她认为你是个不同寻常的角色。
en: Rose is a sly miss, and she recognises you as someone uncommon.

zh: 萝丝是个狡黠的姑娘，但她忠诚。
en: Rose is a sly miss, but loyal.

zh: 萝丝将她的狡猾磨砺到了极点。
en: Rose has sharpened her cunning to a concentrated point.

zh: 在萝丝与目标人物见面的前一晚，后者会收到一个附带一支黄玫瑰的包裹。
en: The night before Rose meets her subject, the subject will receive a package with a single yellow rose.

zh: 萝丝还没有放弃。我怀疑她偷藏了餐具。
en: Rose has not given up. I suspect her of hiding cutlery.

zh: 萝丝早已停止畏惧她的敌人。
en: Rose has long since ceased to fear her enemies.

zh: 萝丝早已停止畏惧她的敌人。
en: Rose has long since ceased to fear her enemies.

zh: 萝丝早已停止畏惧她的敌人。
en: Rose has long since ceased to fear her enemies.

zh: 集结成册的绳结姐妹会教喻；该秘传默观修会存在于每一重历史，且在某些重历史中拥有莫大的力量。本书由署名“希帕提亚”者编集，是致圣宁法的献礼。
en: A volume of collected teachings of the Sisterhood of the Knot, that arcane-contemplative order that existed in all histories, but in some was very powerful indeed. This was recorded by 'Hypatia', and

zh: 印有某秘传默观修会的标志，该修会通称绳结姐妹会。希腊语著。
en: Marked with the tripled sign of the arcane-contemplative order known as the Sisterhood of the Knot. In Greek.

zh: 集结成册的绳结姐妹会教喻；该秘传默观修会存在于每一重历史，且在某些重历史中拥有莫大的力量。本书由署名“努列尔”者编集，是致圣特里丰的献礼。
en: A volume of collected teachings of the Sisterhood of the Knot, that arcane-contemplative order that existed in all histories, but in some was very powerful indeed. This was recorded by 'Nuriel', and d

zh: 印有某秘传默观修会的标志，该修会通称绳结姐妹会。希腊语著。
en: Marked with the tripled sign of the arcane-contemplative order known as the Sisterhood of the Knot. In Greek.

zh: 绳结姐妹会一个遭人唾弃支派的异端教喻；该秘传默观修会存在于每一重历史中。本书由署名“瓦兹内”者编集，是致圣勒庇爵的献礼。
en: The heretical teachings of a disgraced offshoot of the Sisterhood of the Knot, that arcane-contemplative order that existed in all histories. This was recorded by 'Waznei', and dedicated to St Respici

zh: 印有某秘传默观修会的三倍形标志，该修会通称绳结姐妹会……但加上了第四片草叶，整个标志被破坏了。希腊语著。
en: Marked with the tripled sign of the arcane-contemplative order known as the Sisterhood of the Knot... but a fourth leaf has been added, disfiguring the sign. In Greek.

zh: 这或许能让我找到所需的人，或者这意味着某种完全不同的东西。
en: This might lead me to someone I need; or it might mean something else entirely.

zh: 会将“大功业”卡牌转变为其不稳定，待修复的版本。
en: Converts a Great Work item to an unstable version that needs fixing.

zh: 萨利巴是一个有着惊人胃口的人。他早就过了通过满足欲望获得欢愉的阶段。但他学到了很多。
en: Saliba is a man of prodigious appetites. He is long past the point where their satisfaction has given him any pleasure; but he has learnt so very much. [You might be able to recruit this acquaintance

zh: 萨利巴了解鲜血，但尚未了解生育。
en: Saliba understands blood. He does not yet understand birth.

zh: 萨利巴在一间没有窗户的房间中享受欢愉。他的伙伴会一次又一次地回来找他，即便每一次后他们都比原先减损了一点。
en: Saliba's pleasures are taken in a room without windows. His companions return to him again and again, even though each time they are a little less.

zh: 萨利巴的魅力如大锤抡下般猛烈。想抗拒他的欲望是极度困难的。
en: Saliba's charisma strikes with the force of a hammer. It is horribly difficult to refuse his desires.

zh: 萨利巴的愤怒如地下的岩浆。
en: Saliba's anger burns like buried lava.

zh: 萨利巴不能忍受无聊。
en: Saliba does not tolerate boredom.

zh: 萨利巴不能忍受无聊。
en: Saliba does not tolerate boredom.

zh: 萨利巴不能忍受无聊。
en: Saliba does not tolerate boredom.

zh: 一处供人冥想，甚至获得安宁的地方。
en: A space designed for contemplation and perhaps even peace.

zh: 他们所说的只是真理的影子，但我们的影子暴露了我们。
en: What they say is only a shadow of the truth, but by our shadows we are discovered.

zh: 全世界都将知道我的丑事。
en: I won't be easily forgotten.

zh: 一种不可逆的行为导致的不可逆的结果。
en: The irrevocable outcome of an irrevocable act.

zh: 浮士德：我以为地狱只是个传说。梅菲斯托费勒斯：嗳，尽管这么想吧，经历会改变你的看法。
en: FAUSTUS: I think Hell's a fable. MEPHISTOPHILIS: Ay, think so still, till experience change thy mind.

zh: 我拥有如此之多的生命，很难停止不动。我坐下休息时，关节痛如火烧。我知道我一旦止息，或许便是永远。
en: I have so much life in me. It is difficult not to move. When I sit, my joints burn. I know that if I stop, I might stop for always. [You've made a permanent sacrifice for power. Be careful: unlike Hea

zh: 我的动脉在呻吟，我的血管在燃烧。我无法再逼迫自己前进了。
en: My arteries sing, my veins burn. I cannot push myself any further.

zh: 并非所有门洞都是创口，但所有创口都是门洞。
en: Not all doors are wounds; but all wounds are doors.

zh: 现在我有时会露出皮肤下的美貌。当我转向合适的角度——当我的衣服滑落一旁——当遇上锐利的眼光——我的形状被完美地揭示无余。
en: Sometimes now I am beautiful beneath my skin. When I bend to the right angle - when my garments falls aside - when the eye is keen - my shape is a perfect revelation. [You've made a permanent sacrific

zh: 雅宁斯说有些课程只能写在皮肤上。或许如此吧，但也可能是雅宁斯太享受他的作品了。
en: Jannings says that some lessons can only be written on skin. That might be so, but it might be that Jannings just enjoys his work a little too much.

zh: 我换上过无数副假面孔。每一副假面孔都让我更难回想起自己的本来面目。即便在情绪高扬时，我面上的筋骨依然柔软可塑。
en: I've worn so many guises. With each new guise it is harder to recall my original face. Even the cartilage softens when my tempers are high.

zh: 我每换上一副表情，五官就像水一样起涟漪。如果我不加小心，我将彻底溶解。
en: With each new expression my features ripple like water. If I am not careful I shall entirely dissolve.

zh: 又朝日落走近了些，看到了夜空中的第一颗星。
en: Step closer to the sunset, and see the first stars of night.

zh: 作者宁在写下本书后追悔不迭。
en: Nyn wrote this, and came to regret it.

zh: 作者宁在写下本书后追悔不迭。
en: Nyn wrote this, and came to regret it.

zh: 对手小心的决定留下了痕迹。
en: Traces left by a rival's decision to move cautiously.

zh: 对手对其敌人下手的决定留下了痕迹。
en: Traces left by a rival's decision to move against an enemy.

zh: 亚兰与她这三千多年以来所养育的众多孩子所使用的语言。
en: The language of Aram, and all her many children these three thousand years and more.

zh: 已经消失的富奇诺湖东侧曾有过一些住户。贺拉斯曾警告说，那里是女巫之地。而这便是他们所使用的语言。有些人将之称作“干涸之语”，另一些人则称其为“女巫之语”。
en: A people lived east of the lost lake Fucino. Horace warned that theirs was the land of witches. This was their language, called by some 'the dry tongue' and by others 'the tongue of witches'.[Some boo

zh: 毫无疑问你对从早期到拜占庭时期的每一种希腊语都十分精通。但是，能拥有那些参考书籍总归是件好事。
en: No doubt you're conversant with every variety of Greek, from the beginning to Byzantium. But it's a good thing you have those reference works.

zh: 一门死去的语言：如金刚石般失去生机。
en: A dead language: dead as diamonds.

zh: 深远的沙漠深处引藏最深的关于最隐秘仪式的手迹。据说，它是蚁母其人与生俱来的语言。
en: The most secret script of the innermost rites of the deep sects of the deep desert. The birth-tongue, it is claimed, of the Mother of Ants herself. [Some books must be translated before they can be re

zh: 弗里吉亚人崇拜的“伟大母亲”就是我们所称的圣杯。他们的土地曾见证过绳结姐妹会诞生，而这门语言便是轰雷之皮飞升时用于恳求的语言。
en: The Phrygians worshipped the Great Mother, who we call the Grail. Theirs was the land in which the Sisterhood of the Knot was born, and this is the tongue in which the Thunderskin pleaded, at his asce

zh: 次大陆的神圣语言。通向四千年的诗歌、典仪和奥秘的大门如今对你敞开，只要你能从它如同莲花般卷曲的词汇中挣出一条路。
en: The holy tongue of the Subcontinent. The door to four thousand years of poetry, ceremony and mystery now opens before you, if you can struggle through the lotus convolutions of its vocabulary. [Some b

zh: 这是早于诸神自血中诞生，更早于猿人直立行走前，就能在太阳居屋里听到的语言。
en: Before gods arose from blood, before ever ape stood upright, this was the language heard in the House of the Sun.

zh: 你的前进被其阻碍。
en: Your progress is prevented.

zh: 不久便会发生其名所述的事情。
en: Something relevant will occur, soon

zh: 即林地之风逆转方向的时节：食欲伴同凡人一并高升。
en: This is the season that the winds of the Wood reverse direction: a time when appetites and mortals both rise.

zh: 我们所有人既是猿猴，又是天使。
en: We are all of us both ape and angel.

zh: 黑夜等待着我。
en: Night waits for me.

zh: 明天会是更好的一天。
en: Tomorrow may be a better day.

zh: 瘴气污浊了夜晚的空气。
en: Malign influences taint the night air.

zh: 即未雨绸缪之期。
en: These are the days of anticipation.

zh: 我是不是正被人盯梢？
en: Am I being watched?

zh: 何等的奇观。
en: Such sights.

zh: 祭司尤赛在本书中对先于人类的司辰，石源诸神的灭绝大加庆贺。
en: The priest Juceh celebrates the extirpation of the Gods-who-were-stone, those Hours who preceded humanity.

zh: 由祭司兼隐世的学者尤赛所作。
en: A work by Juceh, the priest and hermit scholar.

zh: 记录了远古司辰——一位石源神的毁灭。
en: An account of the destruction of a primaeval Hour, a god-who-was-Stone.

zh: 记录了远古司辰——一位石源神的毁灭。
en: An account of the destruction of a primaeval Hour, a god-who-was-Stone.

zh: 又称《剃发之人》。据传说，连善于变形的瓦纳拉猴族都拒绝承认这篇密传。
en: Also known as 'The One Who Has Shaven His Hair'. This, the story goes, is the lore that even the shapechanging Vanaras rejected.

zh: 又称《剃发之人》。据传说，连善于变形的瓦纳拉猴族都拒绝承认这篇密传。
en: Also known as 'The One Who Has Shaven His Hair'. This, the story goes, is the lore that even the shapechanging Vanaras rejected.

zh: 如果我得到的情报无误，那么这个城市的某个地方隐藏着一个崇拜上校的密教。
en: If my intelligence is correct, there's a cult of the Colonel somewhere in this city.

zh: 一所隐秘的神祠，供奉着一位隐秘的神明：这位神灵有时被称为“上校”。我的大敌与上校打过些交道，但他不允许我以前的同伙们造访上校的神殿。
en: A secret shrine of a secret god: the power sometimes known as the Colonel. My Foe had some dealings with the Colonel, and my old mob was discouraged from visiting his shrines.

zh: 一处供奉某位斗争与冲突之司辰的场所。
en: A place dedicated to an Hour of struggle and conflict.

zh: 如果我得到的情报无误，那么这个城市的某个地方隐藏着一个崇拜狮子匠的密教。
en: If my intelligence is correct, there's a cult of the Lionsmith somewhere in this city.

zh: 一所隐秘的神祠，供奉着一位隐秘的神明：这位神灵有时被称为“狮子匠”。我以前的同伙都知道应该避开他的神殿。
en: A secret shrine of a secret god: the power sometimes known as the Lionsmith. My old mob knew to avoid his shrines.

zh: 肉体皆会衰败。但我得到了保证，我的肉体会衰败得稍晚一点。
en: All flesh fails. But I've been promised that my flesh will fail a little later.

zh: 本书记载了对修习无形之术所付出代价的警告和忏悔，乃十七世纪的术士（据传亦是永生不死者）朱利安·科赛利馈赠学生之物。
en: Warnings and confessions about the cost of the invisible arts, addressed to a student by the seventeenth-century magus (and reputed immortal) Julian Coseley.

zh: 一部咏颂贪婪享乐的诗集，作者是传闻中从女继承人成为贵妇又成为诗人的阿拉贝拉·达斯克，该名很可能是伪名。
en: Poems of greedy delight composed by the possibly pseudonymous Arabella Dusk, the rumoured heiress-turned-madam-turned-poetess.

zh: 克雷格·思雷，天文学者兼先锋派诗人，因自己健康不济而对怪力乱神产生了兴趣。
en: Craig Slee, the astronomer and avant-garde poet, has developed an interest in the esoteric as his health wanes.

zh: 思雷向所有愿意听的人热心讲述他的理论：群星恍若小小的创口，而太阳恍若辉煌的血流。他的诗比以往更古怪了。
en: Slee speaks earnestly of his theories to all who will listen: the stars as tiny wounds, and the Sun as a haemorrhage of glory. His poetry is more eccentric than ever.

zh: 思雷的头痛愈发严重，他的理论却一天天地实用起来。他的诗也越写越好了。
en: Slee's headaches have become crippling, but his theories grow more practical by the day. His poetry's getting better, too.

zh: 思雷仅用数学和诗歌的语言讲话。两者皆引人入胜：其间的差异变得越来越难分辨。
en: Slee speaks exclusively in mathematics and in poetry. Both are compelling: it is increasingly difficult to tell the difference.

zh: 思雷着了魔似地在墙上写诗。如果我们不提供墨水，他就用他能弄到的液体书写。
en: Slee writes poetry on the walls, obsessively. If we don't provide ink, he uses what he can.

zh: 思雷总巴望弄到更多资源好钻研他的理论。
en: Slee is always hungry for resources to pursue his theories.

zh: 思雷总巴望弄到更多资源好钻研他的理论。
en: Slee is always hungry for resources to pursue his theories.

zh: 思雷总巴望弄到更多资源好钻研他的理论。
en: Slee is always hungry for resources to pursue his theories.

zh: 我为我的对手下好了圈套。
en: I have prepared an ambush for a rival.

zh: 有些圈套逃也轻松，有些却是无比牢固。
en: Some snares are easy to escape. Some are webs of steel.

zh: 要想招募信徒，我必须构建一些让他们笃信的东西。
en: To recruit believers, I must build something for them to believe.

zh: 米卡·席，有时也称“石英术士”，用酸液将本作蚀刻在了石头上。本作后被多次复制，不过复制品往往散佚于山体滑坡。
en: Micah Xi, sometimes called the Magus of Quartz, etched this work in acid on stone. It has been reproduced many times since, but the reproductions are often lost to landslides.

zh: 一部时遭损毁的著作，作者是米卡·席。
en: Micah Xi's often-destroyed work.

zh: 作为活动地点，或工作基地的房间。
en: Room for a venue, or a base of operations.

zh: 斯宾塞颈戴一个小小的圣亚割妮徽记，贴在他心口的伤疤上。
en: Spencer carries a tiny icon of St Agnes on a chain around his neck, to rest on the scar above his heart.

zh: 有人延道路而行，而有人则是他人的道路。斯宾塞不再对我们说他做出了决定：相反地，他称决定做出了他。
en: Some follow the Way, and some are the Way for others. Spencer no longer tells us he made a decision: instead, he suggests that the decision has made him.

zh: 斯宾塞已在各种可能性之间摇摆了许久……如今，他做出了选择。
en: For so long, Spencer flirted with these possibilities... and now he's made his decision.

zh: 斯宾塞不会再允许任何事物存于他的明察之外。如今他已前进到最后一个门关。
en: Spencer will no longer permit anything to be kept from him. Now he approaches the final threshold.

zh: 我开始重复我自己了。
en: I have begun to repeat myself.

zh: 长生者正在谋划下一次对付我们的行动。如果我们派出密探，或许能找到揭开他们图谋的线索。
en: The Long is planning their next action against us. If we send a spy, we might be able to discover something of their intentions.

zh: 这次，我们的敌人准备用某种方式直接攻击我们。
en: Our enemy intends a direct attack of some kind, this time.

zh: 长生者正在谋划下一次对付我们的行动。如果我们派出密探，或许能找到揭开他们图谋的线索。
en: The Long is planning their next action against us. If we send a spy, we might be able to discover something of their intentions.

zh: 这次，我们的敌人不会对我们下手。他们会修整……或许在休整中变得更强大。
en: Our enemy will not move against us, this time. They will rest... and perhaps in resting they will grow stronger.

zh: 长生者正在谋划下一次对付我们的行动。如果我们派出密探，或许能找到揭开他们图谋的线索。
en: The Long is planning their next action against us. If we send a spy, we might be able to discover something of their intentions.

zh: 这次，我们的敌人准备用诡计对付我们。
en: Our enemy intends to use trickery against us, this time.

zh: 我方的密探颠覆了敌人准备工作中的一些环节。
en: Our spy has upset some element of our enemy's preparations.

zh: 我们永生不死的敌人正在准备另一次行动。
en: Our immortal enemy is preparing another action.

zh: 此计已暴露。
en: This strategy's been exposed.

zh: 一盘硝酸盐胶片，标签上写着“STUMM!（噤声!）”——意为“沉默”，或者“哑”。旁边颤抖的的字迹则是导演的姓名：“杰尼克·克罗塞”。
en: A can of nitrate film labelled STUMM! - 'Silent' or, perhaps, 'Mute'. The director's name, 'Jernik Krosse', is shakily appended.

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 蜕衣俱乐部的所有者，言语简练，精力充沛，目光炯炯，从不眨眼。
en: The laconic, energetic, bright-eyed, unblinking owner of the Ecdysis Club.

zh: 本书网罗了无敌太阳教会——即第三和第四史中的飞升者——的典礼中的各种祷文，仪式和赞歌。该教派在置闰——即太阳遭到分裂时——发生分裂，留存下来的部分不再具有不可小觑的势力。
en: A miscellany of the funerary prayers, ceremonies and hymns of the Church of the Unconquered Sun, which was ascendant in the Third and Fourth Histories. It schismed during the Intercalate, when the Sun

zh: “无敌太阳教会”的经文，该教会在许多重历史中被迫转入地下。拉丁语著。
en: A holy text of the Church of the Unconquered Sun, which in many histories was driven underground. In Latin.

zh: 有限，就像人一生的时间。
en: Finite, like a lifetime.

zh: 可能会引得警探调查。
en: A detective may wish to investigate this.

zh: 西尔维娅九岁那年在树林里失去了一只眼睛。每年圣烛节，她都会回到那个她埋葬眼睛的地方。
en: Sylvia lost her eye in the woods when she was nine years old. Every year, at Candlemas, she returns to the place she buried it.

zh: 西尔维娅在屋脊、枯潮的河流、爱尔兰威士忌以及变魔术上有很深的造诣。
en: Sylvia has a detailed knowledge of roof-ridges, of the river at low tide, of Irish whiskey, and of conjuring tricks.

zh: 西尔维娅最爱用的策略是微笑并闭上单眼。值得注意的是，与她交谈的人时常会将自己内心的所想脱口而出。
en: Sylvia's favourite tactic is to smile and close her eye. It is remarkable how often her interlocutor will blurt out exactly what they are thinking.

zh: 西尔维娅已经学会迅速从某处离去，甚至快到可称还未进入既已离去。有时，她会将枯叶留在在窗台之下，有时她会在将名字写在不会被人找见的地方。
en: Sylvia has learnt to get out of a place almost before she has gotten in. Sometimes, she leaves dead leaves beneath the window-sill. Sometimes, she writes her name where it won't be found.

zh: 我们每一小时都会监看一下西尔维娅，以防万一。
en: We check on Sylvia every hour, just in case.

zh: 西尔维娅的日程安排不清不楚。人们怀疑连她自己也搞不清楚。
en: Sylvia's agenda is not clear. One suspects it is not clear even to Sylvia.

zh: 西尔维娅的日程安排不清不楚。人们怀疑连她自己也搞不清楚。
en: Sylvia's agenda is not clear. One suspects it is not clear even to Sylvia.

zh: 西尔维娅的日程安排不清不楚。人们怀疑连她自己也搞不清楚。
en: Sylvia's agenda is not clear. One suspects it is not clear even to Sylvia.

zh: 昆虫学家朱莉安娜·萨斯托斯基最终坚称本书为寄居在人类体内的昆虫所作。她从来没为她的说法提供过证据。
en: The entomologist Juliana Zawistowski eventually came to insist that this text was written by insects inhabiting a human skin. She never presented evidence for her claim.

zh: 昆虫学家朱莉安娜·萨斯托斯基最终坚称本书为寄居在人类体内的昆虫所作。她从来没为她的说法提供过证据。
en: The entomologist Juliana Zawistowski eventually came to insist that this text was written by insects inhabiting a human skin. She never presented evidence for her claim.

zh: “Caput gerat lupinum（罪人当佩狼首）”，他们过去经常如此谈论不法之徒。“这是划分人与兽的底线。”或者其他类似的话。拉丁文从来都不是我的强项。眼下我站在了这条分割线上错误的一侧……然而这或许也是一个机会。
en: 'Caput gerat lupinum', they used to say of outlaws. 'This is the line of division.' Or something like that. Latin was never my strong point. I'm on the wrong side of that line of division now... but p

zh: 我从一个信任过我的清算人首领那里偷走了一大笔岁月。他会跨越大陆追杀我，但他的能力也有极限。
en: I've stolen a fortune in years from a reckoner lord who trusted me. He'll pursue me across continents, but even his reach has limits. [Achieve seven Obscurity to send a taunting message to your reckon

zh: 赤红的太阳低垂天际时的凉风。海浪原先的话音。在我们身边一同跋涉积雪的虚影。都压在时间的玻璃板下，变得粉碎。我须得重温失去的事物。
en: The cool wind that blew when the sun was red and low. The voice the waves once used. The shapes that loped beside us in the snow. All crushed behind the flat glass of time. I must revisit what was los

zh: 该猎人有三分之一几率能在攻击下逃出一命，即使该攻击对其他人而言是不可阻挡的。
en: This Hunter will survive perhaps one physical assault in three, even when the attack would otherwise be unstoppable.

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 书籍是不死的记忆。
en: Books are the memory which does not die.

zh: 亚兰与她这三千多年以来所养育的众多孩子所使用的语言。
en: The language of Aram, and all her many children these three thousand years and more.

zh: 已经消失的富奇诺湖东侧曾有人居住。贺拉斯曾警告说，那里是女巫之地。而这便是她们的语言。有些人将之称作“干涸之语”，另一些人则称其为“女巫之语”。
en: A people lived east of the lost lake Fucino. Horace warned that theirs was the land of witches. This was their language, called by some 'the dry tongue' and by others 'the tongue of witches'. [Some bo

zh: 这是一门属于哲学家、神学家和英雄的语言。
en: A tongue for philosophers, theologians and heroes.

zh: 一门死去的语言：如金刚石般失去生机。
en: A dead language: dead as diamonds.

zh: 深远的沙漠深处引藏最深的关于最隐秘仪式的手迹。据说，它是蚁母其人与生俱来的语言。
en: The most secret script of the innermost rites of the deep sects of the deep desert. The birth-tongue, it is claimed, of the Mother of Ants herself. [Some books must be translated before they can be re

zh: 弗里吉亚人崇拜的“伟大母亲”就是我们所称的圣杯。他们的土地曾见证过绳结姐妹会诞生，而这门语言便是轰雷之皮飞升时用于恳求的语言。
en: The Phrygians worshipped the Great Mother, who we call the Grail. Theirs was the land in which the Sisterhood of the Knot was born, and this is the tongue in which the Thunderskin pleaded, at his asce

zh: 次大陆的神圣语言。通向四千年的诗歌、典仪和奥秘的大门如今对你敞开，只要你能从它如同莲花般卷曲的词汇中挣出一条路。
en: The holy tongue of the Subcontinent. The door to four thousand years of poetry, ceremony and mystery now opens before you, if you can struggle through the lotus convolutions of its vocabulary. [Some b

zh: 这是早于诸神自血中诞生，更早于猿人直立行走前，就能在太阳居屋里听到的语言。
en: Before gods arose from blood, before ever ape stood upright, this was the language heard in the House of the Sun.

zh: 凯勒的鸿篇巨制，一首写于无影众王宫廷的禁欲主义诗歌。
en: The grand labour of Kalle, an ascetic poet writing at the court of the Shadowless Kings.

zh: 凯勒的鸿篇巨制，一首写于无影众王宫廷的禁欲主义诗歌。
en: The grand labour of Kalle, an ascetic poet writing at the court of the Shadowless Kings.

zh: 禁酒活动家C·G·布里尔在宣传小册子里刊登了该密续的部分内容；当这本小册子变得过于流行时，人们不得不将其回收销毁。
en: C.G. Burial, the temperance campaigner, published elements of this in a pamphlet, which had to be withdrawn and destroyed after it became too popular.

zh: 禁酒活动家C·G·布里尔在宣传小册子里刊登了该密续的部分内容；当这本小册子变得过于流行时，人们不得不将其回收销毁。
en: C.G. Burial, the temperance campaigner, published elements of this in a pamphlet, which had to be withdrawn and destroyed after it became too popular.

zh: 神秘主义者布罗德纳克斯在本书中讨论了双生女巫和轰雷之皮的起源。
en: The mystic Brodnax discusses the origins of the Sister-and-Witch, and of the Thunderskin.

zh: 研究神秘学的富奇诺语文献，署名布罗德纳克斯。
en: A Fucine text attributed to the mystic, Brodnax.

zh: “我要把他们劈成两半。”
en: 'I'll break them in half.'

zh: “我搞定了工作。”
en: 'I get the job done.'

zh: “也许我们这会儿不该谈政治。”
en: 'Perhaps we shouldn't discuss politics just yet.'

zh: “我对门很在行。”
en: 'I'm good with doors. '

zh: “我感受到了。我感受到了你的与众不同。”
en: 'I see things. I see that you're different.'

zh: “很高兴与您共事！”
en: 'How delightful to be working with you!'

zh: “我搞定了工作。”
en: 'I get the job done.'

zh: 我需要去一个能保证我安全的地方——至少安全一小段时间。
en: I need to get somewhere I'll be safe - at least for a little while.

zh: 阿米拉·扎拉在本书中预言了后人称为“置闰”的事件：名为骄阳的司辰被分裂。
en: In which Amira Zahra prophesies the events later known as the Intercalate, when the Hour called Sun-in-Splendour will be divided.

zh: 阿米拉·扎拉在本书中预言了后人称为“置闰”的事件：名为骄阳的司辰被分裂。
en: In which Amira Zahra prophesies the events later known as the Intercalate, when the Hour called Sun-in-Splendour will be divided.

zh: 人人艳羡的好房子。咱们得把窗帘拉紧。
en: The envy of every passerby. Let's close the curtains tightly.

zh: 人人艳羡的好房子。咱们得把窗帘拉紧。
en: The envy of every passerby. Let's close the curtains tightly.

zh: 一个出卖我行踪的痕迹，我的追兵可能会认出来。
en: A betraying clue that my pursuers might recognise. [Reckoners appear more quickly and are more powerful when there are more Traces on the board. The Foe himself can appear once there are at least five

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 我的追兵报告说我已经离开了这座城市。在更多的搜寻者到来之前，我会有一段短暂的缓冲期。
en: My pursuer's reported that I've left the city. I'll have a brief period of grace before any more searchers arrive. [While this is present on the board, reckoners won't arrive in your current city. But

zh: 虚假但有说服力的证据证明我在某个地方，但我不在。
en: False but persuasive evidence that I'm somewhere I'm not.

zh: 即使在我这样的生活中，也有可以停下来思考的时刻：该来的，总会来。
en: Even in a life like mine, there are moments when it's possible to think: what may come, may come.

zh: 一套带注解的克里斯托弗·伊利奥波里的梦境手记，有时也被称为“唯一能读懂的密教典籍”：文笔精妙，富有趣味，扑朔迷离。
en: The annotated dream-journals of Christopher Illopoly, sometimes called 'the only readable occultist': literate, entertaining, bewildering.

zh: 一套带注解的克里斯托弗·伊利奥波里的梦境手记，有时也被称为“唯一能读懂的密教典籍”：文笔精妙，富有趣味，扑朔迷离。本书为第二卷。
en: The annotated dream-journals of Christopher Illopoly, sometimes called 'the only readable occultist': literate, entertaining, bewildering. This is the second volume.

zh: 一套带注解的克里斯托弗·伊利奥波里的梦境手记，有时也被称为“唯一能读懂的密教典籍”：文笔精妙，富有趣味，扑朔迷离。本书为第三卷。
en: The annotated dream-journals of Christopher Illopoly, sometimes called 'the only readable occultist': literate, entertaining, bewildering. This is the third volume.

zh: 由十七世纪的神秘主义者兼古董商人克劳德·海索所著，本书描述了梦境界域上的“边境”或称“下层”概念。此人也警告说，这些概念随时间变化。
en: Herein, the seventeenth-century mystic and antiquarian Claude Hersault describes the 'Bounds' or 'Underplaces' on the borders of dream. He warns that they shift over time.

zh: 崔斯坦讨厌干扰。崔斯坦有事必成。
en: Tristan dislikes distractions. Tristan gets things done.

zh: 崔斯坦讨厌干扰。崔斯坦有事必成。
en: Tristan dislikes distractions; Tristan likes results.

zh: 崔斯坦会去做那些不得不为之事。要想创造，有时必先摧毁。
en: Tristan will do what is necessary. To create, sometimes one must first destroy.

zh: 换一个时代，崔斯坦可能会是个铁匠。换一场人生，他可能是个炸弹制造师。
en: In another age, Tristan might have been a smith. In another life, he might have been a bomb-maker.

zh: 崔斯坦会尽力利用他所处的形势。
en: Tristan will make the best of his situation.

zh: 崔斯坦正在铸造一个小小的王国。有必要用火时，他就会用火。
en: Tristan is forging a little kingdom. When fire is necessary, he will use fire.

zh: 崔斯坦正在铸造一个小小的王国。有必要用火时，他就会用火。
en: Tristan is forging a little kingdom. When fire is necessary, he will use fire.

zh: 崔斯坦正在铸造一个小小的王国。有必要用火时，他就会用火。
en: Tristan is forging a little kingdom. When fire is necessary, he will use fire.

zh: 这个地方曾经风光过。
en: This place has seen better days.

zh: 本书记述了“阿斯克利庇之谜”，是一部——据推测——更古老的文献的复制本，制作者署名“李利·格里斯”。书的内容不太可信，然而其在序言即明确提及了“无墙的居屋及其所伴的林地”。
en: Descriptions of the 'Asclepian Mysteries', reproduced by one 'Rylee Grese' from - supposedly - a much older text. It's unlikely to be genuine, but it makes a reference to 'The House without Walls and

zh: 一部——据推测——更古老的文献的复制本，制作者署名“李利·格里斯”。希腊语著。
en: Reproduced by one 'Rylee Grese' from - supposedly - a much older text. In Greek.

zh: “不，他不可能伤害你；他不可能找到你。但我不用受类似的制约。”
en: 'No, He cannot harm you; He cannot find you. But I suffer no such circumscription.'

zh: 他的势力让人望而生畏；他的身份，暂时不为人知。
en: Their power is formidable; their identity, as yet unknown.

zh: 拥有宝贵知识之人。
en: This one has knowledge of value.

zh: 若你向亚兰语学者求教，你便会获得其指导。
en: A scholar of Aramaic who might be prepared to tutor you if you study with them.

zh: 若你向希腊语学者求教，你便会获得其指导。
en: A scholar of Greek who might be prepared to tutor you if you study with them.

zh: 若你向拉丁语学者求教，你便会获得其指导。
en: A scholar of Latin who might be prepared to tutor you if you study with them.

zh: 若你向梵语学者求教，你便会获得其指导。
en: A scholar of Sanskrit who might be prepared to tutor you if you study with them.

zh: 画家尼尔斯·弗雷德里克·马斯凯尔曾做过一件臭名昭著之事：试图以这些苦痛为题作画。画到第五类苦痛时，画作的欲求增长得过于急切，使得他因失血而死。所幸书中的图解没有那么详细。
en: The painter Niels Frederik Malskær, notoriously, attempted a series of paintings based on the Torments. By the Fifth Torment, the demands of the painting had grown so dire that he died of blood loss.

zh: 画家尼尔斯·弗雷德里克·马斯凯尔曾做过一件臭名昭著之事：试图以这些苦痛为题作画。画到第五类苦痛时，画作的欲求增长得过于急切，使得他因失血而死。所幸书中的图解没有那么详细。
en: The painter Niels Frederik Malskær, notoriously, attempted a series of paintings based on the Torments. By the Fifth Torment, the demands of the painting had grown so dire that he died of blood loss.

zh: 就在大战打响前夕，“亚历山大·斯派鹭”——基本确定为假名——出版了宣称“蛇身贵族”影响了国际关系的传单。名为《孪蛇密续》的文章是其主要灵感来源。
en: Just before the outbreak of the Great War, 'Alexander Sparrow' - almost certainly a pseudonym - published leaflets claiming the influence of 'Snake Princes' on world affairs. The text called the Twin-

zh: 一部尤为晦涩的密续经文。
en: A tantra shrouded in particular obscurity.

zh: Recipe aspect.
en: Recipee aspect.

zh: 事件性相
en: Recipe Aspect

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 使用次数耗尽的物品也就确实没有了价值。
en: Something with no uses is, literally, useless.

zh: 万查妮已经经营了她的事业近二十年。她从未招过助手。她的双手十分有力。
en: Valciane has operated her establishment for almost twenty years. She has never employed an assistant. Her hands are terribly strong.

zh: 万查妮具有无穷无尽的匠心，她的握力甚至比铁匠的更强。
en: Valciane's ingenuities are endless, and her grip is stronger than a blacksmith's.

zh: 万查妮并不是机器。我们有理由相信这一点，因为人们时不时会发现她犯下人为的错误。
en: Valciane is not a machine. We are reasonably confident of that, because she has been observed, now and then, to commit quite human errors.

zh: 万查妮已经开始不用工具做活了。“它们碍我的事”，她如是说。
en: Valciane has begun to work without tools. 'They get in the way,' she claims.

zh: 非常重要的一点，永远不要进入万查妮手能够到的范围。
en: It is very important never to get within arm's reach of Valciane.

zh: 万查妮用一个抄起瓶子就砸的小青年的劲头对付她的对头。
en: Valciane attends to her opposition with the remorseless brio of a youth smashing bottles.

zh: 万查妮用一个抄起瓶子就砸的小青年的劲头对付她的对头。
en: Valciane attends to her opposition with the remorseless brio of a youth smashing bottles.

zh: 万查妮用一个抄起瓶子就砸的小青年的劲头对付她的对头。
en: Valciane attends to her opposition with the remorseless brio of a youth smashing bottles.

zh: 耐心比力量重要。
en: Patience before strength.

zh: 勇气比狡诈重要。
en: Courage before cunning.

zh: 该组织崇敬刃之准则，我们特以上校和狮子匠之名拜请刃之力。
en: This organisation venerates the principle called Edge, which we call upon in the name of the Colonel and the Lionsmith... among others.

zh: 该组织崇敬铸之准则，我们特以白日铸炉本尊和昕旦之名拜请铸之力。
en: This organisation venerates the principle called Forge, which we call upon in the name of the Forge of Days herself and the Madrugad... among others.

zh: 该组织崇敬杯之准则，我们特以赤杯本尊和双生女巫之名拜请杯之力。
en: This organisation venerates the principle called Grail, which we call upon in the name of the Red Grail herself and the Sister-and-Witch... among others.

zh: 该组织崇敬心之准则，我们特以轰雷之皮和丝毧之名拜请心之力。
en: This organisation venerates the principle called Heart, which we call upon in the name of the Thunderskin and the Velvet... among others.

zh: 该组织崇敬启之准则，我们特以蚁母和弧月之名拜请启之力。
en: This organisation venerates the principle called Knock, which we call upon in the name of the Mother of Ants and the Meniscate... among others.

zh: 该组织崇敬灯之准则，我们特以瞳中扉和制花人之名拜请灯之力。
en: This organisation venerates the principle called Lantern, which we call upon in the name of the Door-in-the-Eye and the Flowermaker... among others.

zh: 该组织崇敬蛾之准则，我们特以飞蛾本尊和双生巫女之名拜请蛾之力。
en: This organisation venerates the principle called Moth, which we call upon in the name of the Moth himself and the Witch-and-Sister... among others.

zh: 该组织崇敬冬准则，我们特以残阳和双角利斧之名拜请冬之力。
en: This organisation venerates the principle called Winter, which we call upon in the name of the Sun-in-Rags and the Horned Axe... among others.

zh: 维克多是个狡黠的小伙子，他一下子就能认出稀有的机遇。
en: Victor is a sly fellow, and he knows an unusual opportunity when he sees it.

zh: 维克多是个狡黠的小伙子，但他忠诚。
en: Victor is a sly fellow, but loyal.

zh: 维克多将他的狡猾磨砺到了极点。
en: Victor has sharpened his cunning to a concentrated point.

zh: 维克多喜欢和他的目标先混熟，这样他就能在最后一刻前私下向他们致歉了。
en: Victor likes to become acquainted with his subjects, so he can apologise to them personally, just before the end.

zh: 维克多的眼睛溢满了愤怒。
en: Victor's eyes are wells of rage.

zh: 维克多已准备好迎接将可能发生的一切了，他的所有刀子都锋利如新兵。
en: Victor is ready for what may come. All his knives are keen as new soldiers.

zh: 维克多已准备好迎接将可能发生的一切了，他的所有刀子都锋利如新兵。
en: Victor is ready for what may come. All his knives are keen as new soldiers.

zh: 维克多已准备好迎接将可能发生的一切了，他的所有刀子都锋利如新兵。
en: Victor is ready for what may come. All his knives are keen as new soldiers.

zh: 妮可·梵·德里尔描述了维也纳发生的怪事——儿童消失、寄生虫大肆传染、动物被切掉肢体、关于蠕虫的噩梦、一个为穷人提供丧葬费，名为“新生丽姬亚”的慈善组织。她指出了这些现象间怪异的联系。
en: Nico Van Driel describes odd events in Vienna - the disappearance of children; epidemics of parasite activity; animal mutilations; nightmares of worms; the activities of a charitable organisation, the

zh: 如果没有某些迫在眉睫的实际困难的话，我可以在这里幸福地过一辈子……
en: I could live out my days here very happily, if it weren't for certain immediate practical difficulties...

zh: 埃弗莱特·文赞特从安纳托利亚的一处悬崖誊下了这份铭文，他因在那里被闪电击中后幸存而闻名遐迩。他死于七年后的同一天。传说称，在他死后，他的骨头仍然触手火热。
en: Everett Vinzant transcribed these from a cliff in Anatolia, where he famously survived a lightning-strike. He died seven years later to the day. His bones, the legend has it, were hot to the touch.

zh: 埃弗莱特·文赞特从安纳托利亚的一处悬崖誊下了这份铭文，他因在那里被闪电击中后幸存而闻名遐迩。他死于七年后的同一天。传说称，在他死后，他的骨头仍然触手火热。
en: Everett Vinzant transcribed these Phrygian incriptions from a cliff in Anatolia, where he famously survived a lightning-strike. He died seven years later to the day. His bones, the legend has it, were

zh: 维奥莱特总是不遗余力地贡献出自己的时间，去缓解垂死之人的痛苦。
en: Violet has always given unstintingly of her time, easing the pain of the dying.

zh: 维奥莱特喜欢画画。她很有天赋，尽管她一直非常谦逊。她的画中从来不会出现人类的形象。世上的人已经够多了。
en: Violet likes to paint. She has a gift, although she's always been very modest about it. She never puts human figures in her paintings. There are enough people in the world.

zh: 维奥莱特总是很小心。
en: Violet is always very careful.

zh: 现在去睡吧。什么都不要担心。
en: Sleep, now. Don't worry about a thing.

zh: 维奥莱特坚称她没事。看来她没事。
en: Violet insists that she's fine. She's fine.

zh: 维奥莱特找到了新的爱好。
en: Violet has found new hobbies.

zh: 维奥莱特找到了新的爱好。
en: Violet has found new hobbies.

zh: 维奥莱特找到了新的爱好。
en: Violet has found new hobbies.

zh: 客人聚齐！
en: Guests assemble!

zh: 一本类似游记的书。
en: A travelogue of sorts.

zh: 一名腓尼基时代晚期的船长不可思议的旅行记。本书自有记录的历史以来一直遭到封锁：据说读过的人会失踪。
en: The impossible travels of a sea-captain of the late Phoenician era. Suppressed throughout recorded history: those who read it were said to disappear.

zh: 副标题为“发生在墙壁背后的故事”：非常规历史之杂录，威廉·戈尔编纂。书中记有数个不广为人知的战役和从未载于地图的国家，文风戏谑，明显带有虚构成分。
en: Subtitled 'Stories from Behind the Walls': a miscellany of non-traditional histories compiled by William Gore, including battles not generally acknowledged and countries which appear on no map, told i

zh: 这里空间足够大，虽然别的优点就没多少了。
en: There's plenty of space here, although not much else to recommend it.

zh: 威利斯福特在书中详尽描述了“路权战争”——一桩发生在非我们所在的历史中的事件。然而本书被去掉了大量书页；出版者的名字遭人涂黑；封面上还钉着一张语意不祥的纸条告知读者，威利斯福特已被“抹除”。
en: Willis-Ford describes the War of the Roads, an event in a History other than our own, in detail. But numerous pages have been removed; the name of the publisher is blacked out; and an ominous slip sta

zh: 此人变得小心谨慎，不会再亲自见我了。
en: This one has grown cautious. They will not meet with me in person again.

zh: 有些记忆是描绘梦境的草图。但有些记忆穿过了梦的边境，抵达了林地和其上的居屋。
en: Some memories are sketch-maps of dream. Some even pass through the Bounds of dream, to the Wood and the House that rises above it.

zh: 在梦中，我穿过了纯白之门，又名白骨之门、象牙之门。每当我由此踏入居屋，我的声音都会留在其外。
en: In my dreams, I've passed the White Door, which has been called the Bone Door and the Gate of Ivory. My voice remains outside the House each time I enter.

zh: 我的大敌容易被这一样事物干扰……这或许能帮助我决定在哪儿面对他最好。
en: My Foe is vulnerable to this particular distraction... which might help me decide where best to face him.

zh: 我的大敌容易被特定事物干扰。
en: My Foe is vulnerable to certain distractions.

zh: “这并非高贵之举。我们不会让随便哪个小屁孩蹿上居屋的席位。”
en: 'This is no Noble Endeavour. We'll have no Jack-come-quickly jumping up the House ranks.'

zh: 他的势力让人望而生畏；他的身份，暂时不为人知。
en: Their power is formidable; their identity, as yet unknown.

zh: “斯佩斯”——或许是某个学徒，或许是某个学者结社——根据有限的证据推测出了一些秘密事件，其准确度颇为可信。
en: 'The Speeth' - perhaps a single adept, perhaps a council of scholars - hypothesises secret events from limited evidence with persuasive accuracy.

zh: 以伐诃语写就，讨论了浪游旅人的行程。
en: A discussion in Vak of the Vagabond's travels.

zh: 到野外去。这只该作为我最后的应急手段。
en: Out into the wilds. This should be my last resort.

zh: “棕色”约书亚在哈马城中的一口井里找到了本书。有不可信的传说称本书为亚历山大从伊苏斯撤退时所弃。
en: 'Brown' Joshua found this in a well in Hamath. There's an unlikely story that it was abandoned by Alexander during his retreat from Issus.

zh: “棕色”约书亚在哈马城中的一口井里找到了本书。有不可信的传说称本书为亚历山大从伊苏斯撤退时所弃。
en: 'Brown' Joshua found this in a well in Hamath. There's an unlikely story that it was abandoned by Alexander during his retreat from Issus.

zh: 如果我必须把它换成现金，它会值多少钱？
en: If I had to convert this into cash, how much would it be worth?

zh: 七道伤口便会让我完蛋。
en: Seven of these will end me.

zh: 再多六道伤口我就完蛋了。这个伤口不采取非常措施是无法愈合的。
en: Six more wounds will end me. This wound will not heal without extraordinary measures.

zh: 七道伤口便会让我完蛋。
en: Seven of these will end me.

zh: 七道伤口便会让我的大敌完蛋。
en: Seven of these will end my enemy.

zh: 七道伤口便会让我的大敌完蛋。
en: Seven wounds will end my enemy.

zh: 两道伤口就能干掉大多数清算人，四道则会杀死小头目。
en: Two Wounds will kill most reckoners. Four will kill an Underboss.

zh: 两道伤口就能干掉大多数清算人，四道则会杀死小头目。
en: Two Wounds will kill most reckoners. Four will kill an Underboss.

zh: 某人慷慨捐赠的一年，至少灰烬账簿上是这样写的。
en: A year of generously donated life, as entered in the Cindered Tally.

zh: 伊莎贝特总在月光下感到最自在。她拥有绝佳的夜间视力。而在日光下的她经常被人忽视。
en: Ysabet has always been most comfortable by moonlight. She has excellent night vision. She is often overlooked in daylight.

zh: 伊莎贝特的睡眠断断续续。她会在黄昏醒来，去做一个名叫伊莎贝特之人该做的事。
en: Ysabet sleeps in fits and starts. She rises at dusk to do the things an Ysabet does.

zh: 伊莎贝特刚才就在此处，就在片刻前。就是此处。几乎是确定的。
en: Ysabet was just here, just a moment ago. Just here. Almost certainly.

zh: 如今伊莎贝特绝不说谎。她也不需要。
en: Ysabet never lies, now. She doesn't need to.

zh: 若说伊莎贝特是在哭泣，她也只是用她那干涸的双眼在哭。
en: If Ysabet is weeping, she does so with dry eyes.

zh: 伊莎贝特在没人能看到她的地方等待着。
en: Ysabet waits where she cannot be seen.

zh: 伊莎贝特在没人能看到她的地方等待着。
en: Ysabet waits where she cannot be seen.

zh: 伊莎贝特在没人能看到她的地方等待着。
en: Ysabet waits where she cannot be seen.

zh: 扎迦利的推理同往常一样确凿无疑，但现在，其论调变得更冷冽了。
en: Zachary's reasoning is as certain as ever, but now, it is chillier.

zh: 扎迦利已选定了自己的结尾。
en: Zachary has chosen his ending.

zh: 扎迦利总是遵循自己的理性，不论它引向何处；如今，它将他引出防剿局，带进了严寒的黎明。
en: Zachary has always followed his reasoning wherever it leads: and now, it has led him out of the Suppression Bureau, into a bitter dawn.

zh: 扎迦利在一步步接近自己无可避免的终局。
en: Zachary approaches his inevitable conclusion.

zh: 祖尔菲亚留下了一张字迹潦草的纸条，上面写着她还要“在林地里散会儿步”。
en: Zulfiya has left a scrawled note indicating that she will be 'walking the Wood' for some time yet.

zh: 祖尔菲亚的剪刀从不离身。
en: Zulfiya never goes anywhere without her scissors.

zh: 为了恢复体力，欧索丽娜需要独处的时间，需要亲近大地。
en: To recover her strength, Orsolina needs time alone and close to the earth.

zh: 一个目光坚定、肌肉发达的女人。她曾为狮子匠服务，但在那之后她的食谱发生了变化。不过，她仍然保留了一些从前的力量。
en: A muscular woman with a steady gaze. Her diet has changed since her days in the Lionsmith's service, but she still retains some of her old strength.

zh: 瓦西尔已经耗尽了力气，体形也随之软化。现在他全身滴着水，呱呱地叫着，躲到温暖的地方去了。
en: Vasil's exertions have softened his shape. For now he has retreated, dripping and cawing, to somewhere warmer.

zh: 一个丝绒般俊美的男人。他血统中非人类的部分并不明显，除非你特别在意他的手指为什么异常灵活、或者为什么他总是在流鼻涕。
en: A smoothly handsome man whose unhuman ancestry is not apparent, unless you know to look for his abnormally flexible fingers, or wonder why his nose tends to run.

zh: 多明卡斯去火炉边补充体力了——还带上了一把铲子。
en: Dominykas has left to replenish himself, at the hearth and with the shovel.

zh: 多明卡斯的脸上随时都带着微笑。不幸的是，他有个总是剔牙的习惯。
en: Dominykas has a ready smile and an unfortunate habit of picking his teeth.

zh: 柴玛藏身于只有她自己知道的黑暗角落，正在舔舐伤口。
en: Chaima has gone wherever she goes to lick her wounds in darkness.

zh: 一个身躯灵巧、面容严肃的柏柏尔女人。灰棕色的条纹长袍为她增添了些法国-摩洛哥式的风情。
en: A lithe, hard-faced Berber woman in the grey-brown striped jellaba of a French-Moroccan auxiliary.

zh: 乔吉奥说自己必须躲起来，直到他的神经恢复。
en: Giorgiou is in hiding until (he says) his nerves have recovered.

zh: 一个神情忧郁、带着些哲学家气质的走私商人。他走私稀有的香烟，稀有的毒药和更稀有的武器。
en: A melancholy and philosophical smuggler of rare cigarettes, rare poisons and rarer weapons.

zh: 米蕾娅躲在床单底下瑟瑟发抖。
en: Mireya is hiding, shivering, beneath her coverlet.

zh: 一个胖乎乎的、有着明亮眼睛的女人。她的神情如在梦中一般恍惚，又总是吞吞吐吐，犹豫不决。
en: A plump, dreamy, hesitant woman with very bright eyes.

zh: 在得到新的补给品之前，雅宁斯暂时歇业。
en: Jannings has retired from the field until his new supplies arrive.

zh: 他是拆解和装配方面的专家。
en: A specialist in disassembly and also in assembly.

zh: 此举将以死亡告终——他们的，或我们的。
en: This will end in a death - theirs, or ours.

zh: 追随者将被囚禁。
en: Followers will be imprisoned.

zh: 我或许能够找到提供资助的人。
en: I might find someone who could offer assistance.

zh: 你的学生、合伙人、护卫、拥护者、被你欺骗、被你当作祭品的……伴侣。
en: Student, helpmeet, defender, champion, dupe, sacrifice... companion.

zh: 我为这位追随者授予了礼物。
en: I have honoured this follower with a gift.

zh: 浮士德：我以为地狱只是个传说。梅菲斯托费勒斯：嗳，尽管这么想吧，经历会改变你的看法。
en: FAUSTUS: I think Hell's a fable. MEPHISTOPHILIS: Ay, think so still, till experience change thy mind.

zh: 并非所有门洞都是创口，但所有创口都是门洞。
en: Not all doors are wounds; but all wounds are doors.

zh: 更近一步走向日落，已看到夜晚的初星。
en: Step closer to the sunset, and see the first stars of night.

zh: 此人因为我办事而受了伤。
en: This one has suffered in my service.

zh: 通过交易，我令此人短时间为我效力。
en: I have negotiated this one's service, for a little while.

zh: 我参与的社交圈子中的显要之人。
en: A figure of note in the circles where I move.

zh: 此仪式将复现太阳乖蹇的命运。愚蠢的学徒会在迫不得已时使用此仪式。
en: This rite recapitulates the Sun's abominable fate. It is used in extreme times by foolish adepts.

zh: 助手须以真实情感啼哭，洒泪于存留影响之处。如果一切顺利，残阳会耗尽这份影响，告知一种结尾，并提供帮助。
en: The assistant must cry tears of genuine emotion, which are asperged in the place where the influence lies. If all is done well, the Sun-in-Rags will consume the influence, acknowledge an ending, and l

zh: 助手是献给蚁母的祭品。不过蚁母对治疗和下毒同等了解，且她将宽仁待我，接受次一级的祭品作为替代。通常是这样。这一经验并不能给助手带来宽慰。
en: The assistant is offered in sacrifice to the Mother of Ants. But the Mother knows healing as well as poison, and she will be merciful, accepting the substitute of a lesser offering. Usually. The exper

zh: 此仪式将贯彻白日铸炉的一项教导：工匠只有摧毁他最珍视的工具后，方能达成至高成就。狮子匠正是因此在上校面前砸碎了自己的剑。所以我的助手也必须摧毁一件器具。
en: This rite enacts a teaching of the Forge of Days: the artisan may achieve their highest goal only by destroying their most precious tool. The Lionsmith knew this when he shattered his sword before the

zh: 助手的名字在法力显现之地，或之时，被从他头上夺去；而他也被裂为九份。他曾像畜类一样遭到追猎，但现在时移势迁。
en: The assistant's name is taken from them in a place, or on an occasion, of power; and they are divided into nine parts. They used to be hunted like an animal, too, but times changed.

zh: 此仪式需用存有法力的器具将鲜血点燃：火焰是世界表皮上的破损。执行此仪式的助手极少能全身而退。
en: This rite uses an instrument of power to set the blood afire: fire is the lesion in the skin of the world. The assistant rarely survives unharmed.

zh: 此仪式将唤起守夜人所失去的事物，他常行走于无墙的居屋。（光在其前，影在其后。绝不能让他看到你。）用存有法力的器具召唤他取走祭品：存在于你思维根源的珍贵的知识。必须有助手为你吟诵稳固神智的话语，所以请选择一个信赖的人。
en: This rite recalls the loss of the Watchman, who walks the house which has no walls. (Light goes before him and shadow behind. He must not see you.) The instrument of power is used to summon him to tak

zh: 我并非无形之术的学徒，但在我的职业生涯中，我曾经不得不杀死过几个这样的人。人就是要边干边学。
en: I'm no adept, but I've had to kill a few adepts in my time. You pick things up as you go.

zh: 我们的祖先铸造刀剑、教授武艺、在战争前夕咒骂——这些行为都遵循相同的规律。
en: When our ancestors forged swords, taught the arts of martial movement, spoke curses on the eve of battle - all these things shared certain patterns. [Use this in a Rite with a corpse and Winter power,

zh: “千夫长在悠久的过去中学到了有关于鲜血和伤口的刻骨故事。刀剑嘶鸣声，正大声诉说着这个故事。” 它比听起来更实用。
en: 'This is the bone-deep story the thousandman learnt in antiquity, of blood and its exits. To speak it aloud is to hear the sword hiss.' It's more practical than it sounds.

zh: 比德说过：“血可再生。时无再还。”比德知道自己是什么。
en: Biedde said: 'Blood can be replenished. Time cannot.' Biedde knew what he was about.

zh: “这是伟大者之子在折断其刀刃时得到的教诲。即使是现在，它也像波斯阳光下的金属碎片一样闪闪发光。”它比听起来更实用。
en: 'This is the lesson the Great One's Son found in the breaking of his blade. Even now it glints like shards of metal in the Persian sun.' It's more practical than it sounds.

zh: 千夫长在悠久的过去中学到了有关于鲜血和伤口的刻骨故事。刀剑嘶鸣声，正大声诉说着这个故事。
en: This is the bone-deep story the thousandman learnt in antiquity, of blood and its exits. To speak it aloud is to hear the sword hiss. [Use this in a Rite with a corpse and Winter power, and the corpse

zh: 一套严格的训练：将皮肤武装起来，使每一根手指都化作致命的矛尖。
en: A set of exacting exercises that will armour the skin and make each digit lethal as a spear-point.

zh: “在勇士倒下的地方，多翼林鸮聚集在一起，纪念他们的肉身。在英雄倒下的地方，多翼者聚集在一起，把他们的灵魂引领到司辰面前。”我不想倒下，所以我学会了林鸮保护自己的方法。
en: 'Where warriors fall, the many-winged striges gather to honour their flesh. Where heroes fall, the Many-Winged Ones gather to convey their souls before the Hours.' I don't intend to fall, so I've lear

zh: 一系列需要为之献身、为之惊骇的神秘活动。
en: A series of mystic exercises that require both dedication and terror.

zh: 一系列需要为之献身、为之惊骇的神秘活动。
en: A series of mystic exercises that requires both dedication and terror.

zh: 上校在不同的时代有着不同的形象。他目盲；他耳聋；他不受创伤；他不容违抗。他的具名者皆如极年迈之人一般狡诈。
en: The Colonel has been many things in many ages. He is blind; he is deaf; he cannot be wounded; he cannot be denied. His Names are cunning as only the very old can be.

zh: “裂分之狼具三性：毁灭性，毁灭性，以及，毁灭性。” 学习司辰的技艺不代表你必须喜爱它。
en: 'Three natures hath the Wolf Divided; he unmaketh; he unmaketh; at the last, he unmaketh.' You don't have to like an Hour to learn from it.

zh: 狮子匠，按司辰的标准来看尚显青涩，而按战争的标准来看相当老成。他的具名者皆是刚被招募的新成员，其吟诵有一种野蛮的力量。
en: The Lionsmith is young by the standards of the Hours, but old by the standards of war. His Names are recent recruits, and their recitation has a savage power.

zh: 上校在不同的时代有着不同的形象。他目盲；他耳聋；他不受创伤；他不容违抗。他的具名者皆如极年迈之人一般狡诈。
en: The Colonel has been many things in many ages. He is blind; he is deaf; he cannot be wounded; he cannot be denied. His Names are cunning as only the very old can be.

zh: 七七四十九种裂肉之技。
en: Seven times seven techniques for the sundering of flesh.

zh: 七七四十九种毁灭之术。
en: Seven times seven techniques of unmaking.

zh: 七七四十九种碎骨之法。
en: Seven times seven techniques for the shattering of bone.

zh: 狮子匠，按司辰的标准来看尚显青涩，而按战争的标准来看相当老成。他的具名者皆是刚被招募的新成员，其吟诵有一种野蛮的力量。
en: The Lionsmith is young by the standards of the Hours, but old by the standards of war. His Names are recent recruits, and their recitation has a savage power.

zh: 如果我要成为一柄利器，我就必须知晓自己的灵魂在何处被其经历强化，以及我的极限在何时会变得至关重要。
en: If I am to be a weapon, I must know where my soul is reinforced by its history, and where my limits become fundamental.

zh: 此为确保终结的技艺。
en: These are the arts that ensure an ending.

zh: 我是一柄利器，注定先被摧毁、后被重铸，直至锋锐无瑕。我以筋骨与人争辩，终将立于不败之地。
en: I am the weapon that will be broken, and remade again without flaw. The argument of my flesh will at last be irrefragable.

zh: 此为确保终结的技艺。
en: These are the arts that ensure an ending.

zh: 狡诈乃胜利的根基。经验乃狡诈的根基。没有痛苦就没有经验，而伤疤是痛苦的外在表现。
en: Cunning is the root of victory. Experience is the root of cunning. There can be no experience without suffering, and a scar is suffering given form.

zh: 上校和狮子匠是肉源之神，但是裂分之狼是血源之神。狡诈、力气与痛苦乃胜利的三大根基，下文即其秘密教义……
en: The Colonel and the Lionsmith are gods-who-were-flesh, but the Wolf-Divided is a god-who-was-blood. Cunning, Strength and Agony are the three roots of victory, and here is their secret doctrine...

zh: 每次重生都伴随更强的力量。被毁之物可被重铸。未毁之物必被重铸。
en: In each rebirth there is greater strength. What has been broken can be reforged. What has never been broken must be reforged.

zh: 上校和狮子匠是肉源之神，但是裂分之狼是血源之神。狡诈、力气与痛苦乃胜利的三大根基，下文即其秘密教义……
en: The Colonel and the Lionsmith are gods-who-were-flesh, but the Wolf-Divided is a god-who-was-blood. Cunning, Strength and Agony are the three roots of victory, and here is their secret doctrine...

zh: 在五块大陆上，铁匠对钢铁低语相同的字句。众所周知，杀人犯也会低语这些字句。而学徒自然也是如此。在仪式中念出这些字句可激发出无情的改变。
en: In five continents, smiths have whispered the same words to the iron. Murderers have been known to whisper these words, too. And adepts, of course. These words are spoken in ritual to inspire an unmer

zh: 当我们注视火焰时，我们所视何物？我们找到此物时，它会说出：一个赋予转变神性的词汇，此改变随烧焦的皮肤剥落而出现。
en: When we watch a fire, what are we watching for? When we find it, these are the words it will speak: a word that sanctifies the change that comes when the seared skin peels.

zh: 当改变来临之际，必须运用恰当的知识。
en: The proper words must be employed when the Change comes.

zh: 铸之具名者都念诵过这些话语。
en: The Names of the Forge have all spoken these words.

zh: 破坏一样东西，你就能得到片段。破坏那些片段，你就能得到尘埃。破坏尘埃，然后再破坏它的残余。就这样，你得到了火。
en: Break a thing, and you have fragments. Break those fragments, and you have dust. Break the dust, and then break what remains. Here is fire. [Use this in a Rite with a Consecrated Lintel and a little R

zh: 这是热力向钢铁吟唱的赞歌。如果我们如实模仿，会使嘴唇干裂，舌头脱水。但是，哦！回报多么丰厚。
en: This is the hymn the heat sings to the iron. If we imitate it faithfully enough we will parch our lips and crisp our tongue, but O, the rewards. [Use this in a Rite with a Consecrated Lintel and a lit

zh: 白日铸炉、弧月和昕旦是光源之神。汞、银和金乃功业的种穰，下文即其秘密教义……
en: The Forge of Days, the Meniscate and the Madrugad are gods-from-Light. Mercury, silver and gold are the seeds of the Work, and here is their secret doctrine... [Use this in a Rite with a Consecrated L

zh: 只有使用了适当的墨水，一些字词才能拼写正确。
en: Some words are spelt correctly only when the proper ink is used.

zh: 伟大母亲永记不忘。
en: The Great Mother remembers.

zh: 多么悦耳啊。让人想要一遍又一遍地聆听。正是这些话语使献祭变得甘甜。
en: So pleasant upon the ear. One could listen over and over. These are the words that make sacrifice sweet. [Use this with Byzantine Tinct and a Moth influence - or other resources of equal power - to su

zh: 杯之具名者的典仪进程有刺耳尖叫声的参与。我们要尽可能地重现这些声音，以求得其所提供的搏动之力。
en: Raucous shrieks attend the passage of the Names of the Grail. We reproduce them as best we can, for the pulsing power they lend. [Use this with Byzantine Tinct and a Moth influence - or other resource

zh: 有些快感如此强烈，足以侵蚀现实世界的法则。
en: There exist some pleasures intense enough to corrupt the laws of the physical world. [This formula can be used in a Rite with Bitterblack Salts and a Subtle Rupture - or similar sources of power - to

zh: 制花人无法碰触你；他无法找到你；他永远拥有你欲求的东西。哪怕是细述他作品之精巧的言论也能引起众司辰注意。
en: The Flowermaker cannot touch you; he cannot find you; he always has what you desire. Even an elaboration of the properties of his works can draw the attention of Hours. [This formula can be used in a

zh: 圣杯是第一位血源之神。制花人来自于光。拾滩鸦曾是肉身。“饥饿”、“贪婪”和“渴求”乃食欲的苗床，下文即其秘密教义……
en: The Grail was the first god-who-was-blood. The Flowermaker came from Light. The Beach-Crow was flesh. Hunger and Greed and Yearning are the matrix of appetite, and here is their secret doctrine...  [U

zh: 每一声雷霆都蕴含着共通的情绪。让我们向它们致意吧。
en: There are common sentiments in every thunderclap. Let us acknowledge them.

zh: 这个法术的音节扣人心弦。我发现自己正跟随节拍打着响指。它与我都不想停歇。
en: The syllables of this formula are compelling. I find myself snapping my fingers to its rhythm. It desires not to cease.

zh: 谁听后能依然无动于衷？沉睡之人？离世之人？大地与天空？
en: Who could hear this and remain unmoved? The sleeping? The dead? The earth and the sky? [Use this in a Rite with Byzantine Tinct and a Meteoric Bullet - or other resources of equal power - to summon a

zh: 人们总是祈求双生女巫的护佑，于十字路口，于月初时分，于陆地庇护大海之处。
en: The Sister-and-Witch has always been invoked for protection, at crossroads and at moonrise and where the land shelters the sea. [Use this in a Rite with Byzantine Tinct and a Meteoric Bullet - or othe

zh: 守夜者不入眠，入眠者不守夜。守夜者不入眠，入眠者不守夜。
en: One who watches does not sleep, one who sleeps watches not. One who watches does not sleep, one who sleeps watches not. [Use this in a Rite with Byzantine Tinct and a Meteoric Bullet - or other resour

zh: 丝毧总以否定作答，然而我们能在那声否定中得到荫蔽，就像野兽藏于树杈间。
en: The Velvet always answers 'No', but we can shelter in that 'No' like beasts in the crook of a tree. [Use this in a Rite with Byzantine Tinct and a Meteoric Bullet - or other resources of equal power -

zh: 圣杯将轰雷之皮从肉体撕下。双生女巫生于两个子宫。丝毧觉醒于在林地根部尝到血的味道时。心脏乃鲜血之鼓，下文即其秘密教义……
en: The Grail ripped the Thunderskin from flesh. The Sister-and-Witch was born from two wombs. The Velvet woke when the Wood-roots tasted blood. The Heart is Blood's Drum, and here is its secret doctrine.

zh: 每个锁匠迟早会做梦。现在我们来背诵它的七个阶段吧……
en: Sooner or later, every locksmith has the Dream. Now we recite its seven phases...

zh: 漫宿的每一道门在开启前都需要献祭。伊库维姆诸仪式则规定了献祭的方式。
en: Every door in the Mansus requires its sacrifice before it will open. The Iguvine Rites prescribe the manner of the sacrifice. [Nearly every summoning Rite requires Knock influence. This lore will prov

zh: 要开启某些道路，人必须先开启自己。此种修炼将以蚁母之名的开启之法概括其中。
en: To open certain Ways, one must first open oneself. This practice outlines that opening, in the name of the Mother of Ants. [Nearly every summoning Rite requires Knock influence. This lore will provide

zh: 当月亮的脸上添了伤口，这些字句便出现了。
en: When the moon's face was wounded, these words appeared.

zh: 蛇是不容拒绝的访客。
en: Serpents are the visitors which may not be denied.

zh: 仍有一位石源之神留存，她看守着所有的门关。
en: One god-who-was-stone remains, and she watches all thresholds.

zh: 蚁母是两河的孩子。双角斧是最后的石源之神。弧月源于光，生于月亮。伤口、门槛和揭示皆乃门的面相，下文即其秘密教义……
en: The Mother of Ants is the child of two rivers. The Horned-Axe is the last god-from-Stone. The Meniscate was born in the Moon from Light. The Wound and the Threshold and the Revelation are all the Gate

zh: 其表述如下：“司辰各有其颜色，但颜色只存在于有光的地方。”
en: It has been expressed like this: 'Each Hour has its colour, but colour exists only where there is light.'

zh: 诗章的只言片语；关于我们所有人在一生中都至少要造访两次的某座房屋的一次回忆。对于第一次来到漫宿的人来说，此密传几乎是必不可少的。
en: A snatch of poetry; a single memory of a certain house that all of us visit at least twice in our lives. For one's first visit to the Mansus, this lore is almost essential.

zh: “仁慈，”守夜人道，“仅能在影中觅得。”
en: 'Mercy', saith the Watchman, 'is found only in shadow.'

zh: 瞳中扉指引前路；他并不仁慈；然而从始至今，他一直在照明驱暗。
en: The Door-in-the-Eye navigates; he is not merciful; but first and always, he illuminates. [Use this in a Rite with a Knock tool and a Thrilling Air - or other resources of equal power - to summon a Hin

zh: 聚点于漫宿之灯处守候。它又被称作道路之室，还有结尾之室。其错综复杂或许超出了人类的理解范围，但这些法术可以对其进行基本的近似。
en: The Concursum waits at the Lantern of the Mansus. It has been called the Chamber of Ways and also the Chamber of Endings. Its intricacies are probably beyond human comprehension, but these formulae ar

zh: 刀刃阶梯是守夜人最血腥的考验。行过此梯且已死去之人都受到了残阳的接待。而活着的行过此梯之人都距辉光近了一点点。
en: The Ascent of Knives is the bloodiest of the Watchman's tests. Those who pass it and are dead enter the service of the Sun-in-Rags. Those living who pass it are a little closer to the Glory. [Use this

zh: 弧月、昕旦、制花人和白日铸炉是光源之神。尚有第五位，但现在我们知道那不是守夜人。不过守夜人仍与前五者共用一条秘密教义，下文即此秘密教义……
en: The Meniscate, the Madrugad, the Flowermaker and the Forge of Days are the gods-from-Light. There is a fifth, but now we know it is not the Watchman. Yet the Watchman shares a secret doctrine with tho

zh: 一位林地之神乐于将缕缕发丝从头皮上分离。以求关注，则将之焚烧。以求机遇，则将之埋葬。
en: A power of the Wood enjoys the separation of the lock from the scalp. For attention, burn it. For opportunity, bury it. [Speak this over a corpse in a Rite, with a Winter influence present, and the co

zh: 夜不能寐，侧耳聆听。微风穿梭于枝桠间，窃窃私语。房屋沉溺于自己的梦中，大声呼号。这皆是混沌所经之路。
en: Lie awake, and listen. The wind speaks in the branches. The house cries out in its sleep. These are the roads that chaos rides. [Speak this over a corpse in a Rite, with a Winter influence present, an

zh: 蜕衣舞者的谜语是：“何物会失去？”每个蜕衣舞者的寓言都是回答谜语的一次尝试。
en: The Ecdysiast's Riddle is 'What may be lost?' Each Ecdysiast's Parable is an attempt to answer the Riddle.

zh: 鼹鼠的头顶覆满青苔，双脚深陷泥土，且使树木被紧锁住。此文是鼹鼠给你的警告。
en: Moldywarp keeps her crowns in the moss and her feet in the dirt and the trees locked jaw-tight. Moldywarp has these warnings for you.

zh: 蜈蚣自沙中来，往根下去。她找到了一个新家，但现在永远也回不去了。她在除此之外的所有地方爬过：锁眼，诸历史，还有生育的、视觉的、听觉的通道。此文是她要说的话。
en: The Centipede came up out of the sands and went in under the roots. She found a new home but now she can't go home. She creeps in everywhere else: keyholes and histories and the channels of birth and

zh: 藤冠的飞蛾王在死去的轰雷王的大腿处孵化。饮尽他腹部的淋巴液。上述为你将看到的景象。
en: Vine-crowned moth-king hatched in the thigh of the thunder-king who's dead. Drink up his belly-lymph. These are the sights you'll see.

zh: 真正的司辰不会面见虚源之神，环杉却在林地茂盛处与树中牝马会面。她们的交合是被禁止的，而被禁止的事物拥有力量。此即他们交合的故事。
en: The real Hours don't meet the gods-from-Nowhere, but the Ring-Yew meets the Mare-in-the-Tree in where the Wood grows lush. Their couplings are forbidden, and forbidden things have power, and this is t

zh: 隐秘的历史被掩盖于我们所知的历史之下，就像珍贵红酒的气味。这便是其中一重历史中的一个细节。
en: Secret histories are layered beneath the one we know, like the notes in rare wine. This is a detail from one of those histories. [Exploring with this scrap of knowledge may uncover secret locations in

zh: 从毗邻的故事中悄然透露出的一个细节。不要在可能被窃听的地方谈论这个。
en: A detail smuggled in from an adjacent narrative. Don't speak this where you may be overheard.

zh: 一段故事，有关于一座没有大门的城市，一位非曾出生的女王，一场非曾打起的战争。
en: A story of a city without a gate, of a queen who was not born, of a war which was not fought.

zh: 众司辰被最为血腥的战争吸引了注意。而后，重重历史被编结如发辫。
en: The attention of the Hours is drawn to the bloodiest wars. Afterwards, the Histories are braided like hair. [Exploring with this scrap of knowledge may uncover secret locations in the Land Beyond the

zh: 在漫宿中，司辰互相抗衡。纷争平定后，祂们消解了不可能之事，歌颂了可能之事，将纠缠交错的已成之事细密编织成如金色丝带般的未来。万事落定，历史成为过去。然而例外依然存在。
en: In the Mansus, the Hours strive one against another. As the struggles are resolved, they iron out the impossible, exalt the possible, tie the fraying braids of what has been into one golden ribbon of

zh: 浪游旅人是一位无法踏足漫宿的司辰，但她能前往其他任意地点……并且，如果你相信她的自夸，她还是唯一知晓每重历史的人。有时候，她会让某人记录下她的行踪。
en: The Vagabond is the one Hour who can never enter the Mansus, but she goes everywhere else... and, if you believe her boasts, she's the only one who knows all the Histories. Sometimes, she lets someone

zh: 午港是长生者享受大海与阳光，饮下泉水被世界遗忘，躲避纷扰，或者说躲避大部分纷扰之处。你能在希耶尔沃旅店听到的故事，就像一杯将回忆五次蒸馏、不加冰块的鸡尾酒。
en: Port Noon is where the Long go to enjoy the sea and the sun, to drink from the Spring that makes them forgotten, and to stay out of trouble. Mostly out of trouble. The stories you can hear in the Hote

zh: 据说，某种特定的知识只能通过特定种类的沉默来表达。那些淘气的注释者建议你闭着眼睛阅读这些知识。
en: Certain knowledge, it is said, can be expressed only through the particular quality of a silence. It has been suggested that one can only read such knowledge with one's eyes closed, but only by mischi

zh: 当我说起它时，我的双唇不会因寒冷而结霜。每次都是如此，令人惊讶。
en: When I speak it, my lips don't crisp with frost. Each time, this is surprising.

zh: 唤起、知悉并接纳通往沉默的道路。
en: This invokes, acknowledges and welcomes the passage to silence.

zh: 有一位司掌纪念与哀悼的神灵，他已被剥夺至尽，然而他不受欺骗。你或可期待将来能亲手碾碎他，把他变成一把碎鸟骨。
en: There is a power that commemorates and grieves, from whom nothing is taken, but who cannot be deceived. You might expect to be able to crush him in your hand, into bird-bone fragments. [Use this in a

zh: 六位已逝。五位源自岩石，一位源自光。现都归于虚界。但他们的回忆仍具神力，而我们现在要拜请这种神力。
en: Six are gone. Five came from Stone and one from Light. All went to Nowhere. Still their memories have power, and now we call upon it. [Use this in a Rite with Martensite Paste and an assistant dedicat

zh: 当骄阳被分裂，它的具名者亦随之崩解。这些是曾为整体的那些具名者们的碎片。
en: When the Sun-in-Splendour was divided, its Names fractured too. Here are the shards of their names which once were whole. [Use this in a Rite with Martensite Paste and an assistant dedicated to Knock

zh: 当铸炉冷却，辉光黯淡，林地化尘，裂分之狼也许方能将息，却止于其能噬己之时。此言几道出了被分裂者的怨恨。
en: When the Forge is cold and the Glory is dark and the Wood is dust, perhaps the Wolf Divided will rest, but only until it can devour itself. This Word almost expresses the Divided One's hatred. [Speak

zh: 我正在对无形之术生出新的理解。
en: I am coming to understand something new about the invisible arts.

zh: 我正在对无形之术生出新的理解。
en: I am coming to understand something new about the invisible arts.

zh: 我正在对无形之术生出新的理解。
en: I am coming to understand something new about the invisible arts.

zh: 我正在对无形之术生出新的理解。
en: I am coming to understand something new about the invisible arts.

zh: 我正在对无形之术生出新的理解。
en: I am coming to understand something new about the invisible arts.

zh: 我很开心，大概吧。
en: I'm happy, I think.

zh: 我已经见得太多了。不知名的恐惧正在用利齿啃噬着我的希望；一种关于存在本身的的恐惧。
en: I've seen too much. A nameless gnawing fear has its teeth in my hopes; an existential horror.

zh: 光透过裂缝漏入。我的头脑比任何时候都更清晰。我升得越高，见得越多。
en: Light LEAKS through the CRACKS. My mind is BRIGHTER than it EVER was. THE HIGHER I RISE THE MORE I SEE.

zh: 某种反响、共鸣、调和。很快便会消逝。
en: An echo, a resonance, an alignment. Something that will fade soon.

zh: 五感变得敏锐。
en: The senses are sharpened.

zh: 群情激昂。
en: The passions are roused.

zh: 愤怒以各种形态彰显。
