en: Anger is implicit in every shape.

zh: 谁人能将注定的战局扭转？
en: Who can avert the finality of battle?

zh: 薄雾与微光。
en: A haze and a shimmer. [A second-order influence: apparent to the perceptive, instantly recognisable to an adept. This can be used in some Rites to summon minions, and some minions will leave it linger

zh: 迸出火花的可能性。
en: The possibility of sparks.

zh: 这是火山的边缘散发出的气息。
en: This is the aura at the volcano's edge.

zh: 改变随火焰而来。
en: Change follows fire.

zh: 所有知觉都被增强；所有感情都被加深。
en: Every sense is heightened; every sensation, deepened. [A second-order influence: apparent to the perceptive, instantly recognisable to an adept. This can be used in some Rites to summon minions, and s

zh: 难以控制自己。
en: It is difficult to control oneself.

zh: 唇舌期盼尝味，十指期盼抓扼。
en: The lips ache to savour; the fingers ache to clutch.

zh: 连空气都似果肉饱含汁水般饱含着可能性。刺一下说不定便会淌出。
en: The very air is succulent with possibility. Pierce it, and it might bleed. [A fifteenth-order influence! This might be the personal attention of an Hour. This can be used in some Rites to summon minio

zh: 轻点脚尖。轻快又舒适。
en: The toes tap. All is brisk and all is well. [A second-order influence: apparent to the perceptive, instantly recognisable to an adept. This can be used in some Rites to summon minions, and some minion

zh: 力量上涌，精神鼓舞。
en: A rising power; a reassuring energy.

zh: 世界随心而动。
en: The heart moves; the world moves.

zh: 天空为鼓，大地已经准备好接受它的痴狂。
en: The sky is a drum; the earth is ready for its ecstasy.

zh: 系带解开——窗户外敞——锁像上过油一样转动。
en: Laces uncoil -- windows swing open -- locks move as if oiled. [A second-order influence: apparent to the perceptive, instantly recognisable to an adept. This can be used in some Rites to summon minion

zh: 云朵分离——墙壁碾动——旧伤作痛。
en: Clouds part -- walls creak -- old wounds ache.

zh: 框架扭曲——门扉爆开——蛇群自土中沸腾而出！
en: Geometry contorts -- doors burst open -- snakes boil from the earth!

zh: 那里有一扇不该开启的门，现在连它的合页都在摇摇欲动。
en: There is a Door that should not open, and even now its hinges are roused -- [A fifteenth-order influence! This might be the personal attention of an Hour. This can be used in some Rites to summon mini

zh: 在世界背后，有这样一道光，凌驾于漫宿之上，能够刺透玻璃与皮肤。它出现在了这里。
en: There is a light behind the world, a light above the Mansus, a light that penetrates both glass and skin. It is present here. [A second-order influence: apparent to the perceptive, instantly recognisa

zh: 光滴落在皮肤上，弥漫在空气中。
en: Light dews the skin and suffuses the air.

zh: 每个颜色都更加明亮，仿佛新染过一样，然而现在它们全都开始流失色泽，褪至纯白。
en: Every colour has grown bright as new dye, but now all of them begin to bleed towards white. [A tenth-order influence! A gathering knot of power and peril. This can be used in some Rites to summon mini

zh: 你可以遮住双眼，随便你。遮也无用。
en: Cover your eyes, if you like. It will serve no purpose.

zh: in gi rum imus noc te et con sumi 喀嚓喀嚓喀嚓
en: in gi rum imus noc te et con sumi snip snip snip [A second-order influence: apparent to the perceptive, instantly recognisable to an adept. This can be used in some Rites to summon minions, and some m

zh: 噢，我们玩弄过的朋友，我们制造出的皮囊，依然在驱策着我们！
en: Oh the friends that we played and the skins that we made; still they drive us!

zh: 情感歪曲了我们的思想。一种激情臣服于另一种，就像被风卷起的叶子！
en: Emotions contort our thoughts. One passion yields to the next like leaves in the wind's grip! [A tenth-order influence! A gathering knot of power and peril. This can be used in some Rites to summon mi

zh: 一股特别的寒意徘徊于我的房间。
en: A peculiar chill lingers in my rooms. [A second-order influence: apparent to the perceptive, instantly recognisable to an adept. This can be used in some Rites to summon minions, and some minions will

zh: 空气冻得皮肤发脆。霜结上了玻璃。
en: The air crisps the skin. Frost marks the glass.

zh: 冬季穿透了衣物，穿透了皮肤，宿居在血管里。
en: Winter passes the clothes, passes the skin, nestles in the veins. [A tenth-order influence! A gathering knot of power and peril. This can be used in some Rites to summon minions. If unused, it will be

zh: 这个世界死寂如冰，每一步都是一段艰辛旅程。
en: The world is still as ice. Each movement is a journey. [A fifteenth-order influence! This might be the personal attention of an Hour. This can be used in some Rites to summon minions. If unused, it wi

zh: 我将承认耐心胜于蛮力，把我的刃之密传拗转为冬之密传。
en: I am acknowledging the victory of patience over strength, subverting my Edge influence to Winter influence.

zh: 我将把我的铸之密传拗转为刃之密传。
en: I am subverting my Forge influence to Edge influence.

zh: 我将把我的杯之密传拗转为蛾之密传。
en: I am subverting my Grail influence to Moth influence.

zh: 我将把我的心之密传拗转为杯之密传。
en: I am subverting my Heart influence to Grail influence.

zh: 我将把我的灯之密传拗转为铸之密传。
en: I am subverting my Lantern influence to Forge influence.

zh: 我将把我的蛾之密传拗转为灯之密传。
en: I am subverting my Moth influence to Lantern influence

zh: 我将把我的冬之密传拗转为心之密传。
en: I am subverting my Winter influence to Heart influence

zh: 我希望扩大这份刃之影响。
en: I hope to augment this Edge influence.

zh: 我希望扩大这份铸之影响。
en: I hope to augment this Forge influence.

zh: 我希望扩大这份杯之影响。
en: I hope to augment this Grail influence.

zh: 我希望扩大这份心之影响。
en: I hope to augment this Heart influence.

zh: 我希望扩大这份灯之影响。
en: I hope to augment this Lantern influence.

zh: 我希望扩大这份蛾之影响。
en: I hope to augment this Moth influence.

zh: 我将加深我对启之影响的了解。
en: I am deepening my understanding of Knock influence.

zh: 我希望扩大这份冬之影响。
en: I hope to augment this Winter influence.

zh: 此仪式将于日出或月出时分召唤拾滩鸦。拾滩鸦将接受祭品，并还之以大地的赠礼。
en: This rite calls on the Beach-Crow at sunrise or moonrise. The Crow accepts the sacrifice, and returns the gifts of the earth.

zh: 浪游旅人是唯一一位从未进过漫宿的司辰。此仪式必须在正确的地点，正确的时间进行，浪游旅人会去将这份正确占为己有。
en: The Vagabond is the only Hour that never enters the Mansus. This rite must be performed in the right place, in the right time, and the Vagabond will take that rightness for her own.

zh: 我找到了一种调和的方式。
en: I've found an alignment.

zh: 血溅当场。
en: Blood has been spilt.

zh: “我没法不去想它们。我应该休息。”
en: 'I can't stop thinking about them. I should sleep.'

zh: 我的一个信徒一触碰陌生人的皮肤就会知道一些东西，只要他的手指稍微带着些湿气。他似乎很享受这一点。
en: One of my congregation knows things when they touch the skin of strangers, as long as their fingers are a little damp. They seem to enjoy it. [A Flowermaker Cult generates a cultist if you achieve at

zh: 此人已经离开了人世。我也最好离开这里。
en: They're not here any more. I should make sure I'm not, either.

zh: 胜利的证明。这将使我的大敌非常沮丧。
en: Proof of a victory. This is going to make my Foe very upset.

zh: 碎骨内含一位更古老的司辰的回忆。
en: Within this fragment lie memories of an older Hour.

zh: 众王究竟是否知晓真正的辉光？
en: Did the Kings ever know true Glory?

zh: 残量极少，但其味道——这份出人意料的味道——
en: Very little remains, but the taste - the unheralded taste -

zh: 感官回忆和向往，绳结的秘密，存于旧自我的一块不被看好的碎片中。
en: Sense-memories and longings, the secrets of the Knot, in an unpromising scrap of old self.

zh: 里面的魂灵已去。到某个时点，它散发的臭气会重到让你不得不把它处理掉，但现在还不至于。它有可能引来注意；也可用来打动那些可打动之人。
en: The spirit is departed. At some point, the smell will be so bad you'll have to dispose of it, but here it is for now. It may attract attention; it may be used to impress the impressionable. [Even afte

zh: 她知道太阳原先的模样吗？
en: Did she know the Sun as it was?

zh: 某个知晓痛苦滋味的人。
en: This was someone who knew pain.

zh: 燃料、药膏、颜料以及耐心：全都随时间耗减。
en: Fuel, ointment, pigment, patience: all are consumed in time.

zh: 涂剑用的圣油。且有着迷人的银绿色。
en: The sacred chrysm of the sword. Also an attractive silver-green.

zh: 狻猊是狮子匠最年轻的仆从。磨成粉后，它效力依旧，却不复美丽。
en: Labhites are the youngest of the Lionsmith's servants. When rendered to powder, they retain their potency but not their beauty.

zh: 狮子匠如今甚少制造剑齿兽了；但是它们很难杀死，而且寿命极长。
en: It is rare for the Lionsmith to make skaptodons, now; but they are difficult to kill, and they live a very long time.

zh: 没有哪个普通的化学家能制造出这种黑亮的晶体——若离了铸炉的触碰。
en: No ordinary chemist could produce these dark-gleaming crystals - not without the touch of the Forge.

zh: 在铸炉的某些行动中，据说日光会驱散月光，如同我们从弧月的钟点过渡到昕旦的钟点。这种精华曾是水银，不过它现在为夏日午后的浓金色。很漂亮。
en: In certain operations of the Forge, sunlight is said to overcome moonlight, as we pass from the Meniscate to the Madrugad. This essence was mercury, once, but now it's the rich gold of a summer aftern

zh: 在铸炉的后期运转中，昕旦让位于真正的白日铸炉，这种精华也开始变红。也许有一天，变红会停止，不过现下我们还有的可用。
en: In the later operations of the Forge, the Madrugad yields to the true Forge of Days, and the essence begins to redden. Some day, perhaps, it will not, but for now we have this.

zh: 躯体死后还保留如此红的红色，并不寻常。
en: It is not usual for a red this red to survive the death of the body.

zh: 制花人的繁多赠礼之一。在更加光明的年代，它或许被赐予了永生。即使在这重枯萎的历史中，它依旧拥有无法比拟的甜腻。
en: One of the Flowermaker's many gifts. In a brighter age, it might have bestowed immortality. In this blighted History, it is still an unparalleled sweetness.

zh: 把你的手置于它残破的褶皱上，感受它的温暖；手不收回，则它将探索拥抱的可能性。
en: Lay your hand on these tattered folds and feel their warmth; leave your hand upon it, and it will explore the possibilities of embrace.

zh: 珍珠对双生巫女而言是神圣的，特别是这种颜色的。它们尤其被用在防护性的仪式中。
en: Pearls are sacred to the Witch-and-Sister, especially those of this colour. They have been used in protective rites, among others.

zh: 从女神赫卡忒在十字路受人敬拜始，这个配方从未改变过。
en: The formula remains unchanged since the days Hecate was worshipped at the crossways.

zh: 轰雷之皮在每一个过去中保护了一个人称“雅纳略”的人免遭危难。时至如今，他的血液仍在持续舞动。
en: The Thunderskin protected the one they called Januarius, against perils from every past. To this day his blood keeps dancing.

zh: 这种深奥的紫色只在“推罗紫”染料不够浓重的历史中才用于穿着。穿这种颜色的人到哪里都出入无禁。
en: The profound purple worn only in Histories where Tyrian dye was not rich enough for emperors. It is difficult to bar entry to one wearing this colour.

zh: 蚁母时而也被称为“毒液的女儿”。这种物质在不计其数的地方洞开着人体。
en: From time to time, the Mother of Ants has also been called the Daughter of Venoms. This substance opens the body in uncountable places.

zh: 此物只能储存在纯由一种元素制成的容器，且绝对不能容许它加温。它或许不是炼金术士追寻的终极溶剂。但它能胜任我们的目的。
en: Store this only in containers of a pure element, and never permit it to grow warm. Perhaps it isn't the final solvent that the alchemists sought. But it will do, for our purposes.

zh: 至纯的白色，在全然的黑暗中依然可见。
en: A white so pure that it remains visible even in utter darkness.

zh: “内外相易，表里互替。”一种对弧月信徒来说地位神圣的毒素。
en: 'What is within, without; what is without, within.' A poison sacred to the votaries of the Meniscate.

zh: 有的时候，瞳中扉打开时，可以收集到一种浅色的，警觉的液体。
en: A pale and watchful fluid that can be gathered, sometimes, when the Door-in-the-Eye is opened.

zh: 取自碾碎的稀有鳞翅目昆虫的翅膀。“想画黑，想画白，这种颜料都能来。”
en: From the crushed wings of rare lepidopterans. 'Painting black, or painting white, this is the stuff that'll see you right.'

zh: 一种粘稠的黑色液体，受到森林中无名村落的喜爱。“舌头与眼睛，现在我们开始！忘记你的名字，忘记你的表皮。”
en: A dark and syrupy liquor favoured in unnamed forest villages. 'The tongue and the eye, and now we begin! Forget your name, forget your skin.'

zh: 某桩古老罪案现场洒落的神秘物质的淡淡印痕，紧封在一个安瓿瓶中。“谁会记得理发师的儿子？”
en: Faint traces of an enigmatic substance spilt in the commission of an ancient crime, tightly sealed in a glass ampoule. 'Who will remember the barber's son?'

zh: 正是人于之无法入眠的时辰的颜色。
en: The precise colour of the hours when one cannot sleep.

zh: 此物冷到任何事物都无法将之融化，除了最炎热的正午阳光。它会一点一点，极缓地蚕食人类的血肉。
en: Far too cold ever to melt in anything but the hottest noonday sun. It will, very gradually, consume human flesh.

zh: 第一次蠕虫大战遗留下的一小罐发育不良的，干瘪的幼虫，与占据已逝众司辰之位的神灵同级。
en: A tiny pot of stunted, desiccated larval remnants from the First Worm War, rank with the power that inhabits dead Hours.

zh: 这里有甜美的空气，这里有美梦的开端……在这里，我们的思绪转向那位名为“制花人”的司辰。
en: Here are sweet airs, here are the beginnings of dreams... here our thoughts turn to the Hour called Flowermaker.

zh: 可以用在画布上。
en: It might be possible to apply this to canvas.

zh: 如果我能发现这个弱点，它就能为我所用。
en: If I can uncover this weakness, it'll give me an advantage.

zh: 制花人的影响中有什么对我的大敌造成了困扰。我可能永远都不知道那究竟是什么。（但他不会特别忌惮其他司辰。）
en: Something about the Flowermaker's influence confounds my Foe. I may never know exactly what. (But he doesn't have any especial fear of the other Hours.)

zh: 我大概可以拍卖此物，虽然拍卖价从来都是未知之数。
en: I might be able to auction this, although value at auction is always uncertain.

zh: 来自亚琛的消息找上了我。马拉喀什的一位老熟人正在那边打猎，想让我帮个忙。
en: A message from Aachen has found me. An old acquaintance from Marrakech is hunting there. She'd like my help.

zh: 战前，我被邀请去亚历山大港隐形的塞拉皮雍。走运的话，那份邀请依然有效。
en: Back before the war, I was invited to the Invisible Serapeum in Alexandria. With luck, the invitation is still open.

zh: 我那位搞走私的熟人M·乔吉奥在阿尔及尔有人脉。他们可以悄悄把我弄进去。
en: My smuggler contact, M. Giorgiou, has people in Algiers. They could get me in there quietly.

zh: 多年来，我为阿姆斯特丹的斯特根加兄弟找了不少好东西。他们也许会为我提供庇护。
en: Over the years I've found a dozen treasures for the Stegenga twins in Amsterdam. They may give me shelter.

zh: 我那位搞走私的熟人，阿维尼翁的M·乔吉奥，提出要和我做一笔生意。
en: A smuggler contact in Avignon, M. Giorgiou, has a proposition for me.

zh: 珀西·斯泰尔斯依然留在巴格达，在戒严中的伊拉克为英国政府卖命。他兴许仍会愿意见我。
en: Percy Styles is still in Baghdad, doing whatever it is he does for the British government in Mandatory Iraq. He'd probably still be glad to see me.  [Once you leave the current city, this will never b

zh: 我听说我的老朋友莉莉把她的黑市生意搬到了布达佩斯。
en: I hear my old friend Lili has moved her black market operations to Budapest.

zh: 我离开克里特岛已经有很长一段时间了，但我又梦见了它，梦见了山楂树的香味……
en: It's a long time since I was in Crete, but I've been dreaming about it again, and about the scent of hawthorn...

zh: 自从匈牙利放弃克卢日以来，我的老朋友莉莉在这座城市的生意就不好做了，但我依然可以凭借她的名号打通门路。
en: My old friend Lili's operations in Cluj have suffered since Hungary gave the city up, but her name might still open doors.

zh: 我和格拉纳达的一个画廊老板打过交道。去年她邀请我去她那里住上一阵子。
en: I've had dealings with a gallery owner in Granada. Last year she invited me to stay with her.

zh: 我的老战友凯拉·凯末尔与伊斯坦布尔的黑社会残党有联系。也许他的名号能为我打通门路。
en: My old comrade-in-arms Kara Kemal has connections with the Black Arm remnants in Istanbul. Perhaps his name will still open doors. [Once you leave the current city, this will never be available again.

zh: 面包师多明卡斯最近混得不错，和考纳斯铁狼的高层攀上了交情。我们的关系一直都不太过得去，但是他欠我人情，我也不能太挑剔。
en: Dominykas the Baker has done well for himself: high-level connections in the Iron Wolf in Kaunas. We never really got on, but he owes me, and I can't be fussy. [Once you leave the current city, this w

zh: 我听说丽姬亚中最饥渴的人在基辅。我的追兵在那里得处处小心——不过我也一样。
en: I've heard rumours the hungriest of the Ligeians is abroad in Kyiv. My pursuers will need to tread carefully there, but then, so will I. [Once you leave the current city, this will never be available

zh: 多年来，克拉科夫的伊兹乔克·阿什拉格一直靠我来获得罕见的密教文献。他是个好客的人。
en: Yitzchok Ashlag in Krakow has relied on me for years for the rarer occult texts. He's a hospitable man.

zh: 在俄罗斯出售岁月的“雅典人”是清算人暴徒的死敌。但我曾和其中一个叫苏斯金的人喝得酩酊大醉。希望他还记得我。
en: The 'Athenians' who sell years in Russia are sworn enemies of the Reckoner mobs, but I've been known to get drunk with a certain Sushkin. Let's hope he remembers me. [Once you leave the current city,

zh: 伦敦的奥塔维奥·伯顿传话说他需要我的帮助。他是个恶棍，但我们算是老相识了。
en: Ottavio Berton in London has put out the word that he'd like my help. He's a scoundrel, but we go back a long way.

zh: 一个叫纳维尔的隐士不知怎么发现了我。他的留言只写着“来马拉喀什，以辉光之名”。
en: A marabout named Navil has somehow discovered I'm here. His message reads only 'COME TO MARRAKECH, FOR GLORY'

zh: 在马什哈德有一位年迈的诗人，她一直是我最喜欢的主顾之一。她一定会款待我的。
en: A very old poet in Meshad has long been a favourite customer of mine. She'll certainly offer me hospitality.

zh: 慕尼黑的戈特洛布·雅宁斯伯爵向我发出过一份长期有效的邀请，去他所支持的决斗者协会参加集会。
en: I have a standing invitation from Count Gottlieb Jannings, of Munich, to attend a meeting of the duelling society he favours.

zh: “雅典人”苏斯金一直在下诺夫哥罗德经营进出口业务，但在苏联统治下生意不佳。他在那里的人谅必会因我的帮助而高兴。
en: Sushkin of the 'Athenians' has kept his import-export operation in Nizhny running, but it's difficult under the Soviets. His people there might be glad of my help. [Once you leave the current city, th

zh: “艾德琳姑姑”搬走了，但她在巴黎还有人，他们会为我提供庇护的。
en: Tante Adeline's moved on, but she still has people in Paris, and they'll give me shelter.

zh: 布拉格那个自称“艾德琳姑姑”的老流氓可能用得上我的帮助。
en: The old rogue in Prague who calls herself 'Tante Adeline' could use my help.

zh: 我的联络人雅宁斯伯爵在罗斯托克有一所房子可以供我使用。
en: My correspondent Count Jannings has a house in Rostock that I could use.

zh: 我曾经在撒马尔罕给一个扎吉德派的术士提供过违禁材料。也许他还记得我。
en: I used to supply proscribed materials to a Jadidist magus in Samarkand. Perhaps he remembers me.

zh: 被称为“食鸟者”的有罪学徒已经彻底违反了凡人和漫宿的法律。我听说理发师祖尔菲亚在斯大林格勒加入了他们。食鸟者可不是好人，但我一直喜欢祖尔菲亚。
en: The criminal adepts called the Bird-Eaters have set their faces against the laws of both man and Mansus. I had heard that Zulfiya the Barber had joined them in Stalingrad. The Bird-Eaters are not good

zh: 孚日山脉的“丘陵之子”有时会以人形行走在斯特拉斯堡的街道上。我以前和他们打过交道。他们和任何人一样知道如何保持低调：也许他们能帮上忙。
en: The Hügelkinder of the Vosges sometimes take human shape to walk the streets of Strasbourg. I've dealt with them before. They know as well as anyone how to be unobtrusive: perhaps they can help. [Once

zh: 在斯维尔德洛夫斯克，理发师祖尔菲亚过去曾用珠宝向我购买岁月。后来她变得很奇怪，并且搬到了另一个城市。但在内战之前，她向我介绍了她的一些联系人。
en: Zulfiya the Barber used to buy life from me with jewels in the city of Sverdlovsk. She's turned strange and moved on to another city, but she introduced me to some of her contacts, back before the Civ

zh: “魅裔”瓦西尔很久以前在梯弗利斯躲避清算人。尽管他可能不欢迎我的来访，但是他欠我人情。
en: Vasil the Fivegoer took refuge from the reckoners in Tiflis long ago. He owes me, though he might not welcome my visit.

zh: 在这一切变得糟糕之前，我正与索古总统在地拉那的一个手下商议关于岁月的生意。我还有她的地址。
en: Before all this went bad, I was negotiating a sale of life with one of President Zogu's people in Tirana. I still have her address. [Once you leave the current city, this will never be available again

zh: 我在的黎波里没有熟人。我压根不记得这里，但是很久之前，在的黎波里还属于奥斯曼的时候，我在这里出生了。
en: I don't have any people in Tripoli. I don't remember it at all. But quite some time ago, in the days of the Ottomans, I was born here. [Once you leave the current city, this will never be available ag

zh: 我的老战友凯拉·凯末尔在瓦莱塔流亡。这是很久之前的事了，但我确实在大战中救过他的命……
en: My old comrade-in-arms Kara Kemal is in exile in Valletta. It's been a while, but I did save his life in the Great War...

zh: 我在威尼斯认识一个叫欧索丽娜的女人。她并不总是一个女人。但是她在那里住了很久。
en: I know a woman named Orsolina in Venice. She wasn't always a woman. But she's lived there a very long time.

zh: 它一度是“白城”，现在却被称作“红色维也纳”。这里有一位丽姬亚，蠕虫大战留下的伤疤对那些知晓此事的人来说仍然可见。但我想在死前再看一眼这个老地方。
en: It was the White City once. Now they call it 'Red Vienna'. There's a Ligeian here, and the scars of the Worm Wars are still visible to those who know. But if I'm to die, I'd like to see the old place

zh: 探索完毕的地点。除非重现，否则不会再次开启。
en: A location has been exhausted, and won't reopen unless explicitly renewed.

zh: 当局注意到了此地发生的异常举动。他们派来了一名守卫。访问此地将耗费更长的时间，而且很快会变得完全不可能。
en: The authorities have noticed peculiar activity in this place. They have set a watch upon it. Visits will take longer, and very soon they will no longer be possible at all.

zh: 在醒时的世界，和在梦中一样，死者通常位于下方。
en: In the waking world, as in dreams, the dead are most often found below.

zh: 阿利斯泰尔·蒙特梅伦齐，是蒙特梅伦齐家族第三位也是最后一位地图测绘师。他那位神秘的赞助人蒂莫西·麦克唐纳收藏了这些尺寸不大但引人注目的杂项藏品。在他们那次失败的极地冒险后，这些藏品最后面向公众开放，在安静的公园旁边一栋其貌不扬的灰色建筑中展出，展出条件是：禁止拍照，且藏品不能暴露在日光之下。
en: Alistair Montmerenzi, third and last of the Montmerenzi cartographers, and his enigmatic benefactor Timothy McDonald, collected this small but striking miscellany. After their loss on their final pola

zh: 一切事物皆有其所在之处，如此物位于此处。
en: Locations! Everything must be somewhere, and this is here.

zh: 奥里弗拉姆专卖古玩，香水，生物标本和稀有文献。自被一场无法解释的大火烧毁后，如今，它于局促的新址展开业务。
en: Oriflamme's specialises in curios, perfumes, taxidermy specimens, and rare texts. It operates from cramped new premises, now, since the destruction of the old by an unexplained fire. [You can buy or s

zh: 一家据传与密教有关的夜总会。
en: A cabaret with an occult reputation.

zh: 一家据传与密教有关的夜总会。只在每周特定的夜晚开放；不久即将重新开业。
en: A cabaret with an occult reputation. It opens only certain nights of the week; it'll re-open soon.

zh: 有时，月亮的光辉是开启其他空间的钥匙。我发现一处地点，在那么一两个晚上，街巷会以离奇的方式扭曲。如果我走去那里，或许我能洞见真知，又或许我会沾染上疯狂。
en: Sometimes, the light of the moon is a key to other spaces. I've found a place where, for a night or two, the streets curve in unfamiliar ways. If I walk here, I might find insight, or I might be touch

zh: 对手大胆的决定留下了痕迹。
en: Traces left by a rival's decision to move boldly.

zh: 当我从林地之梦里醒来时，发现床边一只密封的瓶子里装着这个。
en: When I woke from the Wood, this is what I found in the sealed bottle beside my bed.

zh: 沉睡心灵的窗外有一片幽深的林地。林地中的树枝上有一个黑色蛛丝与白色头发编成的鸟巢。祖尔菲亚准备了一顿盛宴，好让我今晚能够前往此地。她用墨水在我背部的皮肤上画下了一个图案，并告诉我，那是一张我不能看的地图。
en: There is a dark Wood that presses against the windows of the sleeping mind. There is a nest in the branches woven of black silk and white hair. Zulfiya has prepared a feast to take me there tonight, a

zh: 赤红如同被遗忘的太阳。
en: Red as a forgotten sun.

zh: 两百年前，一位名叫霍科博尔德的不死者在这里研究炼金术。再过一百年，它可能会滑入运河。达里欧宫被迷雾笼罩，更有阴郁的幽魂在其中徘徊，但欧索丽娜相信从未有人入侵过这里的藏宝库。
en: Two hundred years ago, an immortal named Hokobald worked alchemy here. In another hundred years, it may slide into the canal. It's wreathed in mist and prowled by gloomy dead, but Orsolina believes it

zh: 活跃时的衾下魅是一个柔韧灵活、散发着恐怖魅力的半成型怪物，渴望吞噬或孕育凡人的血肉。但在休眠状态下，它不过是一团友好的触手，不是发出呱呱的叫声，就是打着呼噜，而且还滴着黏液。
en: When active, an undergoer is a flexile, half-formed, horribly alluring thing, eager to consume or be impregnated by mortals. But in its dormant state, it's a friendly knot of tentacled flesh, cawing,

zh: 五十年来，这个秘密授业座一直在传授贝·伊莱的四种传统技艺：禁锢灵体、驱逐怪兽、终结顽强的诅咒、封闭危险的通道。我们必须找到这个地方并潜入其中；那里的拉比和学生们会阻挠我们，而且我们或许还得制服那衾下魅本身。
en: For fifty years this secret yeshiva has taught the Four Traditions of the Bei Ilai: the imprisonment of spirits, the expulsion of monsters, the conclusion of tenacious curses and the sealing of troubl

zh: 多明卡斯需要的“调味料”：一罐看起来相当恶心的蛆虫残骸。这个罐子摸起来像冰一样冷。
en: The 'seasoning' Dominykas wanted: a pot of nasty-looking larval remnants. The pot is icy cold to the touch.

zh: 这个秘密部门归属于当局，专门研究那些致畸现象。等我们找到了它的位置之后，我们还必须冲破它的围墙，并且经受住从它的核心中发出的可怕辐射。
en: The authorities maintain a clandestine department dedicated to teratogenic phenomena. Once we've found its location, we'll have to breach its walls and weather the ghastly influences radiating from it

zh: 它会随着内部结构的腐败迅速消融。
en: It's dissolving quickly as its internal structures corrode.

zh: 狮子匠的一件不那么尊贵的作品潜伏在亚琛的下水道中。 我们必须先找到一个隐藏的入口，忍受住下水道中恶臭的空气，然后才能与“欢宴兽”决战。
en: In the sewers below Aachen prowls one of the Lionsmith's less dignified works. We'll have to locate a hidden entrance and survive the sewers' atmosphere before we face the Feaster itself.

zh: “山里有一股邪恶的力量蠢蠢欲动。它被封存在地下多年。但一场地震让大地洞开。防剿局告诉我那里有什么能当武器使的东西。说实话，我宁愿得到它的是你，而不是当地那些闹事的混蛋。”
en: 'There's a nasty sort of power stirring up in the mountains. Sealed away under the earth for years. But there was an earthquake, big hole opened up. Bureau tells me there's something weapon-y in there

zh: 藏有一些珍贵之物。
en: There is something precious here.

zh: 我进入了隐形的塞拉皮雍靠近外侧的一部分——一条洒满阳光的柱廊，体积颇有些不合情理。某位布莱克伍德博士会在这里给我一个座位和一杯冰芙蓉茶。
en: I've entered one of the outer elements of the Invisible Serapeum - a sunny pillared porch of paradoxical size, where a certain Dr Blackwood will offer me a seat and a glass of iced hibiscus tea.

zh: 塞拉皮雍几乎无从找寻……即使身在其中也是如此。不过，有了邀请，我就有了更大的机会。
en: The Serapeum is almost impossible to find... even when you're already inside it. With an invitation I have a better chance, though.

zh: 我已得到了所需之物。
en: I have what I need.

zh: 一直以来都有传言说，塔拉斯克公爵的家族会饲养怪物。他们雇佣了来自法国和德国的逃兵来守卫他们迷雾缭绕的城堡。
en: The family of the Duc de Tarascon has long been rumoured to breed monsters. They've employed French and German deserters to guard their mist-shrouded chateau.

zh: 它还在动呢！
en: E pur si muove!

zh: 在山城阿勒普耶罗斯的高处聚居着一群为轰雷之皮舞蹈的人。他们不愿被人看见，因而准备在画家米蕾娅身上倾泻他们的不满，除非我出手阻止此事。我必须击退他们派出的追兵，并保持清醒，直到黎明。
en: High in the Alpujarras, there is an enclave of those who dance for the Thunderskin. They dislike being spied upon, and unless I prevent it, they will enact their dislike on the painter Mireya. I'll ha

zh: 雅宁斯说有些课程只能写在皮肤上。或许如此吧，但也可能是雅宁斯太享受他的作品了。
en: Jannings says that some lessons can only be written on skin. That might be so, but it might be that Jannings just enjoys his work a little too much.

zh: 一幢有着尖顶山墙的漂亮砖房，门前矗立着两棵古老的松树。马穆拉俱乐部的成员在阁楼上举行痛苦与勇气的仪式。这会是一段难捱的时光。
en: A charming brick house with crow-stepped gables and a pair of venerable pines at the front door. In the attic, the Mamurren perform their rites of pain and courage. This will hurt.

zh: 在那城市上方，吹拂渴求与狂热之风的山丘上，破落的木制教堂立于荒凉的无叶橡树林中。伊兹乔克告诉我，其中定会有我用得上的物件，但他也告诫道：“我查阅了我所有的书籍去搜寻圣玛赞那的消息。然而这个名字无处可寻，或许虚界才有。”
en: On a hill above the city where the wind blows keen and fierce, a decaying wooden church stands in a bleak grove of leafless oak trees. Yitzchok tells me there's something in there I'll find useful, bu

zh: 我所居住的这个拥挤的首都中的一处隐秘场所。
en: A secret place in the teeming capital city where I live.

zh: 这里是斯特拉思科因的宅邸，此人专爱收藏各式稀罕物件。斯特拉思科因收藏的宝贝概不出售。不过若能得一个狡诈的共犯，我们或许有办法进入宅邸自行取用。
en: The house of Mr Strathcoyne, a collector of rare objects. Strathcoyne will not sell his treasures. But a sufficiently cunning accomplice might gain entry.

zh: 寻奇尚异之人已然把这座远古的寺庙翻过一个遍，不过这里的某处或许还藏有一扇暗门。
en: Curiosity-seekers have picked over this ancient temple, but there may be a Hidden Door here somewhere.

zh: 在那场夺去两名业主生命的爆炸后，这里已废弃不用。机器无声地矗立其间，房屋也成了一具塌陷的空壳。凯特先生以爱在办公室摆放小装饰品出名。那些东西或许还有些留下。
en: It's been abandoned since the explosion, when both the owners died. The machines stand silent; the building is a collapsing shell. Mr Cater was known to display peculiar trinkets in his office. Perhap

zh: 以前这里的医生医术精良。确实，他们使用截肢疗法的次数是偏多了一点，但是总有些人大家并不希望见到他们行走在城市大街上。
en: They did good work here, once. Certainly there were a few too many amputations, but then there are individuals one would prefer not to walk the city streets.

zh: 加尔文宗一支非正统流派在国教地盘上的一座孤岛，隐藏在市区的偏远角落。他们以不相宜的亢奋高歌赞美诗，使用一种极不似拉丁语的喉音粗重的语言。
en: A Nonconformist enclave of a heterodox sect of a Calvinist offshoot, tucked away in an odd corner of the city. They belt out hymns with unsuitable gusto, in a throat-scraping language that is very muc

zh: 腐臭的河水两侧是灰色的河堤，一排仓库在那里等待着我们。我差不多已经认出了其中存有不同寻常的货物的那一栋。腐臭的河水两侧是灰色的河堤，一排仓库在那里等待着我们。我差不多已经认出了其中存有不同寻常的货物的那一栋。
en: Along the grey banks of the rotting river, the warehouses wait. Perhaps I have identified one where an unusual cargo was stored. [You've found all the Expeditions in the Capital, although you can find

zh: 穿越狭海，在敌国的未知角落。
en: Across a narrow sea, in unsuspected corners of rival nations.

zh: 关于拉维林伯爵家，风行的传闻称他们是最初的太阳王的后裔。还有传闻讲述他们如何化为狼形巡猎于森林之中。这个家族早已消亡，只余朽坏的家族城堡，其中尚有未为人所查的凶险之处。
en: The favourite stories of the Comtes de Raveline claim they descend from the first Sun-King. Other stories have them prowling the forest in wolf-shape. They are long gone. Their rotting chateau remains

zh: 罗伯特·费米尔将他很多来路不正的财富捐赠给了这座坐落于密林之中的宏大修道院。这里的僧侣不喜欢访客。他们的教义不能算正统。他们以某种惹人憎恶的活力庆祝某个收获的节日。
en: Robert Fermier spent much of his unsavoury fortune on the endowment of this monumental, forest-crowded abbey. The monks dislike visitors. Their ways are not exactly orthodox. They celebrate a certain

zh: 松树林犹如狗背脊上的毛发般浓密。树林深处的空气混着松脂和尘土，厚重得令人窒息。根据消息来源的说法，每当黎明时分，树林黑暗深处那棵最大的树上，会栖有一只蒙面的巨鸦。
en: The pines grow thick as hairs on a dog's back. The airs of the inner wood are chokingly thick with resin and dust. The source that brought you here claimed that in the dawn times, the greatest tree, a

zh: 坐落在某偏僻镇子上的一家又窄又小的展览馆，在很久前，保管压花标本的房间爆发过一次奇异的狂喜之潮后便不再对外开放。只有最好奇的人才可能注意到这个地方，及里面把守严密的储物间里不吉利的宝物。
en: A cramped little museum in a provincial town, long closed to the public since an outbreak of peculiar rapture in the room where they keep the pressed flowers. Only the very curious would ever pay atte

zh: 菲利普·福曼是一名寄生虫学家和文物学家。他最好的研究都是在他居住的祖宅中完成的，直到他的研究对象从中脱逃。虫患已被消灭，但是他们说福曼直至今天仍在厅堂徘徊，守着他的藏书与他的耻辱。
en: Filip Forman, the helminthologist and antiquarian, did all his best work here at the family home, until the day his subjects escaped. The infestation was destroyed, but Forman, they say, walks the hal

zh: 维也纳是少数几个在每重历史中都拥有着相同名字的城市之一，尽管学徒们称之为白城，抑或是纯白之城。在满是白雪的街道上，在一栋扭曲的房子中，在发出吱嘎声的高高的阁楼上，我们听说了一名狩猎空想之钥的古怪猎人所著的一小批古怪书籍。
en: Vienna is one of those few cities which has the same name in every History, although adepts know it as the White City, or perhaps the City of the White. In a snowy street, in a crooked house, in a hig

zh: 巨石由早已逝去的祭司阶层排成整齐到偏执的行列。时间早已洗去了原本的血渍，但是当无月之夜来临，当地人会补充残留下的东西。隐秘的暗室中或许藏有隐秘的宝物。
en: Megaliths placed in obsessive, inerrant rows by priest-castes long dead. Time has long erased the original blood-stains, but on moonless nights, the locals supplement what remains. Hidden chambers mig

zh: 浪花拍打着一串绿意盎然的岩石岛屿。
en: Wave-washed chains of rocky islands vibrant with green life.

zh: 一座死火山从错综缠结的翡翠色丛林中探出头来。受同类最卑劣的罪行所害的不死者被流放到这里生息，捕食不设防的旅客，大吃大嚼他们的血和碎裂的骨头。这些年间他们肯定抓到了不少有意思的玩具。
en: An extinct volcano lifts its head from a fervid tangle of emerald jungle. Immortals fallen to the vilest crime of their kind are exiled here to roost, guzzling the blood and cracking the bones of unwa

zh: 三百年前，一个贪婪成性的帝国占领了一座遍地碧玺矿的岛屿。这个帝国和这座岛屿没有在任何正史中出现过。但是岛上督官的堡垒还在，或许他的财宝也是。这么说的话，或许他本人也是。
en: Three hundred years ago, a rapacious empire claimed an island ripe with tourmalines. Neither the empire nor the island are mentioned in any reputable history. But the governor's fortress remains. Perh

zh: 一头海兽来向这艘船求爱，但当船不回应这份爱慕时，海兽击碎了她。直到现在，海兽依然不肯离开船的残骸，把它装饰地像密教珍宝一般。在水下，我们需要谨慎行事。
en: A sea-beast came to love this vessel, but when she would not return its affections, the sea-beast crushed it. Even now, the beast will not leave the ship's corpse, bedecked as it is with occult treasu

zh: 僧院危悬在崖边。它早该坠入海中了，但是——我们从书上读到——这里的僧侣把人淹死在底下一个深潭中，换取双生女巫于海的保佑，一年一次，一次一年。
en: A monastery hangs grimly to a cliff-edge. It should have fallen into the sea by now, but - we have read - the monks drown victims in a deep pool beneath, to win the Sister-and-Witch's protection from

zh: 每个不死者都要面对下列抉择：侍奉一位司辰，打回凡籍，还是自行消灭。这座阳光明媚，名为“午”的小小岛港是拒绝上述选项者的流放地。
en: There is a choice that every immortal must make: enter the service of an Hour, or return to the mortal ranks, or face extinction. The sunny little island-port called Noon is the exile-realm of the imm

zh: 一艘破旧却惊人豪华的蒸汽轮船，多为躲避丑闻的演员、不愿露富的百万富翁、感觉身下有活水更为安全的密教学者所用；并且仅有一次，为丽姬亚俱乐部所用，此团体因要使用七间客舱而包下了整艘轮船。
en: A battered but surprisingly plush steamer, much used by actors fleeing scandal, by shy millionaires, by occult scholars who feel safer with moving water under them; and, just once, by the Ligeia Club,

zh: 烟笼雾罩的小径，偏居世外的村落，世界的表皮在这里变得稀薄。
en: Misty passes and remote villages, where the world's skin is thin.

zh: 每七次日落，狡诈的男人便从下方的村落上来更换蜡烛。岩洞入口处的地上浮着一层臭气熏人的蜡油。他号称烛光能威慑岩洞深处的某个生物。不过它更可能是因为享用了他带来的祭品才心满意足，甘心闭门不出。
en: Every seventh sunset, the cunning man comes from the village below to renew the candles. The floor of the entrance chamber is a slick of stinking wax. He claims the light keeps the creature below cowe

zh: 在突厥人势力滔天的年代，一位有力之人曾统治此地。许多年间，他平复了许多人的干渴，直到败给自己的干渴。我听说他的金银财宝，加上为他所害的人们，或许还留存在这座城堡下的某个无人涉足的地下宝库中。
en: In the days of the great power of the Turks, a man of power ruled here. Over many years he fed many thirsts, until his own thirst overcame him. I have learned that both his treasures, and his victims,

zh: 刷了白石灰的农场外围驻扎着一群裰着补丁的帐篷，仿佛一座小小的城市。格吕内瓦尔德女士将她可观的财产投入到了维护这个组织的运转上：没人知道为什么。马戏团演员讨厌别人擅闯幕后。
en: A tiny city of patched tents around a white-washed farm. Hunched performers shiver in the dripping rain. Frau Grunewald has devoted her considerable fortune to the maintenance of this institution: who

zh: 一座荒废建筑的残垣。空气仿佛溃烂般散发着脓水味道。毁灭此地的那种力量依然停留不去。地面上只残存着一层楼，但是我们猜测地下还有更大空间。
en: The blasted stump of a building. The air festers. Whatever power destroyed this place still clings. Only one floor remains above ground, but we suspect more space below.

zh: 库兹涅佐夫公爵将他沾满鲜血的财富的四分之一捐给这座地方大学以建立末世学学部。学部的员工现在变得怪里怪气，捐赠的楼房则烂在了一片冰封的沼泽里，不过库兹涅佐夫公爵的遗赠或许有部分被花在了购置财宝上。
en: Prince Kusnetsov gave one fourth part of his blood-soaked fortune to the establishment of a Department for Eschatology at this provincial university. The staff have grown peculiar, now, and the buildi

zh: 狐百合的花香能够勾起人如狼似虎的食欲。它们只在这个海拔生长或许是人的幸运。农民们已经组织起来驱逐寻宝者，但有些寻宝者还是被狐百合变成了饥饿的俘虏。他们自己的宝物落在自己的白骨旁，周而复始。
en: The scent of foxlilies inspires ferocious appetites. It is probably fortunate that they grow only at this altitude. The peasants have organised to keep treasure-seekers away, but some treasure-seekers

zh: 在这个干旱又遥远之地，放眼望去只有绵延的沙丘。
en: In a dry and distant place, dune-chained horizons await you.

zh: 这里曾是地上诸神的圣地，直到一颗流星从冬日的夜空落下。后来，朝拜者渐渐回到了圣地的废墟。再后来，掉落的陨石反成了朝拜的对象，能够带人入梦的神物。旧祭坛或许还未损毁，还藏在陨石下方的某处。
en: Here was a place sacred to the gods of earth, until a meteor fell on a winter's night. In time, worshippers crept back to the broken ruins. In time, the meteorite itself came to be worshipped as a bri

zh: 那是一块低矮的斜长方形泥砖，藏在沙漠一个隐蔽的沙谷中。它外表并不显眼，看不出拉贡其实位列不死的长生者，从没有盗墓贼掘开过他的坟墓。
en: A low slope-sided rectangle of mud brick, in an obscure fold of the desert. It is not remarkable to look at, but Lagun was counted among the immortal Long, and no robber has ever breached his tomb.

zh: 河湾地坐落着一座僻静而又富庶的村庄——像一点亮眼的青绿衬在沙土地上。村民们拥有超过自然限度的长寿。他们背后有什么秘密？
en: A quietly prosperous village rests in the crook of the river: a surprising splash of green against the dusty land behind. The longevity of the villagers surpasses the natural. What are they hiding?

zh: 那是山隘中一座坍圮的寺庙。貌似建于第三史，那里蠕虫横行欧洲的时间要晚得多，不过它现在存在于每一重历史中。
en: A crumbling temple in a shadowed defile. It was built, it seems, in the Third History, the one where much later the Worms overran Europe, but it exists in all the Histories now.

zh: 这里曾有一座城市。我们可以像豺狗搜刮陈年骨殖一样搜刮城市残迹——宫殿墙壁，被掩埋的道路，残破不堪的喷泉。有时是我们寻到死者拥有的知识，有时是死者寻到我们。
en: There was a city here. We can pick over its remains like jackals over old bones: palace walls, buried roads, shattered fountains. Sometimes we may find the knowledge of the dead, and sometimes the dea

zh: 这里有绿色的田野和灰色的山丘。
en: Green fields and grey hills.

zh: 在荒凉的山坡下等待着的是雾气缭绕的湖面。在罗马人到来前，这里曾立有一对祭坛，并流传着被派去玷污祭坛的蛇的传说。
en: Beneath the stark slopes of the fells, the mist-wreathed surface of the lake waits. There was a twin altar here before the Romans came; and a legend of a serpent who was sent to defile that altar.

zh: 散发着臭气的晦黯沼地里遍布着阴险鬼祟的生命。沉默寡言的当地人依然为那些小而隐秘，能使祈祷成真的神明守望着圣地。
en: The reeking murk of the fenlands is ripe with furtive life. Taciturn locals still watch the sacred places of the small and secret gods, the ones that answer prayers.

zh: 远古的部落将这片安息之地留给某人，他最终会成为某位无情司辰的侍奉者，此人最终变得更像伤疤，而非人类。他们用咒歌和混着砂浆的血液封住了这里的入口。
en: The ancient tribes reserved this resting place for one who, by the end, in service to a certain merciless Hour, was more scar than man. They sealed the entrance with binding-songs and mortared blood.

zh: 罗马人在这里采挖银矿，直到挖到他们不该碰的，具名者们藏匿不宜见日光之物的地方。之后又有许多人来过此地，坑洞崩塌时亦然。一些人再也没有回去。
en: Here the Romans dug for silver, until they reached the unsanctioned space where the Names hid things not fit for sunlight. Many others have come since then, even as the tunnels crumble. Some have neve

zh: 在被大海冲垮以前，高威尔旅馆踞守于灰色海面之上的摇摇欲坠的悬崖。拜访它的客人极少——自打旅馆主人向来访学者关闭了他臭名昭著的藏书室后，客人就更少了。
en: On a crumbling cliff above a grey sea, the Gwaer Inn squats, until the day the sea comes for it. It receives few guests - fewer since the owner closed his notorious library to visiting scholars.

zh: 我们沿着白垩丘陵驶向海岸，驶向灰绿色大地与大西洋的灰色海水的交接处，一路寻找通往凯尔伊苏姆镇的道路。那个地方难得寻见，哪怕你曾经去到过也是一样。那里的居民用咒骂，棍棒，和紧紧拴住的门来保守他们的秘密。
en: Drive through the downs to the coast where the grey-green land meets Atlantic grey. Search along the road for signs to Kerisham town. You will rarely find it, even if you have visited before; and its

zh: 有关通常被列为禁忌的历史的诱人消息。
en: Tantalising information on histories that are usually suppressed.

zh: 一些模糊的信息，关于被掩盖的许多旧事的真相。
en: Obscure information on the suppressed truths of many pasts.

zh: 一份非常重要的调查成果，关于那些未被记录的事件，描述着醒时与梦中的世界。
en: A Very Substantial work on the unwritten events which shaped the waking and the dreaming world.

zh: 伊本·阿迪姆博士，人称阿勒颇人，向你提出了一项委托。他希望你提供你能找到的所有关于世界隐秘历史的情报。
en: Dr Ibn al-Adim, called the Aleppine, has offered you a commission. He would like you to provide him with any information you can assemble on the secret histories of the world. [You'll need a matching

zh: 伊本·阿迪姆博士，人称阿勒颇人，向你提出了一项委托。他要寻找一篇关于世界隐秘历史的详尽论文。
en: Dr Ibn al-Adim, called the Aleppine, has offered you a commission. He's looking for a Substantial treatise on the secret histories of the world. [You'll need a matching Lore fragment of at least level

zh: 伊本·阿迪姆博士，人称阿勒颇人，向你提出了一项委托。他要寻找一些关于世界隐秘历史的特别隐晦的情报。
en: Dr Ibn al-Adim, called the Aleppine, has offered you a commission. He's looking for particularly obscure information on the secret histories of the world. [You'll need a matching Lore fragment of at l

zh: I must CHANGE
en: I must CHANGE

zh: I must CHANGE
en: I must CHANGE

zh: I must CHANGE
en: I must CHANGE

zh: 密教社团，追求研究五大历史，及其数以千计、亦虚亦实的分支。
en: An occult society dedicated to the study of the five Histories and their thousand demi-real branches. [St Hydra, unusually, allows you to use any aspect to promote Believers; but it has no Exalted spe

zh: 有关无形之术的知识。我可以利用它来吸引追随者，举行仪式，开启通往漫宿的道路，或是寻觅飞升之法……
en: Knowledge of the invisible arts. I can use it to attract followers, to perform rites, to open the way to the Mansus, to seek my ascension... [Lore fragments can sometimes be upgraded with Study, and w

zh: 这是一个难关，但这种程度的秘史可以解决它。
en: There is a complication, but this much Secret Histories would resolve it.

zh: 我希望提升我的秘史之密传。
en: I hope to improve my Secret Histories lore.

zh: 极难注意到的地点。
en: This place is easily overlooked.

zh: 历史是时间在世界身上留下的伤疤。
en: History is the scar on the world's skin.

zh: 由名声扫地的神谕者达马翁·阿谮那滕筛选收集而成，素材源自戈尔迪翁的佩涅罗佩所写的天方夜谭，宫廷诗人凯勒的作品，还有学徒阿雷特雷兹著作中遭禁的片段。
en: The disgraced oracle Damaon Azenaten sifts through the tall tales of Penelope of Gordion, the work of the court poet Kalle and the forbidden fragments of the work of the adept Arrettrez, identifying c

zh: 由名声扫地的神谕者达马翁·阿谮那滕筛选收集而成。
en: Siftings of the disgraced oracle Damaon Azenaten.

zh: 我们永生不死的敌人有所计划。
en: Our immortal enemy has a plan.

zh: 特蕾莎·加尔米耶在书中考察了匠人们所经历的神秘梦境中的相似之处。丛书第一卷。
en: The first volume of Teresa Galmier's examination of parallels in the mystic dreams of artisans.

zh: 特蕾莎·加尔米耶在书中考察了匠人们所经历的神秘梦境中的相似之处。丛书第二卷。扉页已被人拿剃刀划烂。
en: The second volume of Teresa Galmier's examination of parallels in the mystic dreams of artisans. The frontispiece has been slashed with a razor.

zh: 特蕾莎·加尔米耶在书中考察了匠人们所经历的神秘梦境中的相似之处。丛书第三卷。
en: The third volume of Teresa Galmier's examination of parallels in the mystic dreams of artisans.

zh: 特蕾莎·加尔米耶所著的卷帙浩繁之作的第四卷——不再是关于匠人梦境的探讨，而是贵重的对其他职业的调查。笔锋相较《罪行》平静了许多，却时而透出惧意，甚至偏执。本卷于1926年出版，却几乎立刻就被出版社召回销毁——依作者或是政府的要求，随你相信哪种说法。只有数本得以保全。
en: The fourth volume of Teresa Galmier's expansive work - no longer a discussion of the dreams of artisans, but a valuable survey of other works. The tone is calmer than that of 'Trespasses', but occasio

zh: 特蕾莎·加尔米耶所著的臭名昭著之作的第五卷（最后一卷?），始于作者对匠人们神秘梦境中的相似之处的考察，后来变成她自己的探索密教之旅笔记。这一卷从未出版过，而且看样子，还未写出来。
en: The fifth (and final?) volume of Teresa Galmier's notorious work, which began as an examination of parallels in the mystic dreams of artisans but became an account of her own occult journey. This volu

zh: 随着时间推移，这名长生者将会得知我们梦境的形状，增强他从漫宿攻击我们的能力。
en: Over time, this Long will learn the shape of our dreams, improving their ability to strike at us through the Mansus.

zh: 我们永生不死的敌人从我们身上了解到了什么。
en: Our immortal enemy has learnt something of us.

zh: 阶梯升入灰色的霓虹；窗口开启以待。
en: Staircases rise in grey rainbows; windows wait.

zh: 长生者派人监视着这条道路。若我走过，他就会在我的睡眠中更了解我。
en: The Long has set a watch upon this Way. If I pass it, they'll learn more of me as I sleep.

zh: 依靠灯或启能找到暗门。
en: Hidden Doors can be found with Lantern or Knock.

zh: 一名工程师，或学徒，确保了这扇门不会轻易打开。铸或启能够打开它。
en: An engineer, or an adept, has ensured this door will not open easily. Forge or Knock will defeat it.

zh: 关于“遗忘家族”的寓言式史书，书中暗示该组织受蜈蚣的启发。时至如今，“塞雷诺·布莱克伍德”一直是对密教的别称。
en: An allegorical history of the House of Lethe, who it is here implied were inspired by the Centipede. 'Sereno Blackwood' is an occult alias to this day.

zh: 关于“遗忘家族”的寓言式史书，书中暗示该组织受蜈蚣的启发。时至如今，“塞雷诺·布莱克伍德（意为静谧黑林地）”一直是对密教的别称。
en: An allegorical history of the House of Lethe, who it is here implied were inspired by the Centipede. 'Sereno Blackwood' is an occult alias to this day.

zh: 长生者正在谋划下一次对付我们的行动。如果我们派出密探，或许能找到揭开他们图谋的线索。
en: The Long is planning their next action against us. If we send a spy, we might be able to discover something of their intentions.

zh: 为了下次行动，这名长生者大概会试图在梦中得知我们的弱点——哪怕他计划的说辞经常是另外一回事。
en: For their next action, this Long will probably try to learn from our weaknesses in dreams - even if their plans would usually dictate otherwise.

zh: 这次，我们的敌人准备通过漫宿找到我们。
en: Our enemy will find us through the Mansus, this time.

zh: 在梦中，我找到了孔雀之门，时而又名眼瞳之门，或者自满之沮。
en: In my dreams, I've found the way to the Peacock's Door, sometimes called the Gate of Eyes, or Pride's Dismay.

zh: 在梦中，我找到了蜘蛛之门，时而又名鲜血之门，或者更通俗的说，错误的门。
en: In my dreams, I've found the way to the Spider's Door, sometimes called the Bloody Gate or, more informally, the Wrong Door.

zh: 在梦中，我找到了牡鹿之门，时而又名兽角之门、学徒之门。我答出了谜语，已跻身通晓者之列。
en: In my dreams, I've found the way to the Stag Door, sometimes called the Horn Gate or the Adept's Door. I have answered its riddle, and am counted among the Know.

zh: “何物会失去？”答对这个谜语便可穿过牡鹿之门，跻身通晓者之列。
en: 'What may be lost?' Answer this riddle to pass the Stag Door, and be accounted a Know.

zh: “何处觅仁慈？”答对这个谜语便可穿过牡鹿之门，跻身通晓者之列。
en: 'Where is mercy found?' Answer this riddle to pass the Stag Door, and be accounted a Know.

zh: “是何话语使献祭变得甘甜？”答对这个谜语便可穿过牡鹿之门，跻身通晓者之列。
en: 'What are the words that make sacrifice sweet?' Answer this riddle to pass the Stag Door, and be accounted a Know.

zh: “开启这条道路前，必须先开启什么？”答对这个谜语便可穿过牡鹿之门，跻身通晓者之列。
en: 'What must open, before this Way opens?' Answer this riddle to pass the Stag Door, and be accounted a Know.

zh: “不曾诞生的女王身在何方？”答对这个谜语便可穿过牡鹿之门，跻身通晓者之列。
en: 'Where is the queen who was not born?' Answer this riddle to pass the Stag Door, and be accounted a Know.

zh: 我学到了由何路途可前往林地，那团生长、纠缠在漫宿墙壁周围（尽管漫宿无墙）的黑暗。我可以以此入梦来重返林地。
en: I've learnt the path to the Wood, the tangled darkness that grows around the walls of the Mansus (though the Mansus has no walls). I can Dream with this to return to the Wood.

zh: The Wolf will accept anything, and then devour everything, no matter what.
en: The Wolf will accept anything, and then devour everything, no matter what.

zh: “以适当的方式，适当的顺序，做适当的事情。愿上神帮助我们所有人。”——或为海索所说。
en: 'The proper things, in the proper manner, in the proper order. God help us all.' - attributed to Hersault.

zh: 这份记录的作者是洛克·卡胡利，受迦腻色伽所害者兼其情人，也许是在死后写下。
en: Lok Kahuli, Kanishk's victim and lover, wrote this account, perhaps posthumously.

zh: 这份记录的作者是洛克·卡胡利，受迦腻色伽所害者兼其情人，也许是在死后写下。
en: Lok Kahuli, Kanishk's victim and lover, wrote this account, perhaps posthumously.

zh: 《真实准确的无影众王史，卡斯的艾萨克著》。艾萨克在本书中描写了无影帝国持续千年的霸权。
en: 'A True and Accurate History of the Shadowless Kings, by Isaac of Karth.' Isaac describes the thousand-year ascendancy of the Shadowless Empire.

zh: 无影帝国的历史，卡斯的伊萨克著。
en: Isaac of Karth's history of the Shadowless Empire.

zh: 召唤物变作调味影响或阻碍召唤之人。
en: Summonings will be replaced by flavour influences or summon blockers

zh: 一个不起眼的地方，不知为何更容易在夜晚碰上。一根不起眼的蜡烛被随随便便地插进了墙上的一条裂缝里。
en: An unremarkable location that's somehow easier to stumble across at night. A nondescript candle has been jammed haphazardly into a crevice in the wall.

zh: 它还在动呢！
en: E pur si muove!

zh: 在漫宿边界，铸炉四隅，辉光之火烧灼更原始物质之处。那里有一团狡猾的蒸汽，可以侵染你敌人的梦境。
en: At the corners of the Forge, in the bounds of the Mansus, the Glory's fire meets baser elements. Here is a cunning vapour to infect your enemy's dreams.

zh: “我于荒漠中等待、于废墟中燃烧——金铁为水——土石化蜡——肉身成烟——来入我内，不复续存。”
en: 'IN THE DESERT I WAIT IN THE RUINS I BURN - METAL IS WATER - STONE IS WAX - FLESH IS SMOKE - ENTER ME AND BE NO LONGER.'

zh: 时而分散，时而聚集，她时而预言灾难，他时而宣扬食欲。它的预言让人困惑，它们的低语致人混淆。
en: Sometimes it divides, sometimes it unites, sometimes she speaks words of warning, sometimes he advises on appetite. Its prophecies are confusion, their whispers confound.

zh: “改变我。改变我。用力改变我啊。现在——轮到我了。”
en: 'Change me. Change me. Change me harder. Now: my turn.'

zh: “迹形”之灵躲在后面时最自在：舞台后面，镜子后面，你后面。它们从门底滑入，用玻璃杀戮，却活不过活人的注视。
en: The Hinter are most comfortable behind: behind the scenes, behind the mirrors, behind you. They slide under doors and kill with glass, but they cannot survive the gaze of a living mortal.

zh: “噢，世界是平的；噢，可它是平的！我不会久留，我不该久留。告诉我个秘密。”
en: 'Oh, the world is flat; oh, but it's flat! I won't stay long. I shan't stay long. Tell me a secret.'

zh: 亡者有时会通过纯白之门进入漫宿。它们会把说话的能力永远留在门槛外。把它们唤回现世，它们将无法发声，但其落灰的皮囊会遵从你的命令。
en: Sometimes the Dead enter the Mansus by the White Door. At its threshold they lay down their power of speech forever. Call them back to the world, and they will have no voice, but their skins of dust a

zh: “路为玻璃，墙为刀刃，步步见血。”在新开裂的镜子前休憩的亡者，穿过孔雀之门的亡者，征服锐利坡路的亡者：它们将加入侍奉残阳的行列。
en: 'The ascent is glass. The walls are knives. Each step is blood.' The Dead who rest before a new-cracked mirror, who pass through the Peacock Gate, who master the Sharp Stair: those Dead enter the serv

zh: recipe aspect
en: recipe aspect

zh: 詹姆斯·科林·墨菲在本书中详细记录了他在南欧、埃及和安纳托利亚的坟场与墓地中看到的秘密欢宴和迷醉之舞。作者其人坦白了自己是食尸鬼，啃食土中尸体的生物：他称他在大快朵颐时见到了书中的舞蹈。
en: Detailed accounts by James Colin Murphy of secret bacchanals and ecstatic dances observed in necropolises and graveyards in Southern Europe, in Egypt and in Anatolia. The author is a self-confessed gh

zh: 在漫宿边界，铸炉四隅，辉光之火烧灼更原始物质之处。那里有一团狡猾的蒸汽，可以侵染你敌人的梦境。
en: At the corners of the Forge, in the bounds of the Mansus, the Glory's fire meets baser elements. Here is a cunning vapour to infect your enemy's dreams.

zh: 我进食了那只坩埚留在世间的印痕。往最轻了说，这会冒犯到他。他可能会震怒。我不应该再召唤他了。
en: I have consumed the traces that Crucible left in the world. At the very least he will be offended. He might be enraged. I should not summon him again.

zh: 现下坩埚王之火的热力已不如从前。焦黑的印痕犹存，犹如煤的遗骨。
en: King Crucible's fires have sunk to a lesser heat, for now. Blackened traces remain, like the bones of coal.

zh: “我于荒漠中等待、于废墟中燃烧——金铁为水——土石化蜡——肉身成烟——来入我内，不复续存。”
en: 'IN THE DESERT I WAIT IN THE RUINS I BURN - METAL IS WATER - STONE IS WAX - FLESH IS SMOKE - ENTER ME AND BE NO LONGER.'

zh: 时而分散，时而聚集，她时而预言灾难，他时而宣扬食欲。它的预言让人困惑，它们的低语致人混淆。
en: Sometimes it divides, sometimes it unites, sometimes she speaks words of warning, sometimes he advises on appetite. Its prophecies are confusion, their whispers confound.

zh: 伊泽姆会好的，最终会的。
en: Ezeem will be fine, eventually.

zh: “改变我。改变我。用力改变我啊。现在——轮到我了。”
en: 'Change me. Change me. Change me harder. Now: my turn.'

zh: “迹形”之灵躲在后面时最自在：舞台后面，镜子后面，你后面。它们从门底滑入，用玻璃杀戮，却活不过活人的注视。
en: The Hinter are most comfortable behind: behind the scenes, behind the mirrors, behind you. They slide under doors and kill with glass, but they cannot survive the gaze of a living mortal.

zh: 巴尔多梅人已返回睿智骑士小屋中休息。
en: The Baldomerian has retreated to the Sage-Knight's Lodge to rest.

zh: “噢，世界是平的；噢，可它是平的！我不会久留，我不该久留。告诉我个秘密。”
en: 'Oh, the world is flat; oh, but it's flat! I won't stay long. I shan't stay long. Tell me a secret.'

zh: 一股撕裂之力将这具尸体拖回半死不活的状态。它一移动，身体就会像碎玻璃一样嘎吱作响。它的手掌是骨片交织成的利爪。看起来它随时可能倒下。
en: A rending force has dragged this corpse back to near-life. When it moves, it crunches like broken glass. Its fists are claw-nests of bone-shards. It won't hold together for long.

zh: 林地中的植物覆满了这具尸体的器官，缠住了它的心脏，在它的颅骨中胀大如一颗匏瓜，。它的双眼散发狡诈之气，动作仿佛一抽一抽的提线木偶。他的骨头是腐木，很快便要扎地生根，不过在那之前，它会是一名迅捷且低调的仆从。
en: The growths of the Wood have encompassed the corpse's organs, swelled its skull like a gourd, twined around its heart. Its eyes are moist with cunning, and it moves with jerky puppet grace. Its bones

zh: 亡者有时会通过纯白之门进入漫宿。它们会把说话的能力永远留在门槛外。把它们唤回现世，它们将无法发声，但其落灰的皮囊会遵从你的命令。
en: Sometimes the Dead enter the Mansus by the White Door. At its threshold they lay down their power of speech forever. Call them back to the world, and they will have no voice, but their skins of dust a

zh: “路为玻璃，墙为刀刃，步步见血。”在新开裂的镜子前休憩的亡者，穿过孔雀之门的亡者，征服锐利坡路的亡者：它们将加入侍奉残阳的行列。
en: 'The ascent is glass. The walls are knives. Each step is blood.' The Dead who rest before a new-cracked mirror, who pass through the Peacock Gate, who master the Sharp Stair: those Dead enter the serv

zh: 我可以与此物交谈，向它献上我的激情。
en: If I speak to this one, I could offer it my Passion.

zh: 我可以与此物交谈，向它献上我的理性。
en: If I speak to this one, I could offer it my Reason.

zh: 我可以与此物谈论我的健康，它能给予人针对疾病的防护。
en: If I speak with this one about my Health, it can offer protection against disease.

zh: 如果你了解其他相关语言，该灵体可能会教给你富奇诺语。
en: This spirit may be able to teach you Fucine, if you know a related tongue.

zh: 如果你了解其他相关语言，该灵体可能会教给你渊深曼达安语。
en: This spirit may be able to teach you Deep Mandaic, if you know a related tongue.

zh: 如果你了解其他相关语言，该灵体可能会教给你弗里吉亚语。
en: This spirit may be able to teach you Phrygian, if you know a related tongue.

zh: recipe aspect - hit a foe with
en: recipe aspect - hit a foe with

zh: recipe aspect - hit a foe with specifically a weapon
en: recipe aspect - hit a foe with specifically a weapon

zh: recipe aspect - hit an underling
en: recipe aspect - hit an underling

zh: recipe aspect - hit an underling with specifically a weapon
en: recipe aspect - hit an underling with specifically a weapon

zh: 此人本不属于这个世界。
en: This one is not from here.

zh: 我还要在迷雾中走一会儿。
en: For a while yet, I'll walk in mist.

zh: 镜子将进入裂损状态。
en: Mirrors will enter their cracked state.

zh: 凡人。甚至可能是些普通的凡人。但是数量很多。他们可以被打倒，被诱惑，或者被欺骗。
en: Mortals. Perhaps even ordinary mortals. But quite a number of them. They can be fought, seduced, or deceived.

zh: 凡人。甚至可能是些普通的凡人。但是数量很多。他们可以被打倒，被诱惑，或者被欺骗。
en: Mortals. Perhaps even ordinary mortals. But quite a number of them. They can be fought, seduced, or deceived.

zh: 裂光芒之肤，横流其血。
en: Crack light's skin, and light will bleed.

zh: 自从大战爆发以来，要找到武器已经容易很多了。
en: It's much easier to find firearms now, since the War.

zh: 一杆看起来相当破旧的步枪，不起眼地摆在一家古玩店的柜台上。
en: A battered-looking rifle sits prosaically on a counter in an antique shop.

zh: 在一家阴暗当铺的意外发现。
en: An unexpected find in a gloomy pawn-shop.

zh: “六者已失，卅者留存。”画布不是白色的。凑近看，你将隐约看到早已失落的司辰的形影，沿曲折的路途走进通往虚界之门。凑得太近，你将湎于落泪而不能自拔。
en: 'Six were lost, thirty remain.' The canvas is not white. Look closely, and see the hinted shapes of the Hours long lost, walking the winding path to the door into Nowhere. Look too closely, and find y

zh: 自伊苏斯时代他们便起纷争。
en: Since Issus have they striven so.

zh: 如果铸炉可以悔恨，它会为置闰而悔恨，但对它其余的作为，它感到快乐。
en: If the Forge could regret, it would regret the Intercalate, but in all its other work, it knows joy.

zh: 她并没有分裂太阳，但我们可以想象她一定曾经渴望过。
en: She did not split the Sun, but here we might imagine how she must have desired it.

zh: 她一直是一对成双的送葬鸟，而画中的她就如她本希望的一样溺亡。
en: She has been a pair of funeral birds, but here she is as she would wish to drown.

zh: 打在埃兰时，她就已现出这样的外貌。
en: Even in the days of Elam, she had begun to appear thus.

zh: 他来自辉光。我们情不自禁要看它。
en: He came from the Glory. We cannot help but see.

zh: 在第一大陆的森林中，我们的祖先诅咒自己的神连带诅咒自己。
en: In the forests of the first continent, our ancestors cursed themselves with their own god.

zh: 漫宿无墙，爱亦无疆。
en: Love, like the Mansus, defies boundaries.

zh: CAPUT GERAT LUPINUM。太阳曾被分裂，此即其伤口。
en: CAPUT GERAT LUPINUM. The Sun was divided, and this is its wound.

zh: 此仪式将召唤双生巫女以弥合现实与可能间的裂隙。学徒将把祭品倾倒进，或扔进水体：水体最好是，但并不非得是，海洋。
en: This rite calls on the Witch-and-Sister to close the gap between what is and what might be. The adept pours, or hurls, an offering into water: preferably, but not essentially, the sea.

zh: “剑不杀人人杀人。”——小塞内卡
en: 'A sword does not kill; it is an instrument in the hand of the killer.' - Seneca the Younger

zh: 偶然从罕见野兽的骸骨中挖出的镍铁子弹，出土地点跨越欧洲，中东及印度。有人曾经猎杀过怪兽。
en: Taenite-iron bullets are occasionally dug from the skeletons of unusual beasts, across Europe, the Middle East and India. Someone has been hunting monsters.

zh: 这把刀刃割出的伤口会在愈合前不停流血。一说“比德”是十八世纪图卢兹一名喜欢收集刀具的外科医生兼路匪。另一说“比德”只是阿拉伯语“蚊子”的讹传。
en: A cut from this blade will not cease bleeding until the wound is healed. One account has it that Biedde was a knife-collecting surgeon-highwayman in eighteenth-century Toulouse. Another claims Biedde

zh: 狮子匠背离上校时，他用自己的拳心碾碎了自己的配剑。此剑的每一小片至今都在因愤怒而颤动。
en: When the Lionsmith turned against the Colonel, he crushed his own sword in his fist. Each tiniest fragment of that weapon still thrills with rage.

zh: 猩红乃高热之色，而水银则是通往终极融解的钥匙。
en: Scarlet is the colour of heat. Mercury holds one key to an ultimate dissolution.

zh: 一块微小的耳骨，已被高热熏黑。使用得当的话，可以击裂石头。
en: A tiny ear-bone, blackened by intense heat. Applied correctly, it can crack stone.

zh: 一团更大光线源的残留，随衰变发出柔和的光。它可能是裂分之阳的微粒，只是可能。
en: A remnant of a greater radiance, glowing softly as decay. Just possibly, a mote of the divided Sun.

zh: 它们以前用作何用呢？能教会我的双手何事呢？
en: What uses have these known? What lessons might they teach my hands?

zh: 这是一顶属于野人母亲的环冕，上染着萃取液，以零碎毛发编织，并以皮肤残片祝福。
en: The ring-crown of a savage mother, stained with essence, woven with scraps of hair and blessed with flecks of skin.

zh: 对于那些我们用此杯饮酒的夜晚，我们后来并不确定我们做了什么。但我们可以确信，我们做了一向会做的事，而且即便我们不记得，我们的孩子也会记得，在他们最猩红的梦中。
en: On those nights when we drink from the chalice, we are not certain, afterwards, what we have done. But, we may be assured, it is what always has been done, and if we do not remember, our children will

zh: 它知道我的欲望；或者在我的想象中它知道。
en: It knows what I desire; or I imagine that it does.

zh: 马尔鲁维姆人制作木头小人偶以安抚他们所惧怕的湖中孪生女巫。如今湖水基本干涸，但湖底的人偶和孪生女巫仍存，虽然被心之性相所荫蔽。
en: The Marruvii would make little wooden idols to placate the witch-twins they feared in their lake. The lake is almost dry now, but the idols and the witch-twins yet remain, yet shadowed with the aspect

zh: 在他们将雷鸣剥皮的小林子里，他们狂敲手上的器具来激起暴怒之情。那股力量萦绕不去，如同气味。
en: In the grove where they flayed the thunder, they rattled their instruments to rouse the frenzy. The power still clings, like scent.

zh: 密林中，那位君主交出了他不得不交出的最后一样东西，以之造出此永庇疆土之器。后来，可想而知，一个帝国盗走了这面鼓，用帝国一贯的做法。
en: In the deep forests, the monarch gave up the last thing they had to give, and so the tool was shaped to keep their land forever safe. Then, of course, an empire stole it, as empires do.

zh: 波希米亚的圣亚割妮因虔诚地追求苦修肉身而闻名。该徽记形状可怖。
en: St Agnes of Bohemia was renowned for her pious dedication to the mortification of the flesh. This icon makes for grisly viewing.

zh: 这是一扇神灵曾穿过的门的颅骨。
en: This is the skull of a door through which power has passed.

zh: 有用来开门的钥匙，也有用来毁门的钥匙。
en: There are keys that open doors; then there are keys that destroy them.

zh: “午的含义，在现代的语言中，已经和古语有所不同。但是，这块石头仍透着夕阳般的光彩。
en: Noon, the saying goes, is not what it was; but this polished stone still retains a sunset glow.

zh: “午的含义，在现代的语言中，已经和古语有所不同。但是，这块石头仍透着夕阳般的光彩。它已经被裂开了。如果有相应的材料，铸相的追随者应该可以将其复原——又或者，我可以用光里面残余的法力。
en: Noon, the saying goes, is not what it was; but this stone still retains an embery glow. It has been cracked. A Forge-aspected follower could restore it, with sufficient resources - or I could use up w

zh: 多重景观！迷乱视觉！盯着我，以你五感的融解取乐吧！
en: A multiplicity of vistas! A bewilderment of views! Gaze upon me, and rejoice in the dissolution of your senses!

zh: 一道深深的裂痕划过这块亮灿灿的金属。如果有相应的材料，铸相追随者可以将其修复——又或者，我可以用光里面残余的法力。
en: A deep crack runs across the shimmering metal. A Forge-aspected follower could repair it, with sufficient resources - or I could use up what power remains in it.

zh: 这是一面不可注视第二回的镜子。
en: This is the mirror into which one does not look twice.

zh: 铸相的追随者可以将其修复，但代价高昂——又或者，我可以用光里面残余的法力。
en: A Forge-aspected follower could repair it, but the cost would be high - or I could use up what power remains in it.

zh: 白骨屋上的阁楼有扇窗子。窗子旁边有个盒子。盒子里面有卷绷带。绷带里面有团乱发。乱发里面缠着只人偶，因为窗户从未打开过。
en: In an attic above a skeleton house there was a window. Beside the window there was a box. In the box there was a bandage. In the bandage was a twist of clotted hair. In the hair was wound a doll, beca

zh: 在月光照不进的森林之中，树枝像绑带或恋人一样缠在一起。月亮将自己变成一只蚂蚁，一只小鸟或自己的姐妹，但不管她如何爱抚森林，森林仍然抗拒她进入。有一天我们携剪子前来，斩断树枝，直到月光和鲜血斑驳地撒树底的腐叶上。
en: In the forest where the moon couldn't go, the boughs of the trees were woven together like bandages or lovers. The moon might change herself to an ant or to a bird or to her sister, but the forest wou

zh: 北方有一座富庶的城市，里面曾住着一名世界的守护者。他有一天陷入了永恒的沉寂。这把剪刀被用来先剪他的头发，再剪他的手指，然后剪剩下的一切。
en: In the North there is a city of great wealth where dwelt a protector of the world. One day he fell silent. These shears were used to take from him first his hair and then his fingers and then what rem

zh: 有时，很难确定一只箭究竟是杀了希罗底皇后还是欧多克西亚皇后。有时，一只镀金钟有可能既在维也纳遭洗劫时被盗，也在罗马的藏宝地里安放着。
en: Sometimes, it is hard to be sure if an arrow slew the Empress Herodias, or the Empress Eudokia. Sometimes, it is possible that an ormolu clock was stolen in the sack of Vienna even as it rested safe i

zh: 世界的形状在每个过去中都有所变化，但里面都倒映出了漫宿的形状。人虽不能为漫宿绘制地图，却可以尝试绘制它的倒影。
en: The shape of the world changes in each past, but the shape of the Mansus is reflected in all of them. One cannot map the Mansus, but one can make an attempt to map its reflections.

zh: 此物的声响将会停歇。
en: Its sound will cease.

zh: 此物不会放松钳制。
en: It does not relinquish its grip.

zh: 先由此铃发声，而后一无所响。
en: First, its voice, and after, nothing.

zh: 一件很好的武器，但只是俗世之物。
en: A fine weapon, but a worldly one.

zh: 因导致达菲德·琼斯和梅雷迪斯·布莱恩之死而臭名昭著；这两位早年间的物理学家都称自己拥有该书的唯一抄本，最终于1840年进行了一场决斗。
en: Notorious for its involvement in the deaths of Dafyd Jones and Meredith Blaine, two early physicists who each claimed to have the only copy and who ultimately fought a duel over it in 1840.

zh: 因导致达菲德·琼斯和梅雷迪斯·布莱恩之死而臭名昭著；这两位早年间的物理学家都称自己拥有该书的唯一抄本，最终于1840年进行了一场决斗。
en: Notorious for its involvement in the deaths of Dafyd Jones and Meredith Blaine, two early physicists who each claimed to have the only copy and who ultimately fought a duel over it in 1840.

zh: 一件平常的重家伙。
en: A prosaic weight.

zh: 一件平常的重家伙。
en: A prosaic weight.

zh: 一杆刻着五重多翼者标记的古董步枪。
en: An antique rifle incised with the five-fold mark of the Many-Winged Ones.

zh: 一杆刻着五重多翼者标记的古董步枪。里面只剩一颗镍铁弹丸了。
en: An antique rifle incised with the five-fold mark of the Many-Winged Ones. Only one taenite-iron ball remains.

zh: 一杆刻着五重多翼者标记的古董步枪。
en: An antique rifle incised with the five-fold mark of the Many-Winged Ones.

zh: 一杆刻着五重多翼者标记的古董步枪。里面还剩两颗被称为“怪兽杀手”的镍铁弹丸。
en: An antique rifle incised with the five-fold mark of the Many-Winged Ones. Two taenite-iron balls - monster-killers - remain. [The Lionhunter's Rifle is a sacred weapon, but it can only be used twice m

zh: 一杆刻着五重多翼者标记的古董步枪。里面还剩三颗被称为“怪兽杀手”的镍铁弹丸。
en: An antique rifle incised with the five-fold mark of the Many-Winged Ones. Three taenite-iron balls - monster-killers - remain. [The Lionhunter's Rifle refreshes quickly, but it can only be used three

zh: 在其漫长的历史中，这件武器也被称为造门者、伤疤之剑和孤儿。据说它被铸造出来，是为了佩于王者之身、攻陷被包围的城池，但它也能毫无困难地切掉凡人和长生者的皮肉。
en: In its long career, this weapon has also been called Gatemaker, Scarsword, and the Orphan. It was commissioned to be borne by kings against sieged cities, but it will cut bits off mortals and immortal

zh: 在其漫长的历史中（这是伊兹乔克告诉我的），这件武器也被称为造门者、伤疤之剑和孤儿。据说它被铸造出来，就是为了佩于王者之身，在未知的季节对抗一座尚未落成的都市。然而它最终还是被用于其他目的，而它仍然能毫无困难地切掉凡人和长生者的皮肉。
en: In its long career (Yitzchok tells me), this weapon has also been called Gatemaker, Scarsword, and the Orphan. It was, apparently, commissioned to be borne by a king against an unbuilt city in an unkn

zh: 这把刀刃割出的伤口将会不停流血，直至愈合。一说“比德”是十八世纪图卢兹一名喜欢收集刀具的外科医生兼拦路劫匪。另一说“比德”只是阿拉伯语“蚊子”的讹传。但我的大敌曾经把比德列为上校的具名者。
en: A cut from this blade will not cease bleeding until the wound is healed. One account has it that Biedde was a knife-collecting surgeon-highwayman in eighteenth-century Toulouse. Another claims Biedde

zh: 一说“比德”只是阿拉伯语“蚊子”的讹传。但我的大敌曾经把比德列为上校的具名者。
en: One account has it that Biedde is a bastardised Arabic word for 'mosquito'. But my Foe used to number Biedde among the Colonel's Names.

zh: 它看起来平平无奇，但只要一碰到它，肉体就会灰飞烟灭。也许它真的是从七蟠内部将其杀死的那件武器的最后一块碎片。
en: It doesn't look like much, but at its touch, flesh perishes. Perhaps it really is a last fragment of the weapon that slew the Seven-Coiled from within.

zh: 它看起来平平无奇，但只要一碰到它，肉体就会灰飞烟灭。也许它真的是从七蟠内部将其杀死的那件武器的最后一块碎片。
en: It doesn't look like much, but at its touch, flesh perishes. Perhaps it really is a last fragment of the weapon that slew the Seven-Coiled from within.

zh: 图拉古拉里，即受雇猎杀长生者之人，曾从上校麾下的持盾蛇卫那里习得了他们的技艺。这把投枪的尖头是一颗剑齿兽牙。
en: The Tragulari, who are employed to hunt immortals, learnt their techniques from the Colonel's Histids. This dart is tipped with a skaptodon's tooth. [This is a sacred weapon that can only be used once

zh: 当一只剑齿兽在战斗中死去时，狮子匠的仆人们通常会将它的牙齿和爪子制成武器来纪念它的牺牲。
en: When a skaptodon falls in battle, the Lionsmith's servants often honour their sacrifice by shaping their teeth and claws into weapons.

zh: “去寻找书籍——它是一种武器。” —— 布莱希特
en: 'Reach for the book - it is a weapon.' - Brecht
